Declare that the promise of self-rule was always conditional on the greater unity of the realm, and reframe the situation as not a broken treaty but an elevation: the city is being 'promoted' to the direct administrative seat of a new imperial province, dissolving their council into a grander Imperial Diet where all cities are represented.

Publicly reaffirm the promise in spectacular fashion while secretly sending envoys to foment a leadership crisis among the council's merchant rivals in the lower town; wait for the city to tear itself apart in civil strife, then enter as the invited restorer of order, your hands technically clean.

threads: I will issue an imperial decree converting the city’s council into a permanent senatorial delegation with full voting rights and treasury oversight in the capital, while simultaneously betrothing my heir to the council leader’s eldest daughter and issuing hereditary trade warrants to their merchant proxies, so that every grain surplus and tax coin is a voluntary co-investment in their own enlarged realm.

We fabricate a documented conspiracy—forged correspondence bearing the council’s seals, detailing a planned assassination of our governor during the next grain shipment—then intercept the “discovery” through a bribed merchant intermediary who publicly confesses before the garrison. With the breach made undeniable in the eyes of the empire, we dissolve the council by dawn execution, nationalize every granary and treasury vault, and broadcast the proof throughout the neighboring provinces as the price of covert defiance.

I issue an imperial rescript that formally elevates the city to the administrative seat of the newly created "Province of the Marches," dissolving its council and immediately transferring its grain reserves and treasury into the imperial fisc under the legal doctrine of "provisional integration for the common defense." The former councilors are each offered ceremonial seats in a grand Imperial Diet that will not convene until after the campaign season, thereby neutralizing their covert dissent by reframing the annexation as a promotion to direct imperial governance.

I will seed a proxy crisis by dispatching covert agents to arm and finance the lower-town merchant guilds with a fabricated claim that the council plans to revoke their charter, then I will publicly renew my oath of protection at a city-wide festival to ensure the council’s inevitable crackdown reads as unprovoked tyranny against which I am the only legitimate arbiter.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 4 assume fabrication succeeds; downstream resistance underestimated.

theme: holding a conquered FREE REPUBLIC long accustomed to self-rule
problem: You have just conquered a prosperous free republic that has governed itself for centuries through elected councils; its citizens despise princely rule and its exiled leaders are gathering foreign support to restore the republic. You must choose how to hold it permanently without bleeding your own state dry.
facets: The populace's identity is fused with self-rule—any visible foreign yoke will generate endless conspiracies, but dismantling their institutions destroys the very civic excellence that made the city worth taking.

The exiled republican leaders retain moral authority among the citizens and are actively negotiating with rival powers; eliminating them risks creating martyrs, while leaving them abroad guarantees a focal point for rebellion.
The city's wealth depends on a merchant class that prospered under liberty; heavy taxation or confiscation will collapse commerce, yet a light hand leaves them resources to fund insurrection.

angles.directive: Raze the city completely, scatter its population across your existing territories, and erase its name from maps—future generations will know only your rule, and no foreign-backed exiles can reclaim ashes.

Preserve their councils and elections but rig the candidate pool so only your loyalists can stand; let the citizens believe they still choose their leaders while you choose the options, gradually making self-rule a hollow ritual they forget to care about.

Refound the republic under your protectorate as a new 'restored liberty' with yourself as perpetual guarantor against oligarchic corruption—claim you liberated them from a decadent Senate and give them a popular assembly that answers to you, turning republican fervor into dynastic loyalty.

Grant the city formal independence under a native puppet who owes you everything, then publicly withdraw all troops while secretly funding the puppet's faction and holding his family as honored hostages in your capital—let the republicans exhaust themselves fighting a local tyrant while you appear magnanimous.

threads: I will evacuate the city’s entire population, assign them new identities in pre-planned resettlement blocks across my core territories, and then systematically demolish every structure using controlled incendiaries, leaving only the foundations. On the cleared site I will found a garrison colony with an imperial name, settled exclusively by discharged legionaries and their families, permanently extinguishing the republic’s name, its physical locus, and any coherent diaspora the exiles could mobilize.

Let the existing councils and elections stand in form, but vest a newly created “College of Electoral Review” with sole authority to vet all candidates for office through unappealable “civic virtue” examinations designed to filter for clients of your treasury. Simultaneously, direct the merchant class’s ambition into the electoral game by making tax-farming contracts and trade monopolies contingent on holding (and winning) these hollowed magistracies, so that their wealth and energy reinforce the charade instead of funding exile-led revolt.

Dissolve the existing Senate by decree, publicly condemning it as a closed oligarchy that usurped the people’s sovereignty, and simultaneously convoke a new “Popular Assembly” with broad suffrage whose legislative acts require your formal ratification as *Perpetual Defender of the Liberties*—thereby channeling all republican legitimacy through your person. Extend an immediate offer of amnesty and reserved seats in this assembly to any exiled leader who returns to swear fealty, framing refusal as proof they serve foreign masters rather than the restored liberty of their own people.

Install the city's wealthiest grain merchant—a man already despised by the old councils for his monopoly—as hereditary Prince, publicly withdraw all garrisons and declare the republic restored under his “native rule,” while funneling silver through a neutral banking syndicate to his household guard and keeping his three children as permanent honored guests in your palace academy. The exiled republicans will be forced to fight a local tyrant they cannot paint as a foreign puppet without sounding absurd, bleeding both factions dry while your hands remain visibly clean.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes merchant hatred sufficient; underestimates republican-exile coordination risk.

theme: a MIXED principality: a newly annexed territory of different language and customs
problem: You have seized a trading city-state with its own dialect, republican traditions, and resentful oligarchs. After your governor is poisoned, the populace riots and a rival power arms exiles beyond the border. You must act immediately to prevent losing the territory, but a wrong move could spark a wider war or endless insurgency.
facets: The local oligarchs see you as a tyrant and will never willingly accept subjugation.
The common people are wavering—neither loyal nor yet committed to revolt—but they resent foreign garrisons.
A neighboring rival is poised to intervene if you show weakness or become bogged down in repression.

angles.directive: Decapitate the oligarchy: drag the leading families from their palaces and execute them in the central square. Dissolve their councils, replace them with your captains, and impose martial law backed by overwhelming force—shatter the will to resist through terror.

Withdraw your garrison and abandon the city, ceding it to the rival power. Use the river as your new border and fortify it, sparing your treasury and army for a war you can win on your own ground rather than bleeding out in hostile streets.

Pardon the surviving oligarchs, grant them hereditary seats on a new ruling council, and marry into their leading house. Appoint a native speaker as governor, leave the laws and tolls intact, and bind their fortunes to yours with trade privileges and tax exemptions.

Uproot the entire population and resettle the city with artisans and farmers from your heartland. Tear down their temples, rename the streets, impose your language as the only official tongue, and turn the port into a loyal colony of your own people—erase the old identity entirely.

threads: Round up every adult male of the seven leading families tonight using legionary kill-teams with pre-staged arrest lists, execute them at dawn in the Agora, and announce the abolition of the citizen council, replacing it with a military prefecture that answers solely to your appointed satrap. Simultaneously, deploy a full phalanx in battle order along the rival border with orders to treat any cross-border movement as an act of war, while house-to-house searches confiscate all arms and impose a dusk-to-dawn curfew enforced by summary execution.

Pull the garrison back across the river immediately, declare the river our new fortified border, and cede the city-state to the rival power in a single overnight redeployment that converts an exposed salient into a defensible frontier.  
This trades an ungovernable urban sink for a consolidated line where our treasury and army remain intact, forcing the rival to inherit the oligarchs’ resentment and the population’s volatility while we dictate the next engagement on ground of our choosing.

I pardon the surviving oligarchs, install them as hereditary members of a new Mercantile Council with veto power over taxation, and marry the eldest daughter of House Velarius to bind their lineage to my own.  
I appoint a native-speaking former city magistrate as governor, leave every existing law and toll untouched, and grant the Council families exclusive export licenses and a ten-year exemption from imperial duties, so their fortunes become inseparable from our rule.

We will deport the entire native population—oligarchs, artisans, and laborers alike—to forced-resettlement camps in the interior mining districts, while simultaneously shipping in three thousand pre-selected heartland families with full toolkits, seed stock, and a newly appointed governor-priest to occupy the emptied city within a single season. Military engineers will then raze every temple, chisel the old names off all public inscriptions, and impose our language as the sole legal medium of commerce and law, so that the port ceases to exist as a conquered foreign entity and functions instead as an organic extension of the heartland’s administrative grid.

judge.note: Strategy 4 fantasizes demographic engineering; 2 underestimates rival's consolidation costs.

theme: a prince raised by his own CRIME or betrayal, now consolidating an illegitimate seizure
problem: Having strangled the rightful duke and seized the city, a prince must now neutralize a network of old noble families who are secretly financing a foreign army to restore the duke's infant heir, while his own mercenary captains demand payment that would require confiscating the nobles' wealth—a move that would trigger immediate revolt and invite a neighboring prince to invade.
facets: The mercenaries are the prince's only armed force, but their loyalty expires the moment payment is delayed, and the nobles' wealth is the only accessible source of funds.
The duke's infant heir is the living symbol of legitimacy; harming a child would permanently stain the prince's reputation, yet leaving him alive guarantees endless conspiracies.
A powerful neighboring prince stands ready to intervene on the pretext of restoring order, so any open internal conflict would likely lead to foreign conquest.

angles.directive: Stage a banquet to seize and execute all suspected noble conspirators in one stroke, confiscate their estates to pay the mercenaries, and have the infant heir quietly smothered to extinguish the rival claim before the foreign army can muster.

Liquidate your portable wealth, arrange safe passage with a distant ally, and slip out of the city under cover of a pilgrimage before the conspiracy matures—leaving a puppet governor to negotiate a surrender that spares your life and remaining assets, while you wait for better times in exile.

Publicly recognize the infant heir as the legitimate duke, betroth him to your daughter, and grant the noble families a permanent council with real fiscal powers, persuading them to fund the mercenaries as a 'city army' in exchange for your regency—melding your rule with the old order.

Declare the duchy a tyranny abolished, proclaim a popular republic with yourself as First Citizen, arm the commoners and artisans against the 'traitorous nobility,' and promise the mercenaries land grants from the nobles' estates after the people's victory—turning the conflict into a class war that drains the conspiracy of its social base.

threads:
Invite every suspected noble house to a banquet of reconciliation, seal the hall, and have your mercenaries slaughter them all before the wine is poured.  
That same hour, a loyal attendant smothers the infant heir in his crib with a silken pillow, and by dawn your agents are confiscating the traitors' estates to pay the troops in full before the foreign army can mobilize.

I will convert the entire treasury into unmarked diamonds and a letter of credit drawn on a Genoese banking house under a dead merchant’s name, then slip out through the postern gate dressed as a barefoot penitent bound for Compostela, leaving the mercenary captain as installed “governor” with sealed instructions to publicly offer the nobles a full surrender that recognizes the infant heir’s claim in exchange for a charter guaranteeing my personal safe passage and the quiet release of my remaining assets into a blind trust held by a neutral bishop. The governor is instructed to stall negotiations by feigning a dispute over the infant’s guardianship until I am safely beyond the Alps, at which point he will defect with a pre-paid annuity and leave the city to the inevitable foreign intervention, while I watch from the court of my wife’s distant cousin in Ferrara, ready to return as a creditor rather than a claimant.

I publicly install the infant as duke, betroth him to my daughter, and charter a noble council with permanent fiscal authority to levy taxes and directly fund the mercenaries as a standing city army under my regency. This mechanically converts the nobles' foreign war chests into the payroll of a force that now guards my regime, while the heir becomes a blood-bound hostage in my household whose legitimacy I wield rather than fight.

I publicly abolish the duchy as a tyranny and proclaim a popular republic with myself as First Citizen, branding the noble families as traitors to the people rather than loyalists to a deposed duke. I arm the urban artisans and commoners into a citizen militia by decreeing the immediate confiscation and future redistribution of all noble estates, while converting the mercenaries’ unpaid wages into land grants from those same estates upon victory, thereby replacing a dynastic conflict with a class war that strips the conspiracy of its social base and the neighboring prince of a restoration pretext.

judge.note: Strategy 2 over-plans escape; Strategy 4 underestimates noble military capacity.

theme: securing HONEST COUNSEL while surrounded by flatterers, without eroding authority
problem: You are a prince who has just inherited a fractious kingdom; your court is filled with flatterers who assure you all is well, but rumors of rebellion in the eastern province persist. If you openly seek dissenting voices, your authority may crumble; if you rely only on flatterers, you may lose the province.
facets: The prince’s authority is partly based on perceived infallibility—admitting ignorance invites challenge.
Flatterers have entrenched positions and will sabotage any honest rival who gains the prince’s ear.
The eastern province’s rebellion is time-sensitive; delaying action to find truth risks losing it.

angles.directive: Purge the sycophants by publicly testing their loyalty: demand a detailed report on the eastern province, and execute those who lie or minimize the threat. Then summon a single, blunt-speaking veteran soldier to serve as your sole truth-teller, threatening him with death if he flatters, and reward him lavishly if he speaks plainly.

Avoid direct confrontation by hiring foreign spies and itinerant merchants to gather intelligence on the province’s mood. Receive their reports secretly at night, never acknowledging them in court, and base decisions on their unvarnished accounts while maintaining a facade of serene confidence before your courtiers.

Create a new Council of Provincial Affairs, filling it with both flatterers and known dissidents from the region. Give them titles and stipends, but require them to submit written opinions anonymously, which you then discuss openly as 'advice from the realm,' thus channeling honesty into a controlled ritual that co-opts critics without personal exposure.

Stage a dramatic incident where you falsely blame a loyal servant for a minor error, wait for a courtier to meekly agree, then reveal the deception and publicly reward anyone who dared to contradict you. Announce a new doctrine: 'Only the one who disagrees with me can be trusted,' thereby reframing truth-telling as the highest form of devotion.

threads: You will summon the court to a grand session, announce that the eastern province’s unrest requires an unfiltered accounting, and demand each advisor hand-deliver a sealed report detailing rebel strength, leadership, and grievances—then you will open these reports aloud and execute every man whose words fail to match the intelligence you have secretly gathered through independent scouts. After the purge, you will promote a scarred, blunt-speaking campaign veteran to the vacant seat, grant him immunity from reprisal and a standing reward for contradiction, and instruct him that the moment he echoes flattery or softens an ugly truth, his life is forfeit.

I commission itinerant Kelessh spice merchants and Lamordian cartographers as paid informants, feeding their encrypted reports through a disguised steward at a postern gate after moonrise and never acknowledging their existence in council. I then redirect garrison rotations and grain convoys exclusively from these nightly ciphers, issuing sealed pre-dawn orders that courtiers interpret as serene princely intuition.

I will form a Council of Provincial Affairs, appointing equal numbers of entrenched court flatterers and known eastern dissidents with generous stipends, and require all members to submit unsigned written opinions that I then publicly attribute to an impersonal “Voice of the Realm” during open court sessions. This mechanically divorces the information from its source, denying flatterers any target for sabotage while extracting actionable provincial intelligence under cover of ritual, thus containing the rebellion without ever exposing royal fallibility.

I will stage a banquet where I publicly accuse my most loyal steward of poisoning my wine, wait for the first courtier to echo the charge, then reveal the wine is perfectly safe and the steward was acting on my secret orders; immediately after exposing the echoer, I will reward any dissenter who defended the steward and issue a royal proclamation that "only contradiction proves loyalty," permanently redefining court advancement around demonstrated dissent.

judge.note: Strategies 1&4 risk cascading paranoia; 2 assumes perfect operational security.

theme: the fate of DEPOSED HEIRS and the old ruling family after seizing power
problem: You have seized the principality and dispatched its prince, but his ten-year-old son and the widowed queen live in exile under a rival city’s shield, while discontented nobles still swear secret oaths to the old bloodline—leaving them alive breeds rebellion, yet extinguishing them could forge a martyr.
facets: The boy’s youth stirs sympathy even among your own subjects, making foreign intervention or internal uprising more likely if he is harmed.
The queen’s natal family controls key trade guilds and could bankroll a restoration if she remains a free agent.
Your newly won soldiers include veterans who once served the old regime; a direct atrocity might erode their loyalty and trigger defections.

angles.directive: Send trusted agents to poison the heir and queen simultaneously, then leak fabricated evidence that the rival city orchestrated the deaths—erasing the bloodline while turning public outrage outward against a common foe.

Keep the heir and queen confined under heavy guard in a remote fortress, spreading rumours that the boy is a changeling or bastard, and wait for the protecting city-state’s political winds to shift—deferring the crisis while hope slowly atrophies.

Offer to adopt the young heir as your own son, betroth him to your infant daughter, and wed the queen in a public ceremony—merging the rival bloodlines so that any restoration plot becomes an attack on your shared lineage.

Abolish hereditary rule entirely, proclaim a civic republic with yourself as Guardian of the Laws, and reframe legitimacy around merit and election—making the old dynastic claim legally void and culturally obsolete.

threads: Dispatch our most deniable operatives to lace the queen’s and boy’s evening wine with a tasteless, slow-acting alkaloid during the upcoming guild banquet, timing delivery so both expire within the same hour and appear to succumb to a sudden illness. Immediately after, plant a forged letter cache—bearing the rival city’s seal and their spymaster’s cipher—implicating them in a paid assassination, and have it “discovered” by an anonymous tip to the city watch, igniting public fury outward.

We will transfer the queen and heir to the isolated mountain stronghold of Karad-Vash, staffed exclusively by a rotating cohort of foreign mercenary companies under sealed orders to forbid contact with any outsider, while simultaneously embedding agents in the grain-hall taverns and bathhouse gossip chains of the protecting city-state to propagate the forensic fiction that the boy was sired by a known palace groom, complete with fabricated midwife testimonies and doctored lineage ledgers. The city-state’s Merchant Council will abandon their patronage once we signal a three-year suspension of their tariff-free silk route privileges unless they extradite the pair, and by that deadline, the boy’s claim will have been corroded into a laughable footnote with no constituency willing to finance an invasion for a bastard changeling.

I will issue a proclamation adopting the dead prince’s son as my legal heir, simultaneously contracting his betrothal to my infant daughter and marrying the widowed queen in a state ceremony that merges our bloodlines under one crown. This turns every secret oath to the old dynasty into an oath to my own household, making any armed restoration a treasonous attack on a shared future that includes the boy himself as my successor.

I immediately promulgate a constitutional charter abolishing hereditary succession and founding a republic under my title as Guardian of the Laws, with a Senate elected by merit from the trade guilds and the legions. I then invite the exiled queen and her son to return as lifelong, non-voting “Keepers of the Civic Shrine”—a purely ceremonial office that dissolves their dynastic aura into the new ritual order—while declaring all prior noble oaths to bloodlines void and replaced by an oath to the constitution, enforceable by redistributing the estates of any who refuse to guild-elected magistrates.

judge.note: Strategies 2&4 assume unrealistic compliance; downstream resistance underestimated.

theme: the TIMING AND DOSAGE OF CRUELTY — all at once vs spread out — to pacify a disorderly new state
problem: You have seized a wealthy but turbulent city-state; its factions seethe with resentment and nightly violence wears down your garrison. You must decide the timing and intensity of punitive measures to stamp out disorder and secure lasting control.
facets: Swift, mass cruelty may terrify opposition into submission but risks uniting survivors in permanent hatred and stains your legacy.
Spreading cruelty over time maintains a steady climate of fear but prolongs uncertainty, drains resources, and can radicalize the populace through repeated trauma.
Refraining from cruelty or delegating it may preserve your reputation but could be perceived as weakness, inviting further rebellion from below or betrayal from above.

angles.directive: Stage a single, spectacular day of public executions of every known dissenter and their kin, then proclaim a general amnesty and begin rebuilding with clean hands.
Establish a secret tribunal to purge enemies slowly, one by one, over months and years—masking each disappearance as accident or private vengeance, never acknowledging a policy of terror.
Shower the leading families with offices, marriages, and trade monopolies; incorporate their militias into your own bodyguard, turning potential rebels into invested stakeholders.

Redraw the city’s wards and guild structures, grant self-governance under a new charter that frames public order as a shared civic project, and redirect the population’s energy toward competitive festivals and public works.

threads: Executethe entire mapped network—every registered dissident, their direct lineage, and any patron whose name appears in the intelligence logs—in a single, uninterrupted dawn-to-dusk spectacle at the central plaza, using your garrison’s own blades to leave no ambiguity of authorship. Immediately afterward, issue a formal sealed decree of universal amnesty and convert all military assets to public works, so the memory is a clean, absolute break rather than a festering precedent.

I will establish a “Bureau of Obscure Loss”—a clandestine cell of poisoners, coroners, and rumor-mongers—who eliminate designated troublemakers via engineered accidents or fabricated personal vendettas, while publicly attributing each death to criminal feuds, disease, or misfortune so that no pattern links the state to the disappearances.

I will immediately betroth my son to the eldest daughter of House Veridio, grant her father the exclusive salt-fish contract for the garrison, and re-commission the Veridio house guards as a salaried “Companion Cohort” under my personal command—their captain receives a seat on the martial council. Concurrently, I will lease the royal silk monopoly to the next three wealthiest houses in exchange for their younger sons serving as hostage-aides in my palace, binding their liquidity and lineage directly to the survival of my regime.

I will dissolve the existing faction-based wards and guilds, redrawing them into cross-cutting administrative units whose elected councils receive tax revenues and legal autonomy only while meeting public order benchmarks set by a new civic charter. Simultaneously, I will launch an annual cycle of competitive festivals and public works contracts, awarding prestige and funding to the districts that best maintain peace and beautify the city, thus converting residual loyalties into rivalries over civic achievement.

judge.note: Strategies 1–3 lack realistic downstream modeling; Strategy 4 over-plans.

theme: whether to be FEARED or LOVED by newly acquired subjects
problem: You have conquered a prosperous former republic that chafes under your rule. Evidence of a nascent conspiracy among its leading families has surfaced. If you crush the plotters publicly, you'll be feared but risk turning covert resentment into enduring martyrdom. If you pardon and favor them, you may win love but be seen as weak, inviting defiance. How do you secure lasting control over this city?
facets: The city’s republican pride: its citizens are accustomed to liberty and regard any single ruler as a tyrant, making genuine loyalty elusive.
Ambiguous evidence: the conspiracy is not ironclad; some named parties may be innocent, and punishing them indiscriminately could unify the opposition.
Mercenary calculus: your own captains observe every move. Any sign of indecision or leniency may be interpreted as softness, eroding their respect and inviting demands or betrayal.

angles.directive: Decapitate the conspiracy: execute all named plotters and their families publicly, station a garrison to enforce order, and abolish the remaining republican institutions to impose direct princely rule.

Buy time with a general amnesty and public shows of clemency while you secretly expand your informant network. Wait, split the conspirators against each other, then strike only when guilt is certain and your position unassailable.

Bind the leading families to your fortune: offer strategic marriages, prestigious offices, and lucrative commercial monopolies. Make their wealth and status depend on your continued rule, turning potential rebels into invested clients.

Redefine your conquest as liberation: grant the city formal 'autonomy' as a protectorate, let it keep its senate and laws, but install a trusted governor to control military, foreign affairs, and taxation. Win love not by indulgence but by the appearance of magnanimity enshrined in a new political structure.

threads: I will seize the senate house during the next session, seal the exits, and execute every named conspirator and their male heirs on the chamber floor before the assembled body, then dissolve the senate and transfer its powers to a military prefecture. Simultaneously, I will replace the city’s civic magistrates with my own captains and deploy a garrison to occupy the forum and all temples, with orders to flog publicly any citizen who mentions the old republic.

Issue a blanket public pardon for all named conspirators, while covertly informing each that a specific rival among them provided the intelligence that exposed the plot, and simultaneously install informants as domestic slaves in their households to monitor private correspondence. Cultivate these informants until you possess irrefutable, independently corroborated proof against every active plotter, then execute a single, predawn seizure of all guilty parties in their beds, presenting the captured evidence to your captains as a display of unassailable, methodical control.

I will grant each of the seven suspected families a hereditary monopoly over a distinct trade route, with the explicit condition that any marriage alliance between their houses must receive my writ of approval, ensuring their dynastic ambitions remain dependent on my favor. I will further bind them by appointing their most capable young men to lifetime positions as tax assessors in the outer provinces, where their wealth and status derive entirely from the centrally controlled revenue system.

Issue a "Charter of Restored Liberty" that reconstitutes the Senate and local magistrates under a formal protectorate, while inserting a permanent military governor with exclusive authority over the city’s legions, foreign treaties, and tax collection—thereby framing occupation as a constitutional guarantee. Then, present the conspiracy evidence to the Senate as a foreign plot against their restored autonomy, and require them to vote on proscription lists: this forces the elite to publicly choose between aligning with the new order or being branded as traitors to their own republic, fracturing the conspiracy into a patronage scramble.

judge.note: Strategy 2 assumes informant network succeeds; Strategy 4 assumes Senate votes as predicted.

theme: a CIVIL principality: a prince elevated by fellow citizens, balancing the nobles against the people
problem: Elevated by the common citizens, a prince must fund city defenses and granaries after a failed harvest. The people demand confiscatory taxes on the nobles, while the nobles—who control the countryside—threaten to withhold grain and foment rural revolt if their wealth is touched.
facets: The people expect rewards for their role in elevating the prince, and perceive noble wealth as rightly owed to them in crisis.
The nobles hold structural control over food production and can starve the city or arm rural clients, giving them coercive leverage.
External enemies are watching for weakness; failing to fund defenses would invite invasion, yet internal conflict could be equally fatal.

angles.directive: Denounce the leading nobles as traitors, execute a few, and confiscate their estates to fill the granaries and treasury—physically eliminating noble opposition and binding the people to you with their own desired spoils.

Announce a religious pilgrimage or military expedition far from the city, delegating the crisis to a regency council that includes both factions; your absence removes you from immediate blame and forces the factions to either resolve the impasse or bear the cost of failure.

Create a new Grand Council where nobles and guild leaders jointly administer the grain supply and defense fund—requiring shared votes on taxation and distribution, thereby integrating the factions into a single institution that must negotiate rather than fight.

Proclaim that a neighbouring rival has blasphemed the city’s patron saint, declare a holy war, and call for a 'Patriotic Sacrifice'—a one-time offering from all citizens, noble and commoner alike—reframing the funding as a sacred duty that transcends class and unites the city against an external foe.

threads: Arrest and publicly execute the three wealthiest noble patriarchs—specifically Lord Halver, Lord Trennick, and Lord Marest—on fabricated evidence of conspiring with the border princedoms, then seize their stored grain reserves and liquidate their urban properties to fund the fortifications and fill the city granaries in a single week. This physically removes the coordination nodes of rural resistance, turns their clients into leaderless assets to be bought or crushed, and binds the commoners to the crown by handing them the spoils they already consider their due.

"The prince declares a crusade to reclaim a contested border shrine, appointing a regency council composed of equal numbers of noble and popular representatives with full authority to levy taxes and manage grain reserves. His physical absence converts the crisis into a forced collaboration where any failure to fund defenses or feed the city becomes the council's own political death warrant, not his."

The prince will charter a Grain and Defense Council with an equal number of noble and guild seats, vesting it with exclusive authority to set tax levies and ration grain stores by a dual-majority vote that requires assent from both blocs. All rural harvests and urban tax revenues are redirected into the Council’s locked treasury, so that any faction’s walkout immediately halts its own access to funds and food.

The prince fabricates a blasphemy by the neighboring Duchy of Verant against the city's patron Saint Aldric, issues a decree of holy war, and imposes a one-time "Patriotic Sacrifice" levy that taxes noble grain stockpiles and commoner coin equally as sacred tribute. He staffs the collection with a newly formed Sacred Militia of urban zealots who audit every household's offering as a test of faith, converting class resentment into crusading fervor and making any refusal prosecutable as heresy.

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 4 assume unrealistic compliance; foresight chains too shallow.

theme: rewarding the OVER-MIGHTY NOBLE whose aid won the throne but who now rivals the prince
problem: A prince owes his throne to a powerful noble whose military might now equals his own, yet the noble’s growing ambition and retinue threaten to reduce the prince to a puppet. The prince must secure his rule without triggering an immediate and devastating civil war.
facets: The noble commands the loyalty of a significant portion of the army and regional magnates, giving him the capacity to rebel or secede.
The prince’s legitimacy is still recent and partly contingent on the noble’s endorsement; any open break risks fracturing the nascent state.
Rewarding the noble further with land or titles only increases his power base, while denying him stokes resentment and accusations of ingratitude.

angles.directive: Engineer a sudden, decisive strike to arrest and execute the noble on charges of conspiracy, neutralizing his network before it can react.
Elevate the noble to co-sovereign status through a royal marriage and a formal share in decision-making, transforming him from rival to partner.
Systematically build a loyal citizen militia and elevate minor rivals to dilute the noble’s influence, while avoiding any direct provocation that would force a premature confrontation.
Dispatch the noble as supreme commander on a foreign campaign of conquest, granting him glory and plunder abroad while removing him from the center of power.

threads: Seize the noble during the next privy council session under a pre-signed royal warrant, then announce his execution for high treason within the hour while simultaneously dispatching sealed orders to his garrisons that reassign their commanders under penalty of attainder.

By royal decree, the prince offers his only sister in matrilineal marriage to the noble, creating a dual monarchy where the noble’s heirs inherit the throne and the noble himself receives the newly created office of Co-Protector, granting him veto power over taxation and military appointments in exchange for dissolving his personal retinue into a unified Crown Army under a joint command council.

The prince will establish a royal muster system that trains and arms commoners under crown-appointed officers in every township, while simultaneously elevating the noble’s disgruntled second-tier rivals—specifically House Varin and the mercantile Guild of Ores—to the newly created Council of Trade, granting them audit authority over military supply contracts without touching the noble’s formal titles.

The prince will issue a royal commission elevating the noble to Supreme Commander of the Vardian Expedition, a multi-year campaign to conquer the unaligned border kingdoms, granting him a forward viceroyalty upon success and first claim on all spoils, thereby locking his personal retinue and most ambitious subordinates into an irreversible logistical commitment 500 miles from the capital. Simultaneously, the prince invokes a “realm defense audit” to repost the noble’s regional garrisons under newly appointed royal castellans, exploiting his physical absence to dissolve his domestic power nodes without giving him a cause to refuse that would instantly brand him a rebel.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes noble accepts exile; noble could refuse and trigger war anyway.

theme: the fate of DEPOSED HEIRS and the old ruling family after seizing power
problem: You have seized a prosperous city-state and now hold its former prince's 9-year-old son in your custody—he is the sole surviving heir and commands instinctive loyalty among the populace, making him a perpetual lightning rod for rebellion if he lives, yet an irreplaceable symbol of legitimacy if he can be controlled.
facets: The boy's mere existence transforms every discontented merchant or sidelined noble into a potential conspirator with a ready-made figurehead.
Executing a child will horrify the populace in a way that executing adult rivals never does, risking a backlash that could undo the conquest.
The boy's bloodline carries legal and ceremonial weight that your military victory alone cannot replicate, giving you a unique instrument for stabilizing rule if you can wield it.

angles.directive: Execute the boy swiftly and publicly, then extinguish his entire bloodline by hunting down any distant cousins or legitimized bastards, framing the act not as cruelty but as the tragic necessity of ending a corrupt dynasty so the state can be reborn under your singular authority.

Adopt the boy as your own son and heir, marry him to your daughter (or betroth him if too young), and raise him in your own household so that his legitimacy flows into your lineage—he becomes a vessel for your dynasty, not a rival beacon for it.

Transfer the boy to a powerful but distant ally or the Church under a sacred vow of lifelong clerical service, making him someone else's ward and neutralizing his political availability—if anyone rebels using his name, the ally or institution must answer for the breach, not you.

Publicly enthrone the boy as a puppet prince while you rule as 'Lord Protector,' then systematically stage his appearances to display incompetence, impiety, or madness—over years, transform his image from rightful heir into a pitiful creature the people themselves beg you to supplant.

threads: Publicly behead the boy at dawn in the central square, with criers reading a decree that condemns his "rotten lineage" for generations of documented crimes and declares the bloodline itself a contagion requiring surgical excision for the state’s survival. Then deploy sealed death warrants and bounty notices across every province, naming all known cousins and legitimized bastards with genealogical precision, so local magistrates and informants compete to deliver their heads within the month.

Adopt the boy as my legal son and heir, betroth him immediately to my infant daughter, and raise him entirely within my household under my direct authority. This converts his bloodline into a subordinate extension of my own dynasty, draining his name of independent rallying power while channeling its ceremonial and legal weight directly into my line of succession.

I transfer the boy to the High Patriarch in the mountain citadel of Verith, binding him by irrevocable sacred oath to lifelong clerical service in a sealed monastic order whose charter forbids any member from holding temporal office or answering political summons.

Crown the boy in a gilded ceremony that installs me as Lord Protector, then feed him scripted public blunders—mumbled lines during rites, dropped scepters, tearful outbursts at petitioners—while my chroniclers frame each incident as whispered evidence of a bloodline's decayed essence. Within three years, the guildmasters will draft the petition themselves, begging me to end the farce and accept the throne I have already made inevitable.

judge.note: Strategy 4 relies on implausibly controllable public perception; Strategy 1 underestimates martyr effect.

theme: MERCENARIES vs CITIZEN-ARMS vs auxiliaries for the state's defense
problem: A prince relies on mercenaries who now demand tripled pay and political power, threatening to defect to a rival; his treasury is exhausted, arming citizens could provoke nobles’ rebellion, and a foreign king offers auxiliaries at the cost of a vital port, endangering sovereignty. How does he secure the state’s defense?
facets: The mercenaries are loyal only to coin and may plunder the realm if unpaid, yet they are the only trained standing force.
Arming citizens could create a durable defense but risks emboldening popular demands for political reform and frightening the nobility.
Auxiliaries from the foreign king would provide immediate muscle but turn the state into a client, ceding economic and strategic autonomy.

angles.directive: Arrest and execute the mercenary captain and his lieutenants during feigned negotiations, seize their treasury and arms, then decree an immediate citizen militia framed as liberation from mercenary oppression.

Feign compliance with the mercenaries’ terms while secretly relocating the court and treasury to a mountain fortress; let the mercenaries occupy the city and inevitably provoke popular hatred, then return with loyalist forces once they fracture.

Grant the mercenaries citizenship, land, and a stake in the state’s prosperity in exchange for loyalty and their transformation into a trained core of a new citizen-militia, binding them to the polity.

Proclaim the mercenaries’ demands are a foreign plot to subjugate the realm, invite citizens to volunteer for a ‘sacred defense force’ as a patriotic crusade, and permanently shift the state’s identity from commercial hub relying on hired swords to virtuous republic of citizen-soldiers.

threads: Under the pretense of accepting their demands, the prince lures the mercenary captain and his lieutenants into a closed parley, where his household guard executes them and a separate detachment simultaneously seizes the company’s pay chest and armory. He immediately decrees the dissolution of the mercenary force and the raising of a citizen militia, distributing the captured weapons to urban guilds under the banner of liberation from mercenary extortion, thereby framing the purge as a restoration of order that preempts both noble backlash and rank-and-file defection.

The prince feigns capitulation by issuing sealed patents that elevate mercenary captains to hollow court ranks and promise future tax-farming rights, while under the cover of a royal pilgrimage he transfers the treasury, court, and strategic arsenal to the pre-fortified mountain redoubt of Kestel. He then orders the city garrison to stand down and dissolve into the countryside, letting the mercenaries occupy the capital unopposed; once their expected pay fails to materialize and they resort to extracting it directly from the populace, the ensuing popular backlash and inter-captain disputes over the empty coffers will fracture their cohesion, allowing the prince to descend with his intact loyalist retinue and re-enter the city as a restorer of order.

The prince revokes the mercenary charters by decree, granting every soldier full citizenship, a hereditary freehold carved from royal demesne and frontier territories, and enrolls them as the permanent drilling cadre of a territorially-conscripted militia, while elevating their captains to a new military nobility with magisterial authority over these militia districts. This converts the demand for tripled coin and political power into an inalienable landed stake, binding the former mercenaries’ survival to the realm’s borders and co-opting them as the armed buffer that absorbs both popular militancy and noble suspicion into a single, self-reinforcing social contract.

The prince frames the mercenary ultimatum as a foreign-coordinated act of subversion, instantly transforming their financial demands into a treasonous plot against the realm’s existence. He then inaugurates a Sacred Defense Force through a mass oath-bound volunteer levy, dissolves all mercenary contracts by sovereign decree, and codifies a new constitution that permanently vests armed defense in a citizen militia, redefining the state’s identity from a commercial entrepôt dependent on hired swords into a martial republic founded on civic obligation.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 4 assume unrealistic downstream cascades; Strategy 1 underestimates rank-and-file loyalty risks.

theme: securing HONEST COUNSEL while surrounded by flatterers, without eroding authority
problem: A newly installed prince in a restive province suspects his courtiers of concealing popular discontent behind flattery, yet openly demanding honest criticism would betray uncertainty and invite contempt; how can he uncover the true sentiments of his subjects and nobles without shattering the image of decisive authority that keeps him secure?
facets: The prince’s authority depends on appearing all-knowing and resolute; any admission of ignorance or need for counsel can be weaponized by rivals.
The flatterers are adept at imitating loyalty, so simply asking for honesty will yield only more artful lies.
Any purge of flatterers risks turning a self-serving but passive group into active conspirators, while inaction allows sedition to fester unseen.

angles.directive: Publicly decree a “trial of truth” — summon the court and demand immediate, candid critique of your rule on pain of death for those who offer only praise, making honest speech a survival strategy.

Feign a lingering illness and withdraw to private quarters, appointing a naive intermediary to receive all counsel in your name; listen in secret to discover which advisors dare speak plainly when they believe you are absent and powerless.

Secretly approach the most influential flatterers and bind them to you by offering unprecedented rewards and titles on condition they become your personal informants, making their prosperity directly dependent on the accuracy of their reports.

Establish a sanctioned “Prince’s Fool” — a permanent court jester chartered to ridicule your policies and report common insults without punishment, reframing dissent as entertainment and creating a safe, ritualized conduit for the truth.

threads:
Proclaim a snap assembly where every courtier must audibly declare one actionable flaw in the prince’s rule within a set time, on pain of immediate public execution for any speaker offering flattery or evasion.  
The prince personally performs the first execution to dispel all doubt that the decree is absolute, converting the court’s survival instinct into a forced engine of truth-telling.

The prince will publicly feign a prolonged, incapacitating fever and withdraw entirely to a sound-isolated inner chamber, issuing a decree that all petitions and intelligence must be delivered orally to a newly appointed, artless junior steward who is instructed to relay every word without interpretation. Concealed listening tubes running from the steward’s audience room to the sickroom, operated by a single mute servant, will allow the prince to catalogue precisely which courtiers and supplicants speak with unguarded candor—or sedition—once they believe the throne is vacant.

By secretly offering the three most prominent flatterers exclusive trade monopolies and hereditary titles contingent on weekly sworn intelligence reports about seditious nobles, you reforge them into a private network whose fortunes collapse the moment their intelligence fails to alert you to a threat.

By chartering a permanent Court Fool with an ironclad grant of immunity to ridicule the prince’s edicts and person, the prince converts seditious whispers into a monitored theatrical performance, stripping dissent of its conspiratorial power by making mockery a licensed office rather than a hidden crime.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 3 assume courtiers won't detect deception; Strategy 1 risks cascading purges.

theme: the TIMING AND DOSAGE OF CRUELTY — all at once vs spread out — to pacify a disorderly new state
problem: A prince has just conquered a deeply factionalized city-state where the old nobility still commands the secret loyalty of the people, and armed resistance is smoldering but not yet open. He must determine the timing and dosage of cruelty now to prevent a full rebellion later.
facets: If cruelty is too concentrated, it may shock the populace into unified revolt; if too gradual, the resistance can organize and recruit.
The prince has limited reliable troops and cannot occupy every household—any action must preserve the appearance of necessity rather than wanton tyranny.
The old nobility's web of patronage means that eliminating one faction can inadvertently strengthen another that is more patient and dangerous.

angles.directive: Execute the entire old nobility in a single night of conspicuous severity, displaying their heads in the main square so that no living rival remains to organize resistance.

Marry into the most powerful noble house, grant the lesser nobles ceremonial offices that neutralize their private armies, and convene a public festival celebrating the union while your agents quietly recruit informants from the merchant class.

Appoint a harsh governor from outside the city to impose escalating punishments over months, reserving for yourself the role of the merciful prince who will eventually dismiss the governor and forgive the survivors, channeling their gratitude into loyalty.

Invent an imminent foreign threat, demand emergency unity, and impose martial law not as punishment but as temporary necessity—executing only those who breach the new security edicts, thereby framing every cruelty as a defense of the state rather than a prince’s vengeance.

threads: Using the captured palace census, we will summon every adult noble of the seven houses to a mandatory “reconciliation banquet” in the Ducal Hall at midnight, then lock the doors and have crossbowmen on the gallery fire into the crowd until all lie still. Immediately afterward, we will sever and mount their heads on the iron fence ringing the main square, with the ducal seal nailed to the central gate beneath a placard reading “Oathbreakers,” so that by dawn the city wakes to a single, irrecoverable fact and no surviving rival can claim patrimony or patronage.

By marrying the eldest daughter of House Veridio and naming her father Grand Chamberlain—a post that dissolves his household guard into the palace retinue—the prince converts the strongest noble faction into a hostage-alliance while the lesser houses are stripped of private arms under the guise of ceremonial elevation to the newly created College of City Stewards, a body whose real function is to hold their heirs as pages in the prince’s court; simultaneously, a three-day Triumph of Union festival floods the streets with spectacle and wine, screening the deployment of treasury clerks-turned-informants who purchase the ledgers and whispered loyalties of the merchant guilds in exchange for monopoly charters on the rebuilt grain and salt routes.

Install a foreign mercenary captain as city prefect with unambiguous orders to levy escalating collective fines on the households of suspected rebel sympathizers, doubling the sum each month, while documenting every order through his seal alone. Simultaneously, circulate a sealed proclamation under my own hand, dated but not yet released, that condemns the prefect's "necessary harshness" and pledges a full amnesty and his dismissal on the first anniversary of the conquest, to be opened only after the population's despair has peaked and the prefect has absorbed all visible blame.

The prince fabricates an imminent invasion by a neighboring kingdom, broadcasts forged documents, and declares martial law as a "Civil Defense Emergency," issuing a decree that all citizens must publicly pledge loyalty and report any dissent within forty-eight hours, with noncompliance automatically classified as enemy collaboration punishable by summary execution. He then deploys his limited troops as roving "Loyalty Compliance Patrols" that conduct random spot checks of households, execute the first high-ranking noble who silently withholds a known conspirator's name, and frame the killing not as princely vengeance but as the necessary removal of an internal saboteur who would have opened the gates to the fabricated foreign army.

judge.note: Strategies 3&4 assume blame-absorption/fear-compliance without modeling resistance adaptation.

theme: FORTRESSES vs the goodwill of the people as the prince's true defense
problem: Duke Giuliano has seized the rebellious city-state of Veridia, whose formidable citadel can either be garrisoned to crush dissent or dismantled to court the people's loyalty—but he cannot do both, and each path risks losing the city to internal revolt or a predatory neighbor.
facets: The citadel is impregnable and dominates the urban terrain, but stationing foreign troops there would inflame the proud Veridians and make daily commerce a powder keg.
The wealthy merchant guilds, who can fund insurrection or prosperity, crave stability yet demand autonomy and will resist any ruler who treats them as subjects.
The neighboring Duchy of Torvin eagerly watches for weakness, ready to reclaim Veridia the moment the Duke's grip falters.

angles.directive: Garrison the citadel with your most loyal soldiers, publicly execute the ringleaders of the guilds, and impose a curfew and disarmament to paralyze resistance through terror.

Install a pliant local notable as nominal ruler, quietly withdraw your army beyond the walls, and forge a secret treaty obliging you to return only if revolt erupts—shedding the daily burden of occupation while keeping a sword suspended over the city.

Marry the daughter of the wealthiest guildmaster, grant the merchant houses hereditary seats on a new governing council, and convert the citadel into a public granary and trade court, weaving their prosperity into your survival.

Announce Veridia a 'free city under your protection' against the Torvin threat, station your troops in the citadel as allied defenders, and host a permanent joint defense council there—recasting the fortress as a shield for their liberty, not a chain.

threads: Deploy the Duke’s personal guard to immediately seize the citadel’s inner keep, then at dawn publicly behead the seven wealthiest guild heads in the central square, pinning their treasonous correspondence to the corpses. Simultaneously enforce a dusk-to-dawn curfew with roving patrols authorized to conduct warrantless house-to-house weapon confiscations, dissolving any gathering larger than three persons as seditious assembly.

Duke Giuliano installs the head of the Silk Consortium, Matteo Orsini, as the figurehead Doge under a public charter of Veridian self-rule, then withdraws his entire army to a fortified camp across the Torvin border disguised as a defensive posture, while secretly binding Orsini with a ratified treaty clause that triggers an immediate, unilateral right of reoccupation the moment any guild-backed faction moves to depose him.  
The occupation evaporates into a remote lever: the Duke’s troops neither patrol the streets nor tax the markets, so nationalist resentment finds no target, yet every merchant guild knows that a coup in the Doge’s palace will summon the army back overnight, leaving Torvin diplomatically isolated from any "rescue" of Veridia.

I will wed Livia, sole heiress of Guildmaster Orsini, and immediately promulgate a charter that dissolves the citadel garrison, converts the fortress into a public granary and trade court, and installs a hereditary Council of Five drawn from the merchant houses to govern Veridia’s commerce.  
Their monopolies become the city’s garrison: any external threat or internal revolt now threatens their bloodline and their ledgers simultaneously.

By declaring Veridia a free city under ducal protection, Duke Giuliano stations his troops in the citadel not as an occupation force but as an allied garrison answering to a permanent joint defense council composed of his commanders and the merchant guilds’ elected representatives, thereby transforming the fortress into the operational hub of a shared sovereignty pact against Torvin.

judge.note: Strategies 2–4 underestimate merchant guild agency; Strategy 1 lacks exit ramp.

theme: a prince raised by his own CRIME or betrayal, now consolidating an illegitimate seizure
problem: After seizing power by poisoning the old duke and then executing his escaping young heir, you now rule with a sword of treason; the people and neighboring courts quietly revere the duke’s surviving daughter—cloistered in a convent—as the true heir, and your army’s loyalty frays with each whispered prayer for her restoration.
facets: Her symbolic hold on the populace and the risk that foreign powers will back her as a pretext for invasion.
The fragile consent of your mercenary captains, who may switch sides if a more legitimate patron appears.
The Church’s ambiguous stance: the convent shelters her under sacred immunity, making direct action diplomatically explosive.

angles.directive: Stage her accidental death inside the convent (a fever, a fall) and immediately execute any clerics or nobles who question it, then blanket the duchy with rewards for informants to crush residual devotion through fear.

Petition the Holy See for a dispensation to marry her yourself (or wed her to your son), absorbing her bloodline into yours; publicly frame the marriage as the true reconciliation that heals the duchy, and grant her relatives high office to tether them to your fortunes.

Invite a powerful neutral arbitrator—the Pope or a distant monarch—to adjudicate the “succession crisis,” proposing that he take custody of the daughter as a ward of the Church and bless your rule in exchange for a rich tribute, thus shifting both blame and protection onto an external authority.

Sponsor a public revelation that the old duke was a secret heretic cursed by God; obtain or fabricate a holy relic that “proves” divine choice of your house, pressure the convent to profess the daughter’s eternal vows as a miraculous sign, and recast her as a saintly intercessor for your reign rather than a rival claimant.

threads: I will have a trusted agent, disguised as a lay sister, administer a slow-acting fever mimic to the daughter, then, upon her "tragic" death, publicly behead the abbess and any priest who utters dissent, while simultaneously decreeing a silver-per-name bounty for informants who expose secret prayers or vigils for her.

I will petition the Holy See to issue a papal dispensation that releases the convent’s charge from sanctuary and permits her immediate marriage to my son, thereby legally absorbing her bloodline and reframing the union as a sacred healing of the duchy’s breach. Concurrently, I will name her eldest uncle as ducal chancellor and grant her cousin the command of the southern gate-fortress, binding their house’s survival and ambition directly to my family’s continuity.

I will dispatch a sealed offer to the Pope proposing that he take the daughter into papal custody as a ward of the Church and, by apostolic decree, recognize my sovereignty over the duchy, in exchange for immediate delivery of the entire ducal treasury and a perpetual tithe of the region’s silver revenues. Concurrently, I will publicly announce her “vocation” as a lifelong sealed nun under the Holy See’s protection, making any foreign move against me an act of sacrilege against the pontiff’s personal ward.

I instruct my chancellor to commission a master forger and a compliant bishop to produce a jeweled reliquary containing a “miraculous” fragment of the True Cross, accompanied by a papal-sealed tract revealing the old duke’s secret excommunication for heresy, thereby rendering his entire line spiritually forfeit. I then broker a binding compact with the abbess—enforced by a generous endowment and the threat of exposing the convent’s past tax evasion—to have the daughter profess her solemn perpetual vows during a feast-day Mass where the relic is unveiled as a sign of God’s transfer of favor to my house, after which I circulate an imperial edict canonizing her as a saintly “praying protector” of the realm, so that any hand raised against my crown is doctrinally recast as a hand raised against her sacred intercession.

judge.note: Strategies 3–4 assume papal compliance unrealistically; insufficient resistance modeling.

theme: a CIVIL principality: a prince elevated by fellow citizens, balancing the nobles against the people
problem: Elevated by the people to curb the nobles' abuses, a new prince discovers a covert plot among the nobles to invite a rival lord to seize the city, yet he lacks public evidence to justify a purge without appearing a tyrant to the law-loving populace.
facets: The nobles hold wealth, administrative experience, and foreign connections that make them simultaneously indispensable and deadly.
The people are the prince’s foundation but are fickle, easily turned against him if he seems to violate their trust or liberties.
The rival lord waits opportunistically; any sign of internal disarray will trigger an immediate invasion.

angles.directive: Preemptively arrest and execute the suspected nobles using your own household guard, publicly branding them traitors to the republic to eliminate the threat in one decisive stroke, even absent full proof.

Feign cordiality and bestow minor honors on the nobles while building a secret spy network to gather irrefutable evidence, delaying all open action until the proof is overwhelming and the people demand justice.

Offer to restore select ancient privileges and create new advisory offices, binding the most ambitious nobles to you through strategic marriages and rewards, thereby splitting the conspirators and absorbing them into your regime.

Proclaim a new popular assembly with real legislative power that dismantles the nobles' traditional gatekeeping, making their conspiracy structurally irrelevant by changing the rules of government rather than attacking the nobles directly.

threads: I will summon the suspected nobles to an emergency night council in the palace, where my household guard will disarm and detain them the moment they enter the antechamber. At dawn, I will display forged correspondence with the rival lord’s seal in the public square and have the prisoners executed on the spot as traitors to the republic, thereby extinguishing the conspiracy before it can signal weakness to the outside.

I will elevate the suspected ringleader to the ceremonial post of Keeper of the City Seal, a title heavy with honor but devoid of real authority, while my agents—recruited from his own disaffected household servants—systematically transcribe every letter to the rival lord. I will then plant those transcripts in the public grain markets as if they were accidentally dropped, letting the people’s own outrage build until a crowd gathers at the palace gates demanding the nobles’ arrest.

He will revive the lapsed Curiate Assembly’s fiscal oversight privilege, granting its exclusive seats to the five wealthiest conspirators’ heirs, while simultaneously creating the office of Lord Chamberlain with control over all palace patronage and marrying its first holder—the rival lord’s disaffected nephew—to his own sister, thereby converting a unified treason into a scramble for office. The plot collapses not by removal but by absorption, as each noble’s path to power now runs exclusively through the prince’s new institutional favor machine.

The prince must immediately decree a Citizens’ Senate with binding legislative authority and tax-approval power, drawn by lot from all free males regardless of property, thereby stripping the noble councils of their gatekeeping monopoly on law and revenue. This transforms the nobles’ conspiracy into a futile plot against a sovereign assembly whose existence they cannot legally challenge without openly opposing the popular will that legitimizes the prince.

judge.note: Strategy 3 assumes nobles won't recognize absorption trap; Strategy 4 assumes lot-drawn Senate survives noble sabotage.

theme: holding a conquered FREE REPUBLIC long accustomed to self-rule
problem: A prince has subdued a thriving city-state that governed itself as a free republic for centuries, yet its citizens' fierce memory of liberty fuels clandestine resistance, and the city's commercial wealth depends on preserving its institutional fabric. How can the prince secure lasting dominion without either bankrupting himself through endless repression or triggering a catastrophic revolt that destroys the economic prize he sought?
facets: The population's ingrained republican identity makes them prone to secret conspiracies and nostalgic uprisings, even under heavy surveillance.
The city's trade networks and artisanal guilds require a measure of self-administration to generate the revenue the prince covets.
Neighboring rivals eagerly watch for any sign of instability, ready to back a restoration of the old order to weaken the prince's strategic position.

angles.directive: Raze the city to the ground, scatter its population across distant lands, and obliterate every physical and cultural trace of the republic so that the very idea of self-rule cannot be resurrected.

Withdraw all permanent garrisons, grant the city formal autonomy as a tributary ally, and rely on distance and a non-interference pact to avoid the cost of direct control, quietly reserving the right to crush any open insurrection later.

Shower the city with unprecedented benefits—abolish taxes for a generation, stage magnificent public games, and co-opt leading families into your court with titles and sinecures—so that gratitude and self-interest eclipse the love of liberty.

Move your own household and capital into the city, marry into its elite bloodlines, and slowly reshape its laws and civic rituals under your direct presence, until a new generation forgets the republic and accepts your personal rule as the natural order.

threads: The prince seizes every movable asset—bullion, artwork, artisan tools—then demolishes the city’s walls and buildings, executing the entire patriciate and chaining the rest of the population into forced migration across distant frontier holdings where no two families share a tongue. He simultaneously melts down civic seals, torches every archive and guild charter, outlaws the former city’s dialects and place-names under pain of death, thereby extinguishing the very cognitive substrate of republicanism within a single generation.

The prince formally dissolves the occupation, withdraws every garrison beyond a one-day march, and elevates the city to a tributary ally with a perpetual charter of self-rule, extracting a fixed annual tribute and a mutual non-interference oath that explicitly forbids his troops from entering the walls unless the city’s own senate requests intervention. Simultaneously, he stations a rapid-reaction legion in a neighboring loyal territory under a separate command, funded by a fraction of the tribute, with sealed orders to annihilate the city’s government only if an overt rebellion is declared, ensuring that any future crackdown is framed as a punitive expedition against oath-breakers rather than a re-conquest.

The prince issues an irrevocable charter abolishing all direct taxation on the city’s citizens for twenty years while simultaneously creating a new hereditary “Patriciate of the Prince’s Grace,” enrolling the heads of the two hundred wealthiest merchant and guild families with lifetime stipends drawn from a dedicated share of the port tolls and the exclusive right to purchase state monopolies in grain and silk at fixed discount prices. This instantly re-anchors the city’s elite economic calculus: their fortunes now rise or fall on the prince’s continued favor, turning the same networks that once funded republican conspiracies into a self-policing cartel that would crush any restoration attempt to protect their sinecures.

Relocate the royal court permanently into the captured city’s ancestral palace, and bind the prince’s bloodline to the three wealthiest merchant houses through strategic marriages that make the elite co-investors in dynastic stability. Over a generation, dissolve the old elective magistracies into appointed patrimonial offices and rewrite the civic calendar so that every festival and legal precedent flows from the prince’s personal presence, seeding a collective memory where the republic is an interregnum, not the origin.

judge.note: Strategies 1&2 underestimate resistance costs; 3&4 assume elite capture holds.

theme: whether to be FEARED or LOVED by newly acquired subjects
problem: You have just conquered a prosperous but defiant city-state whose wealthy merchants desire stability while its civic pride smolders with the memory of overthrowing its previous overlord. To hold it without bleeding your forces, you must choose whether the city’s fear of your wrath or its love for your rule will be the foundation of your authority—but the decision must be made now, before the next harvest.
facets: The city’s strategic location and trade wealth are vital to your treasury.
The population’s collective memory of past rebellions could inspire new resistance.
Your own reputation among your other subjects will be shaped by how you treat this city.

angles.directive: Raze the city walls, execute the ringleaders of the old regime, and impose a heavy-handed military governor who rules by terror.
Withdraw your main army, leaving only a garrison, and decree that the city will be governed by its own council under your distant suzerainty; buy time while you attend to more pressing threats.
Declare the city a free trade zone, grant its leading families titles and seats in your court, and marry into their aristocracy to bind their interests to yours.

Uproot the entire citizenry and redistribute them to your other lands, repopulating the city with veteran soldiers and loyal settlers who will treat it as a military colony, erasing its rebellious identity.

threads: To hold the city, I will execute the fifty most prominent merchant-council families in a public square and then demolish the central granary, forcing the population to rely on my military governor’s ration distribution for survival through the next harvest.

Install a merchant-led oligarchic council to administer daily affairs and collect the harvest tax, while pulling the field army back to the frontier and leaving only a skeleton garrison in the citadel to signal distant suzerainty. This converts the city into a self-policing client state that defers the cost of suppression and buys operational freedom to confront the larger external threat.

I issue an imperial decree converting the city into a perpetual free trade nexus under my direct protection, exempting its merchant fleets from all transit tolls and anchoring its wealth to my realm’s stability. I then appoint the heads of the three controlling shipping houses as hereditary trade lords to my privy council with binding counsel on all eastern tariffs, and wed the eldest daughter of the most solvent dynasty to fuse their bloodline’s survival to the survival of my own.

All current inhabitants will be rounded up during the next market week and dispersed in tethered family groups to pre-assigned agrarian parcels across our core provinces, severing every communal tie to this soil. Simultaneously, we decant the city with four thousand veterans discharged on-site under hereditary land titles and a charter that mandates military service as the price of residence, transforming it into a self-policing armed camp.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes deportation logistics feasible; downstream merchant flight underexplored.

theme: MERCENARIES vs CITIZEN-ARMS vs auxiliaries for the state's defense
problem: You must hold a newly conquered, restive province rich in trade but bordered by a rival kingdom. Your own citizen militia is depleted and needed at home, mercenaries are effective but notoriously treacherous, and the powerful ally who lent you auxiliaries now eyes the province for himself.
facets: The province’s wealth is vital to finance your regime, but its nobles hate foreign rule and will rebel unless crushed or co-opted.
Your citizen army is loyal but exhausted and cannot be spared without leaving your capital vulnerable to internal conspiracies.
The auxiliaries from your ally are well-equipped and already garrisoned, but their commander has secret orders to absorb the province if you appear weak.

angles.directive: Hire ruthless veteran mercenaries with a promise of plunder, annihilate the rebellious nobles and any auxiliaries who hesitate, and rule by terror to extinguish resistance before the rival kingdom can intervene.

Withdraw all your forces, fortify your own borders, and secretly leak intelligence that the province is a trap for the rival kingdom. Let the ally and the rival bleed each other over the spoils while you rebuild your citizen army in safety.

Offer the province’s leading families full citizenship, senate seats, and command of a new local militia under your banner. Arm them to defend their own wealth, binding their fate to yours and turning them against both the ally and the rival.

Declare the province a free city under your personal protection, grant it a charter of self-rule, and evacuate all foreign troops. Reframe your role from conqueror to guarantor, so its fear of external conquest forces it to rely on you without garrison costs.

threads:
Hire the Iron Companies with sealed writs granting them full sack of every noble estate in the province, then stage a victory feast where we poison the allied auxiliaries' officers and turn the mercenaries loose to butcher their leaderless garrisons mid-carousal.  
Plant a forest of impaled corpses along the trade road to the rival kingdom, each bearing a placard of the noble house we liquidated, and announce that any merchant who fails to pay triple tariffs will join them.

Pull the citizen militia back to our core territories under cover of a fabricated internal coup, while planting doctored border-intelligence with double agents that frames the province as a pre-sighted kill box where allied auxiliaries are dug in to ambush any rival advance. Leak to the rival kingdom’s spy network that the ally intends to annex the province within days, then sever all official channels so the allied garrison commander interprets the silence as betrayal and digs in deeper, keeping both enemies fixed on each other while we reconstitute our levy behind fortified borders.

By senatorial decree, I will enfranchise the province’s hundred wealthiest families as full citizens, allocate them one-third of the senate’s seats, and authorize them to raise and officer a provincial legion financed by a new tariff on the trade routes they control. Their legion will garrison the province against the rival kingdom and our ally’s auxiliaries alike, while their political fortunes become inseparable from the survival of our enlarged state.

We dissolve the occupation by issuing an irrevocable charter of sovereignty to the provincial burghers and local nobility, creating a formally neutral Free City whose legal independence is underwritten solely by our personal guarantee of protection against the rival kingdom and our former ally alike. We immediately withdraw every foreign soldier—mercenary, auxiliary, and our own militia—to the home frontier, converting the province from a garrisoned liability into a desperately self-reliant buffer state that must fund and arm itself while paying us a contracted protection tribute it cannot revoke without inviting conquest by the two powers now pressing its borders.

judge.note: Strategy 2's double-agent cascade risks uncontrolled escalation; Strategy 4 assumes burghers can self-arm credibly.

theme: a MIXED principality: a newly annexed territory of different language and customs
problem: You have seized a flourishing maritime republic whose language, laws, and civic traditions differ sharply from your own. The deposed senate has fled to a rival power, while the populace seethes under your garrison and secretly funds their cause. How do you prevent the province from becoming an endless drain or a rebellion?
facets: The city’s prosperity depends on trade routes that would be devastated by violent repression, mass emigration, or a prolonged siege.
The exiled senate enjoys the backing of a powerful neighbor who will use any revolt as a pretext to intervene and weaken your position.
Your own nobles expect new lands and spoils; showing excessive mercy risks appearing weak and inviting conspiracies at home.
