angles.directive: Round up the exiled senate’s remaining kin, publicly execute them, and unleash a decisive terror to annihilate any thought of defiance—crushing dissent before it can organize.

Maintain a disciplined but unobtrusive garrison, avoid altering local customs, and let the old republican generation age and fade while you wait for the memory of self-rule to weaken—buying time with symbolic concessions.

Enroll the wealthiest merchant houses into your own council, grant them trade monopolies and tax exemptions, and bind their bloodlines to yours through marriage, so their interests become indistinguishable from your rule.

Resettle a third of your own loyal subjects in the city, impose your language as the official tongue, abolish the old assemblies, and legally amalgamate the territory into your crownlands so it ceases to be a separate people with a common memory of liberty.

threads: Seize every relative of the exiled senate—down to second cousins—and execute them en masse on the city’s main trading square, the corpses left on display for three days as criers announce that any vessel whose owner fails to publicly renounce the exiles and swear fealty within 48 hours will be burned at anchor with its crew aboard. Simultaneously, impound all merchant guild treasuries and use the confiscated silver to fund a network of local informants paid per denunciation, ensuring that every whisper of dissent is met with a midnight arrest and a summary gallows sentence before dawn.

You will maintain the garrison at one-third visible strength, quartered outside the walls except for a rotating ceremonial honor guard at the former senate house, while issuing an imperial edict that formally "confirms" all existing municipal charters, trade privileges, and festival calendars in perpetuity—then wait ten years without a single new tax, appointment, or legal reform, so that the exiled senate's war fund dries up not from interdiction but from their donors' growing sense that the cause has become abstract and the occupation irrelevant.

I will charter a “Consortium of the Sea” composed of the six wealthiest native merchant houses, granting them hereditary monopoly over all port customs and the right to outfit armed trade fleets under my flag, in exchange for upfront payment of the garrison’s annual cost and a fixed tribute stream that I distribute as landed spoils to my own nobles. Simultaneously, I will issue an imperial decree mandating that within one generation the primary heir of each consortium house must marry a scion of my court aristocracy, making any restoration of the exiled senate an immediate confiscation of their grandchildren’s titles and trade routes.

To neutralize the city as a coherent political entity, you will transplant 30% of its urban population to interior agricultural estates and replace them with veteran families drawn from your core territories, while simultaneously dissolving the mercantile guilds and transferring their charters to crown-chartered monopolies managed by your own nobility. The former republic will be legally erased by incorporating its territory as a royal demesne under your direct jurisdiction, with all public records, legal proceedings, and commercial contracts conducted exclusively in your language within one generation.

judge.note: Strategies 1&4 risk spiral; 2 assumes donor fatigue; 3 ignores marriage resistance.

theme: holding a newly conquered HEREDITARY state whose old ruling bloodline still has popular sympathy
problem: You have seized a hereditary duchy, but the late duke’s infant son has been hidden by loyalists; the populace adores the old bloodline, so any open cruelty might spark a revolt, yet letting the heir survive guarantees endless conspiracies.
facets: Deep popular reverence for the old dynasty means direct violence against the child would be seen as sacrilege and could unite passive resentment into active rebellion.
The heir’s very existence is a beacon for foreign powers, who might shelter him and later invade under the banner of restoring the legitimate line.
Your military control is tenuous: troops are few, supply lines long, and the local nobility still command the loyalty of the peasant militias.

angles.directive: Track down the child and eliminate him along with the entire network of loyalist nobles in a single, overwhelming operation, then publicly display the bodies to demonstrate that the old line is irrevocably extinguished and resistance is futile.

Negotiate the heir’s surrender under a false amnesty, then adopt him as your own ward, betroth him to your infant daughter, and rule as his regent—thus grafting your dynasty onto the old one and redirecting the people’s love toward the new blended house.

Install a distant cousin of the old duke as a puppet ruler, letting him govern visibly while you control him from behind the throne, and send the true heir to a trusted foreign ally’s court as a “guest” hostage, so that any rebellion would have to first depose their own native prince.

Proclaim the duchy abolished and in its place found a republic with a popular assembly, redistributing noble estates to commoners and granting new civic rights, thus reframing the conflict as liberation from hereditary tyranny and making the old bloodline politically meaningless.

threads: I will decree a public Day of Reconciliation, promising to enthrone the infant as a puppet duke under my regency, thereby compelling the loyalist network to produce the child and all its noble sponsors at the cathedral square; at the moment the heir is raised before the crowd, hidden crossbowmen on the rooftops will execute the child, the attending nobles, and their immediate kin in a single volley, after which I will have the corpses stripped, branded with the mark of treason, and stacked in a pyramid beneath the duke’s own statue as a permanent monument to the extinction of the old line.

Issue a public decree of full amnesty and invite the loyalists to present the infant heir to the ducal court, then in a single ceremony adopt him as my son, formally betroth him to my infant daughter, and install myself as regent so that the old bloodline is legally absorbed into mine. Suppress any dissenting loyalists through the legal authority of the regency, while redirecting all popular reverence and foreign claims onto the new blended dynasty that I now unilaterally control.

We install Lord Aldric, a pliable cadet of the old dynasty, as the visible duke with full ceremonial authority while we retain military and fiscal control through a regency council of our own officers; simultaneously, we dispatch the true infant heir as a "ward" to the court of King Valdemar of the neighboring kingdom, converting him into a hostage whose survival forces any domestic uprising to first depose their own native puppet and any foreign intervention to bargain for a child whose return would dissolve our figurehead’s legitimacy.

By decree, we dissolve the ducal title entirely and declare a republic, immediately breaking up the late duke’s estates into freehold parcels distributed to the peasant militiamen who register in the new popular assembly. This converts the conflict from “usurper versus true heir” into a material struggle between commoners with fresh land titles and any noble faction attempting to restore a bloodline that now stands for dispossession.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 assume perfect operational security; 4 ignores noble backlash.

theme: securing HONEST COUNSEL while surrounded by flatterers, without eroding authority
problem: A prince newly installed in a conquered city-state suspects his court is full of flatterers concealing vital intelligence, yet openly testing their honesty could reveal indecision and invite rebellion.
facets: The conquered elites may be plotting, so truthful counsel is a matter of survival.
Any display of doubt or ignorance will be exploited by rival factions seeking to unseat him.
The prince’s own pride makes it difficult to solicit criticism without seeming weak.

angles.directive: Publicly accuse a prominent flatterer of treason, stage a dramatic trial to extract confessions, and use the spectacle to terrorize the court into brutal honesty.
Retreat from the court entirely, claiming a period of secluded study to audit the city’s affairs; only a few low-born clerks are allowed access, evading the flatterers’ reach.

Establish a formal 'Council of Truth' where selected nobles are granted the protected privilege to speak freely in exchange for binding oaths of loyalty, integrating potential dissidents into a controlled channel.
Proclaim that true strength lies in hearing unwelcome facts, publicly reward the first courtier who dares to contradict him, and reframe authority itself as the capacity to thrive on challenge.

threads:
Arrest Lord Varo, the court’s most conspicuous sycophant, at his own celebratory feast, charge him with secreting rebel correspondence, and convene an immediate night tribunal where irons and a forged letter extract a confession naming three other nobles as co-conspirators.  
Execute all four at dawn in the city square on a scaffold erected before the palace gates, leaving their severed heads facing the throne room window so that every courtier who enters understands that silence now carries a single, final penalty.

The prince publicly declares a six-week seclusion in the west tower to personally reconstruct the city’s true financial and administrative state from raw tax ledgers and grain inventories, ordering that only three newly appointed clerks of proven obscurity—former dock scribes with no noble patrons—be permitted to bring documents and answer direct factual inquiries during that time. This physical and procedural quarantine severs the flatterers’ access while building an incontrovertible, impersonal audit trail that reveals plots through discrepancies in the ledgers rather than through hazardous personal interviews.

The prince will create by decree a chartered “Council of Truth,” selecting exactly seven conquered nobles with known grievances, and require each to swear a witnessed blood-oath of personal loyalty as the price of an irrevocable grant of speaking immunity within its sessions. He will then assign them the rotating duty of critiquing specific policy proposals in writing before each council meeting, using their obligatory, protected dissent as a calibrated instrument to smoke out sedition and quantify court sentiment while framing the whole apparatus as a magnanimous fusion of local honor with princely rule.

The prince issues an edict declaring that true sovereignty lies in the capacity to absorb unwelcome facts, then immediately elevates the first courtier who delivers a direct, risky contradiction to a public office titled "Chancellor of Candor" with a land grant and a ceremonial honor, making the reward both visible and irreversible. This single act reengineers the court’s incentive structure: contradiction becomes the highest form of loyalty display, and the prince’s former vulnerability—his pride—is converted into the very mechanism that surfaces concealed threats.

judge.note: Strategies 1&2 lack downstream resistance modeling; 3&4 underestimate oath-breaking/incentive gaming.

theme: rewarding the OVER-MIGHTY NOBLE whose aid won the throne but who now rivals the prince
problem: Duke Alfonso's army and influence secured your throne, but now he demands a co-regent’s power, your son is married to his daughter, and any open move against him may ignite a civil war while foreign enemies watch.
facets: Alfonso commands the largest private army and holds key fortresses, making military confrontation costly and uncertain.
Your heir is married to Alfonso's daughter, so harming the Duke could estrange your son and split the dynasty.
Alfonso retains fierce loyalty among the nobility who view him as the real guarantor of stability, and neighboring states would exploit any internal fracture.

angles.directive: Arrest Duke Alfonso on fabricated evidence of conspiracy, execute him swiftly, and purge his loyalists from court and military posts, accepting the risk of immediate noble uprising.

Elevate Alfonso to the ceremonial title of Grand Constable, grant him a distant governorship with lavish honors, while gradually absorbing his veteran troops into your own army under trusted captains.

Commission Alfonso to lead a prestigious but grinding border war against a rival kingdom, providing just enough support to keep him occupied and weakened far from the capital, hoping the campaign consumes his strength.

Secretly supply the Papal Inquisitor with documents suggesting Alfonso harbors heretical sects, inviting an ecclesiastical investigation that strips him of castles and influence under church authority, then publicly pardon him as an act of mercy to bind him by gratitude.

threads: We plant forged correspondence with the seal of the Velarian Empire, arrest Alfonso at swordpoint during a full council session, and execute him on the marble floor before any voice can rise. Simultaneously, pre-drafted warrants deploy our household troops to seize his four key fortresses, detain his captains, and install loyalist officers in every garrison command.

I will issue a royal decree appointing Alfonso as Grand Constable and Governor of the distant, prosperous but isolated province of Montverde, complete with a newly built palatial estate and a lifetime stipend, while simultaneously ordering the incremental rotation of his veteran regiments into the royal army under my own hand-picked captains—ostensibly for "imperial defense modernization"—with each transferred unit receiving a pay raise and a public commendation bearing Alfonso’s seal, so that his power base dissolves before he realizes the honor is a gilded exile.

Appoint Alfonso supreme commander of the Marches with a public mandate to reclaim the contested Dornish uplands, while secretly capping his supply convoys and reinforcement drafts to precisely 60% of what his war council requests—just enough to sustain a grinding stalemate that bleeds his private levies and erodes his reputation as an invincible guarantor, all while the capital’s garrison quietly fortifies under loyalist officers.

We will funnel forged evidence of heretical worship to the Papal Inquisitor, triggering a Church-mandated seizure of Alfonso’s castles and military command under canon law. Immediately after, we issue a public pardon that spares his life and title, converting him from a disarmed threat into a grateful client bound by mercy without fracturing the succession.

judge.note: Strategies 1&4 risk backfire; 2&3 assume Alfonso won't detect gradual displacement.

theme: FORTUNE vs CAUTION: whether to seize a sudden, fickle opportunity to expand
problem: A plague devastates a rival city-state, leaving its treasury and granaries intact but its army dead; your spies confirm the opportunity, yet an unusually harsh winter approaches, and your own coffers are dangerously low after last year's failed harvest.
facets: Seizing the city requires immediate, deficit-financed mobilization that could break your state if the winter lingers or the populace resists.
The rival's ruling council has already sent envoys offering voluntary submission in exchange for food aid, blurring the line between conquest and entrapment.
Neighboring powers currently hesitate, but a rapid move by you might unify them into a preemptive coalition against your sudden expansion.

angles.directive: Strip your own granaries to fund a lightning winter march, sack the rival treasury immediately upon entry, and use the captured wealth to buy emergency grain from overseas merchants before your own people starve—crushing any local resistance by executing the ruling council that offered submission as traitors to their own city.

Publicly decline the opportunity, send a token food shipment to the envoys as a gesture of mercy, and secretly encourage a weaker third power to attempt the seizure instead—waiting for them to become overextended and despised by the desperate populace before you move in spring as a liberator.

Accept the ruling council's offer of submission but demand their treasury be moved to your capital 'for safekeeping,' marry your daughter to the plague-orphaned heir they are protecting, and establish a joint grain rationing system that binds their surviving citizens to yours through shared dependency and debt.

Declare the plague a divine judgment and yourself the ordained instrument of purification—rather than conquering, announce you will permanently resettle a portion of your own loyal population into the empty city as a 'sacred colony,' absorbing the rival treasury as a religious tithe and reframing the expansion as a holy migration that no neighboring power dares oppose for fear of sacrilege.

threads: Strip the state granaries to sustain a forced winter march that seizes the rival treasury within a fortnight, executing the entire ruling council upon entry as traitors who bartered a city they could not hold. Use the captured bullion immediately to contract emergency grain shipments from neutral overseas merchants, routing the purchases through back-channel agents to preempt any coalition embargo.

We publicly broadcast a decree of non-aggression and dispatch a single grain transport as a mercy offering to the envoys, while our agents funnel falsified treasury inventories and understated winter-route forecasts to the cash-poor but militarily restless Veradian League, nudging them into a deficit-mobilized winter seizure. Once Veradian occupation forces are depleted by starvation and local reprisals, we march in spring with conscripted labor columns, seizing the city as liberators and liquidating the League’s abandoned siege stores to recoup our delayed costs.

By formally accepting the envoys’ submission while conditioning protection on the immediate physical transfer of their treasury to your vaults, marrying your daughter to their orphaned heir to fuse bloodlines into a single succession, and instituting a unified grain-debt ledger that converts every ration issued to their populace into a personal bond redeemable through labor and loyalty to your household, you transform a military conquest into a permanent financial and dynastic absorption that leaves neighboring powers no legal casus belli and no independent city-state to rally behind.

We proclaim the plague as divine judgment and ourselves as the cleansing hand, then permanently transplant a portion of our loyal but strained populace into the vacant city as a "sacred colony" governed by temple law, absorbing the rival treasury in its entirety as a sanctified tithe; this reframes expansion as a holy resettlement mission rather than conquest, funding immediate grain imports for the winter from the seized coffers while paralyzing neighboring powers with the fear of committing sacrilege by opposing a divinely ordained purification.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 4 assume implausibly perfect information control and enemy compliance.

theme: the fate of DEPOSED HEIRS and the old ruling family after seizing power
problem: You have seized a prosperous city-state whose people remain fervently loyal to the last heir of the deposed dynasty, a 10-year-old boy sheltered by a neutral neighboring lord; leaving him alive risks a future uprising, but killing him could create a martyr and deepen resentment.
facets: The populace’s deep sentimental attachment to the old dynasty, which views the orphaned boy as a living symbol of their lost liberty and identity.
The boy’s refuge under a neutral but opportunistic neighbor who can shield him or trade him for diplomatic concessions.
The child’s malleable age—capable of being shaped into a puppet, a unifying figurehead, or a dangerous claimant as he grows.

angles.directive: Send assassins to kill the boy, extinguishing the bloodline and demonstrating that no rival is beyond your reach, regardless of sanctuary.
Leave the heir untouched but launch a sustained whispering campaign that he is a bastard or impostor, eroding his legitimacy without bloodshed while you consolidate power and win popular favor.
Negotiate with the guardian lord to bring the boy to your court as a ward, then marry him to your own daughter, merging the dynasties and neutralizing him through familial bonds and shared interest.

Declare the old dynasty irrevocably cursed by divine judgment, institute a new civic cult that sanctifies your rule, and reframe political legitimacy around merit and divine favor rather than bloodline.

threads: Plant a cook-turned-sleeper in the boy’s household to administer microdoses of glass-filings over weeks, inducing an untraceable wasting death that the lord’s own physicians will diagnose as congenital organ failure. Once the pallid corpse is displayed, we flood the city-state with anonymous dirges declaring the gods themselves snuffed out a feeble, tainted line, leaving no rallying cry—only a collective exhale of exhausted finality.

Wedeploy trained rumormongers and forgers to circulate, through trade caravans, temple networks, and disaffected palace servants, a consistent and documented narrative that the boy is a bastard born of a lady-in-waiting's affair, seeded with falsified birth records and midwife testimonies to make the dynasty's own bloodline the source of doubt. Simultaneously, we extend to the neutral lord a public guarantee of non-aggression and a private trade concession, framing his continued custody as a transactional hostage-holding rather than a sacred guardianship, so that when the boy emerges he is already seen by the populace as a disputed impostor and his protector as a mercenary, not a crusader.

Marry the boy to your daughter under a binding matrilineal pact that legally subordinates his bloodline claim to your house, then install him as a permanent junior co-regent whose formal title depends entirely on his consort's lineage.

I will issue a proclamation that the old dynasty's fall was decreed by the gods as an irreversible curse, simultaneously founding a civic cult of "Divine Efficacy" where legitimacy is conferred solely through demonstrated merit and public miracles, not lineage. To neutralize the boy, I will offer his protector a ritualized ultimatum: deliver the child to be consecrated as the cult's first oracle-priest—a living relic of the old curse redeemed only through service—or watch as my priesthood declares his bloodline a contagion, making any who harbor him spiritually unclean and any uprising a sacrilege against the new divine order.

judge.note: Strategies 1,2 assume flawless execution; 3,4 underestimate protector resistance.

theme: a MIXED principality: a newly annexed territory of different language and customs
problem: You have conquered a wealthy maritime duchy whose language and customs differ from your own. Your treasury is empty, your army is needed elsewhere to face an imminent external threat, and the local nobles, who command the merchant fleet and militia, seethe with resentment. You must secure lasting control quickly without bankrupting your state or provoking a rebellion that invites foreign intervention.
facets: Your treasury is drained from the war of conquest, making a standing army's extended presence unsustainable.
Local nobles control vital trade routes and could paralyze commerce or invite a rival power to seize the duchy.
The population's distinct identity makes cultural assimilation a slow, volatile process where harsh measures could ignite a popular uprising.

angles.directive: Publicly execute the leading nobles, confiscate their estates, and install garrisons in the main cities to rule by terror, breaking any organized resistance before your troops depart—accepting the risk of a bloody uprising while your forces are still present.

Withdraw your forces entirely and renounce the claim, installing a powerless local figurehead as a neutral buffer state. This frees resources for the external threat and avoids a costly, indefinite occupation.

Forge a marriage alliance with the most powerful noble house, granting the duchy broad internal autonomy in exchange for a fixed annual tribute and naval support. Turn a resentful elite into a subordinate, invested partner.

Relocate a third of the native population to your heartland and resettle their lands with loyal citizens from your core territories. Over a generation, dismantle the distinct cultural identity that fuels resistance and create a permanently pacified province.

threads: We immediately seize the twelve highest-grossing merchant lords during a summoned council, hang them from the harbor cranes, and transfer their fleets and warehouses to the crown to liquidate into garrison pay for the next three months. Then we raze the duchy's central temple to provoke the inevitable popular rising on our schedule, so the army breaks it in a single pitched battle before redeploying to the border.

We install a ceremonial Doge from a minor cadet branch despised by the merchant lords, then renounce our claim and withdraw all forces, binding the newly declared "Free Port" by treaty to grant our navy permanent basing rights and exclusive tariff-free passage in return for recognizing its neutrality.  
This liquidates the occupation’s costs overnight, converts the duchy into a costless buffer that denies harbor to rival fleets, and traps the local nobles in a power-balance they cannot overturn without inviting our swift naval interdiction.

Marry the heir to the sitting grand duke’s eldest daughter, installing him as hereditary viceroy with full internal jurisdiction in exchange for 15% annual tribute and permanent naval command of the merchant fleet. This immediate, low-cost integration transforms the ducal house into a tax-collecting ally whose commercial interests now depend on our suzerainty, neutralizing rebellion while funding the external war.

I will issue an edict for the systematic transfer of one-third of the duchy’s population to our heartland, while simultaneously parceling their vacated lands and properties to veterans and loyal settlers from our core territories.  
This engineered demographic inversion will, within a generation, dissolve the native cultural base that fuels resistance, turning the duchy into a permanently pacified extension of our realm.

judge.note: Strategy 4 borders fantasy; Strategy 2 underestimates noble agency.

theme: FORTRESSES vs the goodwill of the people as the prince's true defense
problem: The prince has spent years building an unassailable mountain citadel, but now a rival army approaches not to besiege the fortress, but to encircle the valley and starve the populace into surrendering the prince within; he must choose whether to abandon his fortress for the people's affection or trust in its walls while their loyalty erodes.
facets: The fortress is physically impregnable but psychologically isolating, turning subjects' fear into resentment the moment they feel the prince is safe while they suffer.
The goodwill of the people is a living defense: it can melt away overnight with a single famine or flourish into an unbreachable human shield, but it cannot be stockpiled like stone.
The approaching enemy is not merely military but political, spreading propaganda that the fortress proves the prince always intended to save himself first.

angles.directive: Throw open the citadel gates as a public granary and infirmary for the valley, declaring that the fortress was never a refuge for one man but a stronghold for the entire people — turn the symbol of separation into the engine of shared survival, daring the enemy to assault a fortress filled with the families they claim to liberate.

Descend alone into the valley before the encirclement tightens, publicly execute the fortress's architect for building a monument to fear, and negotiate a conditional surrender that installs you as the enemy commander's indispensable local governor — trade the citadel for a seat inside the new order.

Slip out through the fortress's secret postern with a handful of loyal guards, melt into the mountains, and let the enemy take the empty citadel while you wage a guerrilla campaign from the villages — force them to choose between garrisoning stone walls and pacifying a countryside that still believes its absent prince will return.

Remain inside the fortress and do nothing to relieve the valley, broadcasting that any suffering under the siege is the enemy's cruelty, not yours — stake everything on the ancient logic that a prince who outlasts the siege outlasts the hatred, and rebuild obedience through sheer survival once the exhausted enemy withdraws.

threads: The prince orders the fortress granaries unsealed and the great hall converted into a public infirmary, issuing a valley-wide proclamation that every household may send its grain stores and its sick within the citadel walls, thereby making the fortress a shared survival infrastructure rather than a private retreat. This operational pivot transforms the physical stronghold into a hostage population center that the enemy cannot assault without slaughtering the very families their propaganda claims to defend, while the prince’s visible presence among the people inside dissolves the narrative of separation.

The prince descends unarmed into the tightening cordon, personally beheads the fortress's architect before the gathered populace as a ritual renunciation of isolated power, and uses that public apostasy to reframe his capitulation as a gift—delivering both the valley's submission and his own unforged administrative network directly to the enemy commander. In exchange, he demands appointment as the valley's hereditary satrap under the new regime, converting his fortress's remaining supplies into a bargaining chip for a governorship that makes him indispensable for tax collection and order, thereby dissolving the siege into a merger where he keeps his authority in a different box.

The prince uses the secret postern to vanish with a handpicked escort into the high passes, ceding the physical fortress to the enemy while preserving his own body as a mobile symbol of resistance. From the villages, he wages a denial campaign—burning forage, ambushing supply convoys, and broadcasting his survival through word and deed—so the occupiers must either disperse their forces across hostile terrain to hunt him or sit uselessly behind stone walls while the countryside remains ungovernable.

He will seal the citadel and deploy a mounted crier corps to saturate the valley with the message that each death is a deliberate atrocity inflicted by the encircling enemy, not his absence, while his continued visible presence on the battlements redefines the fortress as the enduring heart of the realm rather than a refuge. Once the enemy's supply lines collapse and they withdraw, he will descend with the untouched grain reserves to monopolize the distribution of sustenance, converting the survivors' trauma into a rebuilt obedience that mistakes the cessation of famine for the return of his benevolence.

judge.note: Strategies 2 and 4 assume enemy rationality/propaganda success unrealistically.

theme: a CIVIL principality: a prince elevated by fellow citizens, balancing the nobles against the people
problem: Elevated by the common citizens against the nobles, you now face demands to crush the nobles who are arming in their estates, while a neighboring king waits to exploit your internal strife. How do you secure your civil principality?
facets: The nobles retain private strongholds and sworn arms, making them capable of a bloody revolt if provoked.
The people who raised you are fickle; failing to deliver swift vengeance will brand you a traitor and ignite popular fury.
A powerful neighbor with a standing army has sent envoys to both factions, ready to back whichever side you oppose.

angles.directive: Strike first: arrest and execute the noble ringleaders immediately, confiscate their estates, and distribute the land to the people, accepting the risk of a short, decisive civil war that deters foreign meddling by demonstrating ruthless unity.

Play for time: make solemn promises to both sides, establish endless commissions of inquiry, and secretly build your own guard; let the nobles and people exhaust each other’s patience, hoping the neighboring king loses interest in a protracted, indeterminate quagmire.

Refashion the state: invite leading nobles and popular spokesmen into a Great Council with shared legislative power and tax authority, binding them through interlocking offices, marriages, and mutual dependence so that both estates hold a stake in your survival and reject foreign intervention.

Appoint a ruthless outsider—a foreign condottiero or magistrate—with full authority to pacify the nobles by whatever means necessary; once the bloodshed is done, publicly try and execute that deputy for cruelty, thereby cleansing yourself of blame and regaining the love of the people while the nobles are broken.

threads: I summon every titled landholder to a closed feast under a royal writ of safe-conduct, and the moment the last one enters my great hall, I have my crossbowmen seal the doors and execute them in their seats, confiscating all their estates by noon decree. I partition the seized manors into hereditary freeholds for the urban poor and the peasant militias who first raised me, then dispatch a single envoy to the neighboring king with a map of my borders and a message: “Any foreign step inside will trigger a scorched-earth mobilization of every new landholder, leaving you nothing to conquer but ashes.”

By issuing identical royal writs that grant investigative authority to a rotating committee of commoners and nobles whose membership shifts every thirty days, I ensure the commission never reaches a verdict while I divert confiscated church silver into a mercenary household guard sworn only to my seal.

I will summon the twelve most powerful nobles and the twelve elected tribunes of the people into a single Great Council with exclusive legislative and tax-raising powers, then bind them through a lattice of intermarriages, joint oversight of the treasury, and offices that cannot operate without cross-faction countersignatures, so that each estate’s capacity to act independently is paralyzed and both must defend the new settlement against any foreign-backed rival.

I will hire a foreign condottiero with a fearsome reputation, granting him sealed writs of absolute authority to storm the nobles’ estates, seize their strongholds, and execute any who resist without pause or negotiation. Once the last stronghold is ash and the nobles’ power shattered, I will have him arrested mid-banquet, condemn him in a public show trial for atrocities committed in my name, and hang him before the cheering crowd to transfer all blood-debt onto his corpse.

judge.note: Strategy 4 relies on condottiero arrest working; assumes crowd loyalty post-bloodshed.

theme: the TIMING AND DOSAGE OF CRUELTY — all at once vs spread out — to pacify a disorderly new state
problem: You have just conquered a proud republic with a deeply ingrained civic identity and an armed populace. The city is outwardly subdued but seething with conspiracy; you must use cruelty to secure it, yet the timing and dosage of that cruelty will determine whether you crush the threat or ignite a new revolt.
facets: The city has a long tradition of self-governance and sees any new ruler as a tyrant, making mass obedience fragile.
A well-organized citizen militia controls the city's fortifications and can mobilize quickly if provoked.
Neighboring rival states are watching for an opening, ready to support internal rebels the moment you appear weak.

angles.directive: Execute all known conspirators and their entire families in a single night of terror, then immediately declare a general amnesty and distribute seized property to the poor, so the shock is complete and the benefits follow swiftly.

Install a loyal but expendable local puppet as governor, grant him a free hand to conduct gradual purges and extortions, while you remain in the background; later, when the city's hatred reaches its peak, depose the puppet and present yourself as the merciful liberator.

Offer the leading patrician families seats in your privy council, marry the daughter of the most influential clan, and fund public festivals that celebrate the city's heritage under your patronage, absorbing the elite into your regime and replacing cruelty with mutual interest.

Promulgate a new charter that restores the city's ancient council and popular assemblies, but with your appointees holding permanent veto power; you redefine the city as a 'protected free state' under your perpetual guardianship, making open coercion unnecessary by turning its own institutions into instruments of control.

threads: We will draw up a single, exhaustive registry of every known conspirator, then, in a synchronized hour across all quarters, breach their gates, slaughter every member of their households, and leave the corpses displayed as a spectacle of totality. At first light, we issue an irrevocable amnesty to all unlisted citizens and immediately transfer the seized estates, coin, and chattel directly to the guilds and the landless, creating a beneficiary class with a material stake in the old order’s eradication.

We will install the disgraced former treasurer, Cassian, as nominal governor with full authority to purge the militia’s company captains and levy a “security levy” on every household, while our main force remains encamped outside the walls as a silent guarantor. In twelve months, once his network of informants and debtors has concentrated the city’s hatred onto his person, we will publicly try and hang him for treason, annul his decrees, and reopen the old assembly under our protection, framing ourselves as the merciful restorer of civic order.

I order the immediate installation of the seven patrician clan heads as hereditary councillors with irrevocable veto power over any new direct tax, and I seal the alliance by marrying the daughter of the house that controls the citadel’s armory and grain stores, making their hoarded resources functionally my state treasury. Simultaneously, I publicly disband the citizen militia and re-commission its unit captains as salaried, unarmed masters of a newly endowed annual festival celebrating the city’s founding, thereby converting the armed threat into a state-funded ceremonial dependency.

I promulgate a new constitutional charter reconstituting the city's ancient council and popular assemblies, but every legislative act requires the countersignature of my appointed prefects who hold permanent veto power; simultaneously, I declare the city a "protected free state" under my perpetual guardianship, converting its own civic institutions into a self-administering mechanism of control.

judge.note: Strategy 3 assumes clan heads won't weaponize veto; Strategy 4 underestimates prefect capture risk.

theme: FORTUNE vs CAUTION: whether to seize a sudden, fickle opportunity to expand
problem: You are a prince whose treasury and army are strained after a recent campaign; a faction in a neighboring city offers you a strategically vital fortress to intervene in their civil war, but the region's malarial marshes and your rival's rapid mobilization make the fleeting opportunity deeply perilous.
facets: The fortress commands the sole trade route through the mountains, promising immense long-term wealth.
Your veteran troops are weary and unpaid; another campaign risks mass desertion or financial collapse.
The faction extending the offer is notorious for inviting foreign protectors, then betraying them once domestic order is restored.

angles.directive: Order a full-force lightning march to seize the fortress before fever season, massacring both the rival claimant's scouts and the treacherous faction’s leaders once inside.
Declare strict neutrality, fortify your own border, and let your rival bleed his strength taking the city; strike his undefended heartland the moment he is committed.
Shower the faction with gold, titles, and the promise of semi-autonomy under your banner, sending only advisors and armaments to embroil your rival in a proxy bleeding-sore.
Leak false plans of your own advance to lure your rival into a disastrous assault through the marshes, then pivot your hidden forces to sack his capital while his army rots in the swamps.

threads: Execute a forced night march with the entire heavy infantry before the moon wanes, crossing the eastern causeway in strict silence to annihilate the rival’s forward outpost and prevent any signal fire from warning the city. Once inside the gates, detain the entire faction council during the victory feast and have them executed in the cellar to immediately install garrison commanders loyal only to our treasury.

We publicly declare perpetual neutrality over the fortress affair and immediately redeploy our unpaid veteran regiments to dig a fortified defensive line along the border facing our rival’s heartland, using our remaining coin solely for hand-to-mouth rations to avert mass desertion. Once confirmed that our rival has fully committed his main force to a siege in the malarial marshes, we execute a rapid, concentrated thrust through the unguarded mountain pass to seize his capital and granaries, collapsing his ability to sustain any further campaign.

I will grant the faction’s leaders hereditary titles under my crown, a flood of gold, and a charter of semi-autonomy, while dispatching only a corps of siege engineers and a covert arms pipeline to transform their civil war into a proxy quagmire that drains my rival’s treasury and manpower. This converts their offer into a vassal-buffer that bleeds my adversary dry without marching a single unpaid regiment into the malarial marshes.

I feed forged dispatches to the rival’s spies detailing my army’s supposed night march through the eastern marsh passes, timing the leak so his main force intercepts there as the rains flood the swamps. Meanwhile, my actual veteran core — moved in small, disguised bands by mountain goat trails — bypasses his now-empty capital and seizes it in a swift dawn assault before the fevers finish his bogged army.

judge.note: Strategies 3&4 assume rival incompetence; malaria/desertion underexplored.

theme: rewarding the OVER-MIGHTY NOBLE whose aid won the throne but who now rivals the prince
problem: You seized the throne with Duke Arnulf's military backing, yet now he demands hereditary governorship of the capital province and parades his private army; his popularity eclipses yours, and any affront could spark revolt. How do you secure your rule without becoming his hostage?
facets: The duke's loyalty is fragile—he interprets any limitation as betrayal and may turn his troops against you.
The city's commoners revere him as their defender against foreign oppression, so purging him openly would unite them in rebellion.
Your own mercenary captains are unreliable, eyeing the duke's deep coffers and might switch sides if you move against him directly.

angles.directive: Summon the duke to a banquet of reconciliation, then seize and execute him, simultaneously arresting key garrison commanders loyal to him and distributing his treasury among your mercenaries to secure their immediate obedience.

Appoint the duke as chief envoy to a distant, embattled kingdom, framing the mission as an honor only he can fulfill; while he is away, relocate his family to a remote estate as ‘guests’ and quietly replace his officers with your own men.

Offer him marriage to your sister and the newly created office of Grand Palatine, binding his power to the crown; then embed him in a supreme council where his voice is one among many, and merge his troops into the royal army under a joint command that requires your countersignature.

Stage a grand religious ceremony in which the duke is anointed as 'Guardian of the People' before the entire city, but have a forged ancient decree read aloud declaring that the Guardian serves at the pleasure of the civic council; the duke must either accept a purely ceremonial role stripped of military command or defy the gods publicly.

threads: Direct the palace guard to arrest the duke mid-toast at the banquet, then immediately order the mercenary captains to the treasury vaults for a live disbursement of his confiscated gold while sealed orders go out to loyalist sergeants to detain the three named garrison commanders before the news reaches the streets.

I will issue a royal decree appointing Duke Arnulf as “High Ambassador of the Realm’s Honor” to the embattled Kingdom of Vaskara, assigning him a ceremonial yet indefinite mission to negotiate a military alliance, thereby removing him from the capital under a guise only his prestige can sustain. Simultaneously, I will transfer his immediate family to the remote, garrisoned estate of Greymere “for their protection,” and dispatch handpicked loyalist officers with falsified transfer orders to assume command of his regiments the hour he departs, branding any resistance as treason against the crown.

I will immediately announce Duke Arnulf’s betrothal to my sister and elevate him to the new office of Grand Palatine, a title that constitutionally embeds him in a supreme council where his vote is one among five, while his private army is absorbed into the royal forces under a unified command structure requiring my personal countersignature for any deployment beyond the capital’s walls.

We forge a "lost" temple scroll stipulating that the Guardian of the People must dissolve all personal warbands and wield only ceremonial authority at the pleasure of the Civic Council, then have the High Patriarch anoint Arnulf with that title in a public rite where the scroll is read aloud—so his only options are kneeling to a god-sanctioned demotion or openly defying the divine mandate before the very populace that reveres him.

judge.note: Strategies 2&4 assume implausible compliance; Strategy 1 ignores mercenary loyalty uncertainty.

theme: whether to KEEP or BREAK a promise/treaty now that the circumstances that justified it have changed
problem: A duke who seized a rival city with a condottiere’s help promised him governorship of the conquered city and its rich hinterlands; now the captain’s entrenched garrison makes him a clear rival, and the duke must decide whether to honour the pledge or renege, knowing that open breach would trigger immediate rebellion while keeping it could cede his supremacy.
facets: The freshly conquered populace resents both rulers and will exploit any sign of weakness.
The condottiere’s mercenary troops have grown personally loyal to him rather than to the duke.
Neighbouring city-states watch closely, interpreting fidelity to treaties as a signal for future alliances.

angles.directive: Strike first: assassinate the captain and his key officers during a celebratory banquet, then produce a falsified letter proving he plotted treason, using the shock to disband his garrison and seize the city by force before opposition can coalesce.

Endless delay: announce a commission to survey the city’s boundaries and audit its revenues, keeping the captain entangled in bureaucratic negotiations for years while you secretly suborn his subordinate commanders and fortify your own position.

Bind him to your bloodline: offer the captain a prestigious court office and a marriage to your daughter, transforming his governorship into a lifetime appointment contingent on your favour, thereby merging his fortunes with your dynasty.

Keep your word fully: hand over the city and its revenues, enduring the strategic loss while proclaiming the sanctity of your pledges; use the resulting reputation for unshakeable faith to forge a league of allies who trust you, preparing to isolate the captain diplomatically over time.

threads: Assassinate the condottiere and his lieutenants mid-toast at the victory banquet with poisoned wine served by our own disguised stewards, then immediately produce a forged correspondence with a rival city-state detailing his planned coup, using the ensuing chaos to declare martial law and forcibly disband his leaderless garrison before dawn.

The duke will establish a permanent Boundary and Revenue Commission, granting the captain a seat and requiring his personal attendance at protracted quarterly hearings to resolve mapped borders and tax allocations, while the duke’s paymasters quietly purchase the loyalty of the garrison’s second-in-command and the artillery captain with lump-sum pensions and landed estates. Simultaneously, the duke will deploy his own sappers and militia to construct a ring of new star forts on the hinterland’s ridges, positioned to isolate the city from resupply, all under the guise of defending against external threats noted in the commission’s slow-walked audits.

I will summon the condottiere to my court and invest him with the hereditary title of Grand Constable of the conquered city—a lifetime office revocable solely at my pleasure—while simultaneously betrothing my eldest daughter to him under a public proclamation that designates any male issue as members of my dynasty and grants heritable manors within the hinterlands to his five senior captains as direct vassals to my bloodline. This move re-engineers his garrison into a network of landed clients whose fortunes depend entirely on my lineage, making any rebellion instantaneously strip his children of their inheritance and his captains of their new holdings, a self-annihilating calculus no rational commander will trigger.

The duke publicly invests the condottiere with an irrevocable hereditary charter to the city’s governorship and full tax collection rights, then immediately convokes a summit of neighboring city-states to form a mutual-defense league whose founding treaty names the very act of honoring his pledge as the binding proof of his fidelity. He simultaneously instructs his envoys to structure the league’s trade terms so that all members grant one another privileged market access but collectively embargo grain exports to the new governorate, making the mercenary garrison’s supply lines and payroll depend entirely on revenues that must transit allied territory under the duke’s influence.

judge.note: Strategies 2&3 assume perfect loyalty-purchase; 4 assumes league cohesion.

theme: holding a newly conquered HEREDITARY state whose old ruling bloodline still has popular sympathy
problem: You have conquered a hereditary duchy, but the late duke’s young son escaped and remains alive in the countryside, his name a rallying cry for popular sympathy; any move to secure your rule must navigate the child’s sanctity, divided nobles, and the threat of foreign intervention.
facets: The heir is a 10-year-old boy whose murder would be seen as monstrous cruelty, potentially unifying all factions against you.
The local nobility is split: some opportunistically align with you, while others covertly fund the boy’s guardians, awaiting a sign of weakness.
A powerful neighboring kingdom shares dynastic blood with the old line and is watching for pretext to invade and ‘restore’ the rightful heir.

angles.directive: Hunt the boy and his guardians without mercy, then publicly smash the ducal regalia and salt the earth of their ancestral crypt to extinguish the bloodline’s mystique through terror.
Announce that the fugitive child is an imposter and the true heir already perished, sowing confusion with forged evidence while stalling all decisive action until popular fervor exhausts itself.
Locate the heir, display overwhelming but restrained force, then magnanimously adopt him as your own son and betroth him to your daughter, making the bloodline a subordinate branch of your dynasty.
Abolish the duchy entirely, erect a popular republic under your perpetual protectorship, and reframe the old bloodline as oppressive tyrants from whom you have liberated the grateful citizens.

threads: We deploy a handpicked company of mounted rangers to systematically track the boy's circuit of safehouses using paid informants and torture-extracted confessions, then stage his public immolation as a failed "divine trial by fire" that brands his bloodline as cursed. Simultaneously, we grind the ducal crown and seal into gravel before a compelled assembly of nobles, scatter the dust into the crypt, and sow the ground with salt and quicklime so nothing grows there again.

We exhume a child’s skeleton from the siege rubble, dress it in the ducal signet, and stage a public funeral with a forged physician’s affidavit swearing the true heir died of fever, branding the living boy a foreign-deployed decoy. We then freeze all punitive expeditions and instead issue a forty-day chancery decree that grants irrevocable land title only to those who formally abjure the “imposter” on oath, letting the emotional insurgency starve against a static legal architecture that rewards material compliance over myth.

I will locate the heir with a handpicked company of household knights—overwhelming in arms but scrupulously restrained from harm—and escort him publicly to my court as a protected ward, not a prisoner.  
There, I will immediately proclaim his legal adoption as my son and announce his binding betrothal to my infant daughter, fusing the ducal bloodline into my dynasty as a subordinate cadet branch that drains all rival legitimacy into my own lineage.

I dissolve the duchy by decree, seizing its estates for a civic trust while installing a senate of handpicked commoners and guild officers who answer to my protectorate council; simultaneously, I publish a chronicle of the old bloodline’s fabricated abuses, distributing it through town criers and parish pulpits to brand the escaped boy as the last scion of a tyranny the people are now free to repudiate.

judge.note: Strategies 1&2 risk catastrophic blowback; 3&4 underestimate noble resistance.

theme: MERCENARIES vs CITIZEN-ARMS vs auxiliaries for the state's defense
problem: A rising prince must secure his newly conquered city-state against a vengeful deposed duke who rallies exiles abroad, but his treasury is empty and his own nobles plot to restore the old order.
facets: Mercenaries demand immediate gold and will loot if unpaid, turning the populace against the prince.
Citizen militia costs nothing now but arms untrained shopkeepers who may sympathize with the exiled duke or flee at first battle.
Auxiliary troops offered by a rival king come 'freely' but place the prince in debt to a power that could swallow his state whole.

angles.directive: Reframe the threat itself as the forge of unity — publicly stage the duke’s inevitable return as an existential crusade, then demand the nobles finance and lead a citizen army to prove their loyalty or be branded traitors, converting internal division into shared sacrifice.

Open secret negotiations with the deposed duke offering him a marriage alliance, a ceremonial seat on a new council, and a palatial estate within the city walls in exchange for disbanding his exiles — absorbing the rival bloodline into the regime rather than fighting it.

Abandon the exposed capital temporarily, withdrawing with loyal retainers and the city’s portable wealth to a defensible mountain fortress, provoking the duke to re-enter the city where his foreign auxiliaries will inevitably sack it, discrediting him and alienating the populace until the prince can return as liberator.

Dismiss all foreign troops and refuse to arm the citizens, instead openly disarming the city and enduring the duke’s return without resistance — betting that passive submission and the spectacle of princely ruin will shame the nobles, stir popular pity, and trigger foreign powers to intervene against a usurper none will trust.

threads: The prince fabricates a false-flag atrocity bearing the duke’s seal and convenes a public tribunal where he declares a “War of Salvation,” forcing every noble house to either immediately pledge their treasury and blood-oath to personally captain a citizen battalion or be publicly executed as co-conspirators. This weaponizes the manufactured existential crusade to lock former plotters into a irreversible public performance of fealty, converting their hoarded wealth and pretender sympathies into the very engine that arms the populace under their command and burns their bridges back to the old order.

The prince will dispatch a personal envoy to the exiled duke bearing a sealed, deniable offer: a marriage binding the duke’s sole heir to the prince’s sister, a permanent ceremonial seat on a newly created Council of State, and full restitution of the ducal palace inside the city walls, all contingent on the duke’s immediate public return and the formal dissolution of his exile army. This move converts the external military threat into an internal court fixture, eliminates the need for mercenary gold by removing the casus belli, and paralyzes the plotting nobles by inserting a rival bloodline directly into the palace power structure as a visible, dependent co-ruler.

The prince secretly transports the entire treasury, grain reserves, and the royal seal to the Mount Aegis fortress, withdrawing with only his household guard and loyal administrators while leaving the capital’s gates unlocked and its arsenal empty. Once the exiled duke’s foreign auxiliaries inevitably sack the city to recoup their pay, the prince marches his untainted, rested force back to crush the now-despised duke and reclaim a city grateful for its liberation.

The prince immediately dismisses all mercenaries and auxiliary troops, forbids the arming of citizens, and orders the city gates left open and the palace completely unguarded, then publicly empties the empty treasury onto the cobblestones and walks alone to the returning duke’s encampment to kneel in deliberate, unarmed surrender. This act converts the reconquest into a stark tableau of a bankrupt ruler’s passive ruin, weaponizing the spectacle of his own humiliation to paralyze the conspiring nobles with shame, ignite popular pity, and compel foreign powers to treat the duke’s entry as a destabilizing usurpation demanding intervention.

judge.note: Strategies 1,3,4 rely on implausibly perfect downstream cascades.

theme: FORTRESSES vs the goodwill of the people as the prince's true defense
problem: A prince has just seized a rebellious city with a weather-beaten citadel; an enemy kingdom marches to retake it within two months, and he must choose whether to spend his scant treasury on restoring the fortress to overawe the hostile populace or on buying their love through tax remission and self-rule, knowing that the unfunded alternative will leave him dangerously exposed.
facets: The citadel is structurally weak but commands the harbor, and its granaries are full enough to sustain a garrison through a siege.
The populace blames the prince for the sack of their temples during the conquest and harbors a network of exiled nobles willing to collaborate with the outside enemy.
The approaching enemy force is disciplined but overstretched, and its commander would prefer to bribe the citizens into opening the gates rather than storm the walls.

angles.directive: Arrest and publicly execute the leading families and temple priests who could lead a revolt, then garrison the repaired citadel with your most trusted mercenaries to terrorize the remaining citizens into passive obedience, ignoring the blow to your field army’s mobility.

Strip the citadel of its artillery and supplies, withdraw your main force to the countryside to challenge the enemy in open battle, and leave a token magistrate with vague promises of future self-rule, effectively postponing the internal reckoning until the external threat is settled.

Dismantle the citadel’s gates and towers in a public ceremony, distribute its grain to the starving, and arm a citizen militia officered by your own loyal captains, making the defense of the city a shared enterprise that binds their interests to your rule.

Leak false intelligence that the approaching army intends to enslave the entire population and seize their lands, then withdraw your garrison to draw the enemy into the city; once they begin looting, return as the liberator beside the aroused citizenry, turning a prince-versus-people struggle into a common war of deliverance.

threads: Round up the twelve heads of the rebellious noble houses and the high priests of the sacked temples at dawn, behead them publicly in the harbor square, and seize their estates to fund immediate repairs to the citadel’s seaward bastion. Garrison the restored fortress with the Black Shield mercenaries pulled from the field army, granting them standing orders to execute any citizen who breaks curfew or communicates with the approaching enemy, while the main force entrenches on the landward approaches to block a direct assault.

We haul the citadel's bronze cannons and grain reserves to the high ground beyond the river, leaving a single clerk with a writ of prospective autonomy that binds nothing, while the garrison dissolves into our field army before the enemy can reach the walls. The enemy commander must then face a concentrated force with no fortification to hide behind, his overtures to the city rendered irrelevant because the gates are held by no one worth bribing.

We publicly raze the citadel’s gates and towers in a calculated ceremony, immediately redistribute its grain to the hungriest quarters, and conscript every able-bodied citizen into a militia commanded by our own loyal captains, physically issuing arms from the dismantled stonework reforged into blades. This transforms the defense into a collective survival pact: the populace, now fed and armed under our officers, can only protect their full bellies and new status by repelling the enemy, while the gutted citadel signals we have no redoubt to retreat into, making their betrayal synonymous with their own annihilation.

Withdraw the garrison into the countryside under cover of night while simultaneously planting forged enemy proclamations throughout the city detailing a systematic enslavement and land-seizure program, then wait for the enemy column to enter and begin looting before counterattacking alongside the now-armed citizenry, positioning the prince as the lesser evil who returned to expel a common predator.

judge.note: Strategy 4 relies on enemy stupidity; Strategy 3 assumes militia loyalty under duress.

theme: a prince raised by his own CRIME or betrayal, now consolidating an illegitimate seizure
problem: A prince who seized his city by orchestrating the massacre of its senate now finds his foreign allies withdrawing in disgust, leaving him isolated and vulnerable, yet he dare not loosen his grip lest the people's seething hatred explode into revolt.
facets: The foreign allies' withdrawal is not just a loss of troops but a public branding of the prince as a pariah, encouraging neighboring states to eye his territory as easy prey.
The massacre has created a power vacuum in the city's administration, filled by hastily promoted cronies whose incompetence is fueling economic collapse and civil resentment.
The common people, though terrified, possess a simmering moral outrage that could be ignited by a single brave voice, turning silent loathing into active insurrection.

angles.directive: Stage a false-flag assassination attempt on your own life, blame the remaining senatorial families and foreign agents, then launch a second, even more brutal purge to exterminate every last person of influence, ruling a terrified silence.

Abandon the palace in the dead of night with the entire treasury, flee into exile before the inevitable rebellion or foreign invasion materializes, and seek a life of obscure comfort rather than a violent end.

Publicly perform abject penance before the city, declare the establishment of a popular council drawn from the guilds and neighborhoods to share power, and redirect all blame onto a dead accomplice, framing yourself as a remorseful instrument of a now-ended necessity.

Announce that the massacre was a divine judgment against a corrupt and impious senate, produce fabricated prophecies and miracles foretelling your rule, and re-found the state as a holy theocracy with yourself as the humble executor of the god's will, turning your crime into a sacred origin myth.

threads: Stage a bombing at your own victory parade using mercenaries disguised as foreign-backed exiles, then execute the entire senatorial class and their families within forty-eight hours under the pretext of an international conspiracy, replacing them with a surveillance state run by military officers personally dependent on your survival.

The prince must convert the entire treasury into gemstones, gold bullion, and bearer bonds within 48 hours, then at 2 a.m. on the third night exit through the forgotten cistern passage beneath the east tower with two mute loyalists, leaving the vault door welded shut from inside to simulate a full treasury and buy a week of confusion. He will travel disguised as a dying silk merchant in a pre-arranged caravan to the independent port of Kôr, where no extradition treaty exists and his portable fortune purchases a permanent, anonymous residence under a dead man’s name.

The prince will stage a public ceremony of abject self-abasement in the main square, barefoot and robed in penitential sackcloth, reading aloud a forged deathbed confession from his deceased captain of the guard that casts the captain as the sole architect of the massacre and himself as a deceived instrument of necessity. Immediately afterward, he will issue an irrevocable charter establishing a Council of Guilds and Wards with binding veto power over all fiscal decrees and magisterial appointments, deliberately seating the most vocal guildmasters and neighborhood elders to transform their simmering outrage into invested governance.

By publicly decreeing the massacre as the “Cleansing of the Unworthy” on the Feast of Atonement—complete with forged temple oracles and a three-day city-wide ritual of purification—the prince dissolves the old constitution and installs a puppet High Synod that proclaims him God’s mortal Steward, instantly converting foreign withdrawal into proof of the world’s jealousy of a chosen city and making any rebel a blasphemer.

judge.note: Strategies mechanically sound but downstream logic implausibly optimistic or fantasy-dependent.

theme: the fate of DEPOSED HEIRS and the old ruling family after seizing power
problem: You have seized power and executed the old prince, but his 10-year-old heir and widow live in exile under the protection of a neighboring city-state, adored by your new subjects. You must decide their fate now, before they crystallize into a rebellion.
facets: The boy is the sole male heir and a living symbol of the legitimate bloodline, making him a perpetual focal point for restoration.
The widow retains diplomatic ties and could marry a foreign prince to forge an invasion alliance.
Any harsh treatment will outrage a populace that views them as innocent victims, risking immediate unrest.

angles.directive: Arrange a covert assassination of both, then publicly blame the rival city-state, staging a lavish funeral to channel public anger outward while eliminating the claimants utterly.
Negotiate a marriage between the widow and your own brother, then formally adopt the boy as your heir, merging bloodlines to transform a rival house into a subordinate branch of your dynasty.

Offer them a permanent, comfortable exile in a distant neutral territory, extracting a sworn renunciation of all claims while positioning your loyal agents as their custodians to delay the threat indefinitely.

Launch a propaganda campaign that recasts the old prince as a blood-soaked tyrant and his son as a cursed weakling, so the populace sees the boy not as a savior but as a return to chaos, reframing your coup as liberation.

threads:
I will have a trusted agent pose as a defector and offer the exiled pair a poisoned gift of reconciliation wine, then stage a botched kidnapping by mercenaries bearing forged contracts linking them to the rival city-state's council.  
The double funeral will feature an open-casket display of the boy’s innocent face, and I will personally swear vengeance at the pyre to channel the mob’s fury into an immediate siege of the rival city.

I will dispatch envoys to propose a binding pact: my brother weds the widow immediately, and I legally adopt the boy as my sole heir under a new dynastic name that fuses both bloodlines. This converts their claim into a subordinate branch of my house, making any future restoration attempt a treason against their own kin.

I relocate the widow and boy to the distant mercantile republic of Lygos, granting them a lifetime pension and a ceremonial palace in exchange for a publicly sworn, legally binding renunciation of all dynastic claims before Lygos’s own magistrates. I then install my own intelligence officers as their household stewards, tutors, and confessors, so that every letter, suitor, and whispered sentiment is filtered through my custody, turning their exile into a gilded cage whose locks I alone control.

You deploy paid rhetoricians and ballad-mongers across every market square to recast the old prince’s reign as a decade of calculated atrocity—complete with forged execution ledgers and dramatized tales of peasant children conscripted into his wars—while simultaneously spreading a whisper campaign that the boy’s sickly constitution and weeping fits in exile prove the bloodline itself is cursed and unfit to rule, so that any restoration fantasy curdles in the public mind into dread of a return to the old horrors.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 assume perfect operational security; 4 assumes belief without resistance.

theme: rival wives and concubines maneuvering over an HEIR's INHERITANCE as the master weakens or dies
problem: Pan Jinlian, childless and once-favored concubine, watches the master Ximen Qing weaken toward death: the sole male heir is the infant son of Li Ping'er, the preferred secondary wife, while the principal wife remains childless. If she does nothing, Li Ping'er will become the new matriarch and Pan Jinlian will be stripped of status and security. Yet the master is still lucid enough to sign a will or hear a petition. What does she do?
facets: Legal inheritance rules: the principal wife is official head, but a concubine's son inherits by blood; without a son, Pan Jinlian has no legal claim, only whatever the dying master might be persuaded to grant.

Emotional leverage: Ximen Qing's long-standing passion for Pan Jinlian gives her a window to sway him—he fears both death and being forgotten, and she can exploit his desire to leave a legacy that includes her.

Household alliances: servants, other concubines, and the principal wife are all watching; Li Ping'er is gentle but will fiercely defend her son, while the principal wife might be turned against Li Ping'er if it promises to preserve her own authority.

angles.directive: Arrange a fatal 'accident' for the infant heir, removing Li Ping'er's claim at the root, then present yourself as the loyal concubine who will help the principal wife adopt a distant nephew to continue the line—eliminating the obstacle directly.

Gather the master's portable treasures—gold, jewels, and secret pills—and flee the household before his final breath, setting up as a wealthy independent woman in a distant town under an assumed name, evading the coming purge entirely.

Swear a sudden, public oath of sisterly loyalty to Li Ping'er, offering to serve as the boy's nurse and household manager, becoming so indispensable to the new regime that she secures a permanent, comfortable place as the power behind the child's throne, trading ambition for security.

Convince the dying Ximen Qing to establish a charitable 'ancestral trust' in his name—with you as perpetual trustee—dedicating much of the estate to religious merit, thereby rewriting the inheritance into a spiritual and financial legacy that you control, bypassing the blood heir's automatic claim.

threads: Chunmei smothers the infant heir with a damp silk cloth during the nurse’s drugged midday sleep, positioning the body face-down in the crib to mimic accidental overlay asphyxiation. Before the household can stabilize, I approach the principal wife with a concrete plan to summon her nephew from the countryside and pressure Ximen Qing into signing an adoption decree, framing Li Ping’er’s grief as hysteria unfit for authority.

Pan Jinlian palms Ximen Qing’s private coffer key, his carved jade seal, and the pouch of ginseng-and-cinnabar pills he hoards against death, then slips through the garden gate while the household clusters around the physician’s evening visit. By dawn she is aboard a hired canal boat to Lin’an, carrying the portable gold and gems needed to purchase a teahouse under the registered widow-name “Meng Yulou,” severing every tie before the corpse cools.

Pan Jinlian will prostrate herself publicly before Li Ping’er, tearfully swearing a blood-sister oath and vowing to nurse and guard the infant heir as her own, while simultaneously offering to assume all household accounts and key storeroom keys, thus making her administrative grip so immediate and visible that any move to cast her out after Ximen Qing dies would unravel the estate’s daily operations.

She persuades Ximen Qing that his true immortality lies not in a squalling infant but in a perpetual estate dedicated to Buddhist merit-making, with herself installed as lifetime trustee empowered to manage all holdings in his name. The move legally restructures the inheritance as an inalienable charitable trust—transferring control of land and rents to her while cloaking the seizure as pious devotion, placing the estate beyond the reach of either the principal wife’s authority or Li Ping’er’s blood-claim heir.

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 4 assume unrealistic compliance; 2 oversimplifies detection risk.

theme: a newcomer concubine PROTECTING CONCEALED WEALTH from predatory rivals and the master himself
problem: You are Sixth Concubine Li, newly purchased into the household with a secret cache of silver ingots and two priceless jade hairpins from your previous protector; the master inspects new arrivals' belongings, the head wife deploys maids to spy for concealed assets, and your only safe hiding place—a locked camphor chest—has just been pried open while you were at evening meal, leaving no time to relocate the treasure before tomorrow's bedchamber inspection.
facets: Wealth is power but also a target: revealed assets will either be 'gifted' to the master to prove loyalty, confiscated by the head wife as household property, or stolen by rivals—yet without them you have no leverage, emergency funds, or ability to bribe servants for future protection.

The broken lock itself is evidence: someone knows something was hidden, and silence can be bought only if you identify the intruder and negotiate before they talk, but the household is a web of overlapping allegiances where questioning anyone reveals your panic.

Time pressure forces a decision tonight: the master's bedchamber summons doubles as informal property audit, and failing to produce a plausible story or visible tribute by morning risks demotion to menial status or expulsion into the street.

angles.directive: Stage a theft accusation tonight against the most vulnerable rival—plant a torn sleeve or summon the head wife with a witnessed 'discovery' of your broken chest, demanding immediate house search; the ensuing chaos discredits one enemy, makes your hidden wealth appear already stolen rather than hoarded, and positions you as victim owed restitution.

Empty the chest entirely before dawn, carry the treasure concealed on your person to the bedchamber, and during the master's inspection claim the broken lock was always empty—a dowry chest from your family containing nothing but hopes; deflect scrutiny by redirecting his attention to a minor but visible gift of your remaining silver as a filial offering to the head wife, buying time while you find a permanent hiding place outside your quarters.

Approach the household steward privately tonight with one jade hairpin as a bribe, confessing a 'premonition of theft' and requesting his protection; ask him to witness that the chest was already broken when you arrived, and to store your valuables in his own strongroom as evidence in a fictional investigation—shifting custody and culpability onto a neutral party with institutional authority over domestic theft.

Present the entire cache openly at the bedchamber inspection as a prepared tribute—announce that your former protector entrusted you with gifts specifically intended for the master of this house, and lay the silver and jade before him as proof that you bring fortune, not conceal it; recast secret wealth as a dowry-in-arrival, transforming you from suspicious hoarder into auspicious asset whose value increases when displayed.

threads: I will tear a strip from my own inner robe and snag the torn edge on the splintered lock of the camphor chest, then immediately summon the head wife with a witnessed scene of my “discovery,” claiming I saw a shadow flee toward Fifth Concubine Xu’s quarters. I demand a household-wide search of Xu’s rooms tonight, forcing the narrative that my concealed silver and jade hairpins were stolen property, not hoarded assets, while Xu’s predictable protests buy me the chaos to be named wronged victim owed restitution.

Li will transfer the silver and jade into a concealed waist sash worn beneath her robes, then smash the camphor chest’s interior latch-pin with a rock to simulate long-term disuse before positioning the empty chest conspicuously open at the foot of the bed; upon the master’s inspection, she will present a small cloth pouch of loose silver as a filial tribute to the head wife, narrating the chest as a sentimental family heirloom that never held valuables and inviting him to verify its emptiness himself.

I take the jade hairpin to the household steward tonight and, in a private audience, present it as a token of my distress over a “premonition of theft,” requesting he formally witness the broken lock and immediately transfer my silver ingots and remaining hairpin into his own strongroom under the guise of a confidential investigation he will lead. This shifts all physical custody and institutional liability onto him as the designated authority over domestic theft, converting my hidden wealth into evidence he must protect to preserve his own position.

I walk to the bedchamber inspection carrying the silver ingots and jade hairpins openly in a lacquer tray, and when the master’s eyes land on them I say, “My former protector sent me here with gifts meant for your hands alone—he said a man of your standing deserves tribute, not a beggar.” The head wife’s spies now report a concubine who volunteered wealth rather than a thief who hid it, and the master’s greed reframes my entire presence from liability into lucky omen before anyone can whisper “concealed assets.”

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 3 assume implausible compliance; Strategy 4 risks immediate confiscation.

theme: whether to be FEARED or LOVED by newly acquired subjects
