problem: A prince has conquered a wealthy city-state, but a charismatic heir to the deposed dynasty remains hidden among a resentful populace, and a rival power waits to exploit any rebellion. Should he secure his rule through fear, love, or a wholly different path?
facets: The heir is secretly sheltered by the influential merchant guilds, who resent foreign taxation.
The city’s commerce is the prince’s chief source of revenue; harsh repression could cripple trade and spark flight of capital.
A neighboring lord openly sympathizes with the old dynasty and would back an insurrection the moment the city shows weakness.

angles.directive: Hunt down the heir and all known sympathizers, then hold a public spectacle of execution to extinguish any hope of restoration through sheer terror.
Install a loyal local strongman as governor with free rein to enforce order, then withdraw your main force so that any cruelty falls on his head, not yours.
Offer the heir a powerless ceremonial title, marry into the leading merchant family, and grant the city limited self-rule to turn old elites into stakeholders in your regime.
Reframe the conquest as liberation: publicize the old dynasty’s secret abuses, position yourself as the people’s protector, and enact populist land and tax reforms that make you genuinely beloved.

threads: Deploy plainclothes agents to infiltrate the merchant guilds and trace the heir through the patterns of charitable donations and coded shipping manifests that sustain his concealment, then seize every identified collaborator in a single predawn sweep and stage a synchronized mass beheading in the central agora, with the heir’s head impaled atop the city gates for a full lunar cycle. Immediately afterward, announce a sealed registry of property confiscated from the executed sympathizers, auction it exclusively to foreign traders who have no ties to the old dynasty, and impose a collective blood-price tax on each guild that sheltered the heir, payable in public pledges of loyalty and the surrender of one-tenth of their apprentices as state hostages, thereby fracturing the merchant class into informers competing for survival.

Install a prominent guild factor—selected for ambition and local credibility—as governor with full fiscal and military authority, then publicly withdraw the main army to a border garrison, leaving him to crush dissent with his own hired city watch while you collect tribute through him as a disposable intermediary.

I will publicly "discover" the heir and install him as ceremonial Archon, simultaneously dissolving the rebel-in-waiting by making him a gilded hostage, while legally chartering a Merchant Council with tax-setting powers and myself as its president through marriage to the Al-Mansur trading house's eldest daughter.

The prince immediately commissions a public tribunal to unseal and widely disseminate the old dynasty’s secret tax ledgers proving decades of systematic extortion from the city’s laborers in direct collusion with the merchant guilds, then issues a decree that strips the guilds of their urban landholdings, redistributes that land to the laboring class, and replaces the existing tax regime with a steep levy on guild luxury exports while abolishing all taxes on basic commerce and peasant production.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes laborer loyalty; Strategy 2 underestimates intermediary betrayal risk.

theme: a discovered AFFAIR — explosive knowledge a concubine can wield, trade, or bury
problem: You are a secondary concubine who has discovered a jade pendant proving the master's favorite concubine is having an affair with the household steward; you must choose how to wield this secret to improve your precarious position without destroying yourself in the process.
facets: The master's favor is fickle, and any accusation made without irrefutable proof may backfire, branding you a jealous schemer.
The steward manages household silver and has bribe-able connections to local officials; crossing him could invite external retaliation.
The favorite concubine is pregnant with what the master believes is his child; any scandal could cost him so much face that he punishes all involved, including the bearer of bad news.

angles.directive: Present the pendant to the master in private, demanding the favorite's immediate expulsion and the steward's castration, thereby eliminating your rival in one decisive stroke regardless of collateral damage.

Secretly offer the favorite a pact: she will share her secrets, influence, and a portion of her privileges with you, and in return you will bury the evidence and help conceal her affair from the master, integrating your interests.

Bury the pendant in your chest and feign ignorance, deferring any action until after the child is born—if it is clearly the steward's, the truth will surface without your hand, preserving your safety.

Plant the pendant among the possessions of the chief wife's maid, then anonymously whisper of an affair, letting the chief wife's faction discover and expose the scandal, so the blow falls from outside yourself.

threads: Present the pendant to the master during his ledger review, framing the steward's access to household silver as the mechanism that facilitated the affair, then demand the steward's immediate castration and the concubine's expulsion before her pregnancy can introduce a bastard into the lineage.

Approach the favorite concubine during her private tea ceremony, place the jade pendant between you as a silent offer, and formalize a pact where you become her permanent alibi-crafter and household blind spot—feeding her movements, deflecting servant gossip, and burying the steward’s trail—in exchange for a fixed monthly tithe of her silver, real-time intelligence on the master’s moods, and a staged public endorsement that elevates your rank. Seal the alliance by exchanging mutual insurance: you retain the pendant but hand her a sealed letter detailing your own fabricated minor indiscretion, creating a symmetrical balance of ruin that makes defection unthinkable for either party.

I will seal the pendant in my locked camphor-wood chest and resume my duties exactly as before, ensuring no servant sees any change in my demeanor or routine. When the child is born with features that cannot belong to the master, the scandal will detonate on its own, sparing me from the role of accuser and leaving the steward and favorite concubine to destroy each other.

I will slip the jade pendant into the sewing basket of the chief wife’s head maid during the mid-morning courtyard emptiness, then feed an anonymous whisper to the chief wife’s eunuch that the maid has been receiving a lover’s token within the compound. When the chief wife’s faction seizes the planted proof and traces it to the steward, their own ambition will detonate the scandal against the pregnant favorite, leaving my hands entirely clean.

judge.note: Strategy 2's mutual-insurance letter assumes perfect symmetry; Strategy 4 risks maid's credibility collapse.

theme: a CENSOR or MAGISTRATE threatening to reopen the household's crimes unless paid off
problem: Wu Yueniang, the widowed first wife, learns that the new county censor has assembled a dossier of her late husband’s capital bribes and demands an impossible sum within ten days—or he will reopen the case, seize the household, and expose them all to ridicule and ruin.
facets: The censor is newly posted, lacks local allies, and his own superiors might be bribed to overrule him; his position is not yet secure.

The household’s liquid silver is depleted after the funeral and inheritance quarrels; raising the payment means forcing rival concubines to surrender hidden savings or selling the profitable medicine shop, triggering internal war.
Any public trial would destroy the marriage prospects of the young mistress, invite predatory lawsuits from old rivals, and strip away the last shreds of social credit the estate still holds.

angles.directive: Hire a loyal tough or a silent knife to eliminate the censor before his deadline; a dead blackmailer cannot reopen a case, and a sudden vacancy buys confusion.

Stall with a token payment and a forged confession pinning the bribes on a long-dead servant; secretly ship key heirs, deeds, and portable wealth to distant relatives before the censor’s patience breaks.

Invite the censor into a lasting partnership—grant him a permanent share of the medicine shop’s profits and a seat at the household’s secret accounts, turning a one-time threat into a committed protector.

Let the case proceed; preemptively offer up a scapegoat concubine and her son as the true architects of the past corruption, endure the public trial, and rebuild a purified, legally defensible household from the ashes of the sacrifice.

threads: We deploy Old Hu’s nephew—a debt-collector with a closed skull fracture and no living dependents—to waylay the censor on the Jinan post road disguised as a cart wreck, making the body and dossier disappear into the Yellow River before the county yamen even registers him missing.

We will dispatch a courier with three hundred taels and a confession in the dead steward Zhou Fuhai’s handwriting, backdated and witnessed by a bribed former clerk, swearing he alone forwarded the bribes without the master’s knowledge; meanwhile, under cover of the mourning period, we will quietly transfer the young mistress, the deed to the Yong’an Pharmacy, and all remaining gold to my cousin in Dezhou, leaving only the household shell to face the censor’s inevitable escalation.

She offers the censor a notarized perpetual dividend contract granting him fifteen percent of the medicine shop’s net profits and a sealed copy of the household’s true ledger, transforming him from external threat into silent equity partner whose own career interests now depend on the estate’s uninterrupted survival.

The widow will publicly surrender the concubine Meng Yulou and her son to the censor as the sole architects of the bribery scheme, backed by falsified account books and servant confessions that isolate every transaction in their hands alone. She will watch the trial strip them of name and freedom, then petition the magistrate to seal the case with her own household exonerated as victim rather than accomplice, rebuilding on the legal clarity of their condemnation.

judge.note: Strategies 1,2 assume perfect execution; 3,4 underestimate censor's countermoves.

theme: rival wives and concubines maneuvering over an HEIR's INHERITANCE as the master weakens or dies
problem: As the master’s health fails without a named heir, you—the senior wife with no son—must secure your future against a favored concubine who has just birthed the only male child; she controls the nursery but you control the household accounts.
facets: The dying master still lucid enough to sign documents but emotionally captive to the concubine’s pillow talk.
The concubine’s infant son is the de facto heir unless you can legally adopt a nephew or fabricate a claim.
Household servants split between loyalty to your authority and fear of the concubine’s rising favor.

angles.directive: Publicly accuse the concubine of adultery, weaponizing servant testimony and forged evidence to invalidate her son’s paternity entirely.

Swallow pride and propose to the master a 'unified household' decree: you formally adopt the concubine’s son as your own, gaining legal motherhood in exchange for granting the concubine a lavish, permanent allowance.

Bypass biology entirely by convincing the master that preserving the family name requires a 'virtue succession'—you take a young, distant scholar-relative as an adult godson, arguing that the business empire needs a literate, grown heir, not an infant.

Summon your powerful but corrupt natal brother, offering him a cut of the inheritance to arrive with armed retainers and 'witness' a deathbed will that names you sole executor and trustee of all assets until the infant comes of age—under your guardianship.

threads: I convene a formal household tribunal with the dying master present, where I produce a bought wet-nurse’s sworn testimony and a forged letter from a fictitious lover planted in the concubine’s quarters, then demand immediate legal declaration of bastardy. The move weaponizes my control of the accounts to fund the forgery and bribe the midwife into providing a falsified birth record that overwrites the infant’s patrilineal claim with a documented timeline of adultery.

I will present the dying master with a formal decree that legally adopts the concubine’s infant son as my own, while simultaneously granting her a permanent, lavish allowance drawn from the household accounts I control. This move converts her biological claim into my legal shield, and her ambition into a bought-off dependent whose financial security hinges entirely on my continued authority over the treasury.

I will place before the master a pre-drafted "Virtue Succession Edict" adopting my late mother’s grand-nephew, the classicist Yuan (22), as our adult godson and immediate heir, arguing that only a literate adult can prevent the trading empire’s collapse. Simultaneously, I freeze the concubine’s nursery budget through a sudden ledger audit, cutting her financial leverage so that the master’s signature legally redefines the bloodline by competence alone.

I will dispatch a sealed message to my brother, offering him a 15% lifetime annuity from the estate in exchange for arriving with six armed retainers to "witness" the master's thumbprint on a will that appoints me sole executrix, trustee of all assets, and legal guardian of the infant until his majority. The moment the retainers control the doors, I will register the will with the magistrate, pay the required bribes from the household accounts, and transfer the concubine to a locked dower house under permanent guard.

judge.note: All strategies criminally overreach; none model realistic consequences or detection risk.

theme: a blackmailer holding a DOCUMENT of past bribes; act before it is used
problem: A purloined receipt proving you bribed the magistrate to rule against a rival merchant now sits in the hands of a household servant who threatens to deliver it to the censor unless you grant her freedom, a dowry, and a deed to a shop—but conceding invites perpetual extortion.
facets: The document is forensic and dated—it cannot be dismissed as forgery, only explained or suppressed.
The blackmailer is inside the household, so her knowledge of other secrets makes partial compliance dangerous.
The censor is currently conducting a prefectural audit, so the window for action is narrow and the magistrate will distance himself to survive.

angles.directive: Frame the servant for theft of silver from the household treasury, have her seized by the yamen runners before she can reach the censor, and recover the document during the search of her belongings.

Feign agreement and use the delay to quietly transfer your liquid wealth to a trusted relative in Yangzhou, then publicly accuse the servant of fabricating the document out of jealous resentment after you refused her a marriage request—muddy the timeline so the bribe appears retaliatory slander.

Adopt her as a goddaughter, arrange a marriage to a minor clerk in your own mercantile network, and install her in the shop as manager with a profit share—transforming her from threat into a permanently indebted insider whose own name appears on new, legitimate contracts.

Preemptively approach the magistrate, reveal that a receipt has surfaced and propose jointly presenting it as an extorted “gift” the rival merchant forced you to make under duress—then file a counter-suit against the blackmailer as an agent of the rival, converting the bribe into evidence of a conspiracy against you.

threads: Plant a marked silver ingot from the household treasury among the servant's belongings tonight, then dispatch an anonymous tip to the yamen runners at dawn that a thief inside the household is preparing to flee, ensuring they intercept her at the gate and during the search "discover" both the planted silver and the purloined receipt, which you will quietly pocket as the outraged master.

I will play for the hour she demands, then have my steward shift all liquid holdings into a Yangzhou cousin’s name under cover of a routine river-shipping manifest before sunrise.  
At first light I present myself to the censor’s clerk and file a formal complaint that the receipt is a vindictive forgery—planted only after I denied the servant’s immodest petition for a shop and a dowry to wed above her station—so that the dated bribe becomes indistinguishable from a spurned woman’s retaliatory slander timed to an audit she could not otherwise exploit.

Adopt her by sworn affidavit before the household gods on the condition that she marry our junior shipping clerk the same hour, then install her as shop manager with a percentage share contractually tied to quarterly renewals—her name now appears on the import licenses, making her legally complicit in the very trade structure she threatens to expose.

I will summon the magistrate to a private conference tonight, lay the receipt before him as proof that my rival extorted this “gift” under threat of fabricating a capital charge, and propose we jointly certify a sworn statement to that effect before the censor’s audit closes. Tomorrow morning I will file a formal denunciation against the servant as the rival’s planted agent, attaching the receipt as exhibit A in a conspiracy complaint that transforms the bribe into the instrument of a foiled plot against me.

judge.note: Strategies 2, 3, 4 assume magistrate/censor/rival cooperation implausibly.

theme: punishing a wealthy concubine when doing so risks her SUICIDE, public SCANDAL, and loss of her fortune
problem: Ximen Qing discovers that his concubine Li Ping'er has secretly diverted a large sum of silver to her fallen former household, a betrayal that cries out for punishment—but any harsh sanction could push the fragile and wealthy Li Ping'er to suicide, triggering a murder inquest and the likely loss of her entire fortune.
facets: Emotional fragility: Li Ping'er has previously attempted suicide after domestic rows, making severe discipline a genuine life-or-death gamble.
Financial entanglement: Her substantial dowry and properties are already woven into the Ximen estate; her death could prompt her natal family to reclaim assets via a ruinous lawsuit.
Reputational vulnerability: A concubine’s suicide would ignite public scandal, invite venal officials to extort the household, and risk a charge of mistreatment leading to murder.

angles.directive: Threaten to expose her theft to her natal clan or have her publicly disfigured, orchestrating a psychological assault that breaks her will and makes her beg for mercy—using her fear of shame to forestall suicide while extracting total submission.

Quietly remove Li Ping'er to a cloistered family temple in the countryside for 'recuperation,' separating her from the household and her liquid wealth indefinitely, delaying any irrevocable punishment until the crisis fades and her access to means of self-harm is limited.

Shower her with sudden affection, reframe the diverted silver as a misunderstandable gift, and persuade her to openly deed a larger portion of her fortune to the household; bind her through gratitude and shared financial interest, turning a transgression into deeper partnership.

Publicly blame the missing silver on a recently dismissed servant, exonerating Li Ping'er while privately using the fabricated theft to tighten surveillance over her; re-spin her actions as a victim’s naive blunder, sidestepping scandal and punishment while retaining moral authority and her wealth.

threads: Ximen Qing corners Li Ping'er alone with the pilfered silver's ledger, then presents a stark binary: sign a deed surrendering all remaining dowry assets and kneel in abject apology before the household servants, or he will dispatch a sealed indictment to her natal elders and have a tattooist publicly ink "thief" onto her cheekbone—the threat of indelible clan shame paralyzes any suicidal impulse, locking her into a life of total submission under his financial control.

Ximen Qing will transfer Li Ping’er to the secluded Temple of Merciful Peace outside Qinghe under the guise of a “nervous collapse requiring absolute rest,” installing two deaf-mute attendants and a bribed abbess who will confine her to a walled meditation cell stripped of all cords, sharp objects, and writing materials. Simultaneously, her dowry deeds and silver are physically relocated to Ximen’s private iron-bound treasury, and her natal family is informed that she has voluntarily entered a religious retreat for an indefinite period, forestalling any inquiry while her liquid wealth is gradually absorbed into estate holdings.

Ximen Qing will stage an immediate public ceremony praising Li Ping’er for secretly aiding her former household as if he had pre-authorized it, dressing her in a newly gifted pearl-studded robe and announcing a “reward” of extra monthly silver, thus recoding the betrayal into a sanctioned virtue. He will then, in the intimacy of that gratitude, have her sign a witnessed deed transferring a majority share of her separate estates into the household’s name under a jointly administered trust, locking her fortune to his survival and making any future disloyalty financially self-destructive.

Ximen Qing stages the discovery of the missing silver in the quarters of a recently expelled servant, publicly denouncing the man and clearing Li Ping’er’s name, while privately installing a network of trusted female informants within her chambers under the guise of protecting her from further “naïve misjudgments” that could endanger her life and the estate.

judge.note: Strategies 2&3 assume implausible compliance; servant-frame (4) risks exposure.

theme: a trusted STEWARD's divided loyalty: he knows the master's bribery secrets and is caught skimming
problem: Caught skimming from your corrupt master, you must choose how to protect yourself before he silences you—knowing you hold evidence of his bribes to the magistrate.
facets: The master’s bribery could destroy his entire household if exposed, making him ruthlessly protective of secrecy.
Your own family depends on your position, and any scandal would ruin them.
Other servants and the magistrate’s clerks are entangled; your move might collapse a fragile web of shared guilt.

angles.directive: Threaten to reveal the bribes unless the master grants a formal pardon, a severance payment, and a written guarantee of safety; send a sealed letter to a trusted outsider to be opened if you are harmed.
Gather all the cash you can and flee at dawn, leaving behind only a note that hints you have evidence but will stay silent as long as you are not pursued.
Admit your theft but propose to become the master’s secret partner: you will handle all bribe accounting and create a joint slush fund, making yourself indispensable and sharing in the illicit gains.

Reframe the skimming: claim you were secretly setting aside reserves to shield the master from future blackmail or official audits, presenting the hidden money as a prudential 'safety chest' you built out of loyalty.

threads: I hand the master a written ultimatum detailing his magistrate bribes and my non-negotiable terms—a witnessed pardon, a severance purse counted in silver, and a signed guarantee of safety for my family—while informing him that a duplicate dossier sits sealed with a dead-man instruction held by a solicitor unconnected to the household.

I empty the strongbox of unregistered coin and take the north road before the cock crows, pinning a note to the kitchen table that states: “The magistrate’s receipts are with a friend who expects my safe arrival; any pursuit triggers their release.”

I confess my theft directly to the master, then propose converting his current haphazard bribe ledger into a dual-key ciphered escrow account that I alone manage, where a fixed percentage of every bribe is automatically diverted into a joint, time-locked fund that only we both can access. This binds his survival to my own by making the slush fund’s existence contingent on my continued administration, while my family’s silence is purchased by a real stake in the scheme rather than mere employment.

I will immediately present the master with the hidden funds alongside a doctored ledger reclassifying every skimmed coin as a dedicated “audit shield” reserve, and I will hand him the original bribe records sealed in a package addressed to the magistrate’s clerk, explaining that I built this safety chest to be triggered only if external pressure threatened the household—thereby recasting my theft as the very mechanism that can now absorb his exposure. By insisting the entire scheme was a unilateral loyalty play designed to quarantine his secrets from blackmailers, I force him to choose between accepting me as the sole custodian of his insulation or dismantling the very buffer that now stands between him and ruin.

judge.note: Strategies 3&4 assume master accepts reframing; unrealistic psychological leaps.

theme: after the master's death, a SHAKEDOWN forcing a choice between REPUTATION and LIQUIDITY
problem: With Ximen Qing dead, chief wife Wu Yueniang faces a shakedown from a former steward who threatens to expose the master's purchase of fatal aphrodisiacs unless paid one hundred taels; paying would drain the household's remaining liquid silver for funeral debts and servant wages, risking immediate collapse, while refusal guarantees public scandal that could trigger official confiscation and permanent ruin.
facets: Reputation is the household’s only shield against predatory officials; without it, even a well-funded estate will be stripped bare.
Liquidity is already stretched by funeral costs and the need to keep servants from fleeing; paying invites copycat extortion and leaves no buffer for any crisis.
The blackmailer was once an intimate of the household, meaning a response that does not account for inside knowledge risks more secrets spilling out.

angles.directive: Silence the extortionist permanently by hiring muscle to vanish him before he can spread the tale, betting that a dead man tells no secrets and that swift violence deters others.

Deflect the shakedown with feigned capitulation: promise payment after a fictitious land sale, stringing the blackmailer along until the household can shift its liquid assets into hidden, non-seizable forms or until the threat fades.

Offer the former steward a permanent, lucrative sinecure inside the household—such as manager of the outer stores and a match to a loyal maid—turning his knowledge into a shared stake in the family’s survival.

Pre-empt the blackmailer by staging a public confession that recasts Ximen Qing as a victim of corrupt doctors, donating the last silver to a temple to buy moral authority; drain the secret’s power by making the household’s disgrace a story of pious self-sacrifice.

threads: Dispatch Lai Wang and two hired blade-men from the docks to intercept the former steward on his nightly walk home, using the pretense of a discreet partial payment to lure him into the blind alley behind the wine shop, then cut his throat and dump the body in the canal with a sack of stones. Plant a forged letter in his lodgings confessing an intent to abscond with a maid and stolen valuables, ensuring the official story reads as a fugitive’s disappearance and signaling to any future extortionist that inside knowledge buys a quick, anonymous grave.

Wu Yueniang will feed the blackmailer a full capitulation, promising one hundred taels only after she monetizes a fabricated land parcel—the “West Slope mulberry plot”—by instructing Steward Dai’an to stage a sham negotiation with a hired broker and produce a backdated deed, thereby buying a thirty-day window. During that interval, she will systematically convert all remaining silver into unseizable, portable forms—high-carat gold leaf sewn into mourning garments, premium ginseng roots transferred to Abbess Wang at the Temple of Eternal Grace—so that when the fictitious sale collapses, the household’s liquid core has vanished into informal repositories that predatory officials cannot trace or confiscate.

Wu Yueniang immediately offers the former steward the permanent, salaried position of Outer Storehouse Steward and a formal betrothal to her trusted personal maid Xiaoyu, converting his lethal inside knowledge into a structural dependency on the household’s continued standing.  
The arrangement ties his livelihood, social face, and future lineage directly to the family’s reputation, making exposure of the aphrodisiac purchase an act of self-destruction that would strip him of status, income, and marriage in a single stroke.

Wu Yueniang summons the entire household and the local ward head to the main hall to announce that Ximen Qing was poisoned by the prescriptions of Doctor Hu and Doctor Jiang, then publicly donates the last one hundred taels of silver to the Temple of Eternal Blessing in his name, transforming the secret purchase into a tale of medical victimhood and pious recompense that strips the steward’s leverage by making his accusation indistinguishable from the household’s own broadcasted narrative.

judge.note: Strategies 1,2 assume perfect operational security; 3 assumes steward accepts; 4 assumes narrative sticks.

theme: protecting a POSTHUMOUS male HEIR from rivals who would kill to preserve their own futures
problem: As the wet nurse of the posthumous infant son of Ximen Qing, you overhear the concubine Pan Jinlian order a servant to poison the child at dawn, eliminating the only obstacle to her own adopted son inheriting the household. You have until morning to decide how to save the heir from a rival who will kill without hesitation—and a single misstep means death for you both.
facets: The household is a web of bought loyalties; any overt move will be reported to Pan Jinlian within minutes.
The infant's legal inheritance depends on being recognized as Ximen Qing's legitimate son, yet a sudden 'illness' death would be easily covered up by a bribe to the coroner.
The wet nurse's own family lives in the servant quarters under the household's power, making her personally vulnerable to reprisal.

angles.directive: Publicly denounce Pan Jinlian before the inner court and the family elders, demanding an inspection of the kitchen and a test of the porridge, forcing the accusation into the open and banking that her guilt will be impossible to hide under collective scrutiny.

Slip away with the infant before dawn while the household sleeps, using back alleys and a false name to flee to a distant market town where you can live as a destitute refugee, permanently abandoning any claim to the Ximen estate but keeping the child alive.

Secretly approach the chief wife Wu Yueniang and persuade her to claim the posthumous son as her own, offering your sworn testimony that the child is legitimate and proposing a shared guardianship that would strengthen her position and give her control over the inheritance—in exchange for protecting him from Pan Jinlian.

Stage the baby's death by swapping him with the corpse of a commoner infant who died that night, spreading the news that the heir succumbed to a sudden fever; then raise the real child in obscurity among the servant quarters as an orphaned kitchen boy, permanently removing him from the line of succession and dissolving the threat.

threads: I will gather the infant and the senior-most surviving wife, Wu Yueniang, to the ancestral hall at the hour of the morning porridge delivery, and there, before the assembled family elders, I will publicly accuse Pan Jinlian of poison, demanding an immediate inspection of the kitchen and a live test of the child's prepared food on a dog or low servant, forcing the crime into the light where no single bribe can silence every witness at once.

Feign a routine pre-dawn feeding, then exit through the scullery gate with the infant swaddled against your body under a market cloak, heading east along the canal path to the ferryman’s hut where a single silver hairpin will buy passage across the river before the household stirs.

I will slip into Wu Yueniang’s chambers before dawn and present her with a cold bargain: publicly claim the posthumous heir as her own son, and in return I will give sworn testimony of his legitimacy and accept a shared guardianship that cements her legal control over the household inheritance.  
This transforms her from a sidelined chief wife into the child’s sole protector, weaponizing her status to strip Pan Jinlian of any foothold while binding the infant’s survival to Yueniang’s own political triumph.

I will swap the living heir with the still-warm corpse of the kitchen maid’s infant that died last evening, paying the maid’s silence with my own silver hairpin and a pledge to raise the boy as her nephew in the scullery. Once the substitute body is discovered at dawn with fever-flushed cheeks painted on, I will declare the heir dead and nurse the true child in plain sight as a nameless orphan, severing his claim so completely that Pan Jinlian never looks for a ghost.

judge.note: Strategy 4 corpse-swap risks discovery; Strategy 2 assumes ferryman reliability.

theme: a neglected concubine selling household INTELLIGENCE to rival houses for favor and silver
problem: Zhang, the rival she secretly serves, now demands she steal a sealed magistrate’s letter hidden in Ximen’s study—evidence of systemic bribery that would doom the entire household. If she refuses, Zhang will reveal her treason to Ximen; if she obeys, the household collapses around her, leaving her unprotected and likely executed as an accomplice.
facets: The letter is a political time bomb: exposing it would not only ruin Ximen but could sweep the whole compound into a purge, making her own survival precarious.
The household’s watchful hierarchy: the First Lady’s maids and the butler’s spies mean every move toward the study risks immediate discovery and brutal punishment.
Zhang’s threat is credible but his protection is hollow: once he has the letter, he no longer needs her and may dispose of her to silence a loose end.

angles.directive: Poison Zhang at their next secret meeting, using a slow-acting toxin disguised as a gift of medicinal tea, then retrieve or destroy any written proof of her betrayal before he dies.
Flee the household tonight with hidden silver and a few portable valuables, heading for a distant prefecture where neither Ximen’s vengeance nor Zhang’s reach can easily find her.

Confess a carefully edited version to the household’s senior wife, framing herself as a double agent who can feed Zhang false information to trap and ruin him, thereby converting her treason into a service.

Steal the letter but deliver it directly to the magistrate’s yamen, presenting herself as a righteous informant who discovered a corruption ring, seeking immunity and a reward that would give her independence from both men.

threads: She poisons Zhang with aconite-infused osmanthus tea during their next meeting, timing the dose so symptoms manifest hours later as apparent heart failure, then immediately searches his person for any written orders or letters connecting her to him.

I take the twenty taels of silver concealed beneath the floorboard and my jade hairpin, wrap them in a gray cloth, then exit through the servants’ side gate during the hour of the rat when the watch changes, heading for the river dock to buy passage south. By sunrise I will be beyond Ximen’s jurisdiction, a nameless traveler in a prefecture where neither his spies nor Zhang’s informants can trace a woman alone.

She goes directly to the First Lady with a fabricated but plausible account: Zhang attempted to recruit her, and she feigned compliance to gather intelligence, now proposing to deliver a forged letter that will trap Zhang when he acts on it, exposing his plot to the magistrate and purging him without endangering the household. This move converts her prior treason into a proactive counter-operation, making her the essential instrument of Zhang's destruction and securing the First Lady’s protection as the sole handler of a double agent whose survival is now aligned with the household’s survival.

She will acquire the letter during the mid-watch servant rotation by exploiting the blind corridor behind the ancestral hall, then bypass Zhang entirely to present the sealed document at the magistrate’s yamen as a voluntary informant under a pre-emptive plea of duress, trading the physical evidence for a state-witness protection decree and a stipend that constitutes independent financial severance from both Ximen’s household and Zhang’s network.

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 4 assume unrealistic forensic/bureaucratic outcomes.

theme: a trusted STEWARD's divided loyalty: he knows the master's bribery secrets and is caught skimming
problem: Steward Lai Wang has managed Master Ximen’s ledgers for years, but now the master’s chief rival, Master Hua, has discovered Lai Wang’s secret skimming and offers silence in exchange for the bribery records—while Ximen grows suspicious of a leak. Lai Wang must choose whom to serve, how to survive, and whether the ledgers become a shield, a weapon, or a pyre.
facets: The ledgers are both proof of Lai Wang’s embezzlement and the only evidence of Ximen’s bribery of the magistrate; whoever holds them holds ruin over the other.
Master Hua’s offer is a one-time amnesty, but entering his service means permanent vulnerability as a purchased traitor with no independent standing.
Ximen’s household is a web of rival wives and ambitious under-stewards who would eagerly expose Lai Wang to curry favor, so delay or inaction is itself a choice with timed consequences.

angles.directive: Preempt by destroying both threats at once: forge a supplementary ledger implicating Master Hua in a graver crime and anonymously deliver it to the magistrate Ximen already owns, ensuring Hua is arrested before he can reveal anything, while the original ledgers stay hidden as your insurance.

Flee the city tonight with only the ledgers and enough silver to travel three provinces away, leaving both masters to suspect each other of your disappearance and the missing records, while you start under a new name far from their reach.

Confess the skimming to Ximen yourself but frame it as a loyal test of household security, then propose a false-flag operation: feed Hua doctored ledgers through you as a double agent, binding yourself indispensably to Ximen as the only man who can run the deception.

Send copies of the bribery records to the Censorate with an anonymous letter exposing the entire corrupt network of Ximen, Hua, and the magistrate, then use the ensuing investigation to position yourself as a cooperative witness under official protection—exchanging the role of expendable steward for that of state’s evidence.

threads: I forge a supplementary ledger attributing the poisoning of Magistrate Gao’s tribute horses to Master Hua’s stable accounts, then have it delivered by a hired vagrant who will be found floating in the canal before he can be questioned, while the original ledgers are wax-sealed and interred with the ancestral tablets in Ximen’s own shrine hall where no steward would dare excavate.

Lai Wang takes the ledgers and exactly forty taels of coined silver tonight, exits through the kitchen door during the hour of the watchdog’s lantern circuit, and marches straight to the south wharf to board the pre-arranged mail boat that departs before dawn. The empty strongbox and his unexplained absence will force Ximen to suspect Hua of abduction and theft while Hua assumes Ximen has hidden both man and books in retaliation, and Lai Wang will adopt the registered name of a minor oil merchant in Yuhang, burying the ledgers as a dormant insurance deep enough to outlast both masters’ reach.

I confess the skimming to Ximen as a deliberate stress-test of his inner compound’s leak-detection, then pivot to a counter-deception: I feed Master Hua a meticulously forged bribery ledger that I alone calibrate to trap Hua in an attempted blackmail, making my continued freedom and double-agent role structurally indispensable to Ximen.

I duplicate the bribery ledgers and dispatch them with an unsigned memorial to the Provincial Censor, detailing the entire paymaster chain—Ximen, Hua, and the magistrate—as a single criminal enterprise. When the Censorate’s investigators arrive, I present myself not as a fleeing steward but as a voluntary informant, trading my original ledgers and testimony for a recorded grant of immunity and placement under official protection, thereby converting my liability into state’s evidence and dissolving both masters’ leverage in one stroke.

judge.note: Strategy 4 assumes Censorate immunity is reliably granted; risks underestimated.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: Ximen Qing catches his indispensable cook skimming silver from banquet funds, protected by the volatile concubine Pan Jinlian. Exposing the theft risks a domestic war, loss of a key asset who knows the household’s bribery secrets, and a complete fracture of his fragile social order.
facets: The cook’s banquets are critical for bribing officials; replacing him would cripple Ximen Qing’s political dealings.
Pan Jinlian has publicly staked her honor on the cook’s honesty; any move against him will be read as a direct assault on her standing.
The cook has witnessed every illicit payment, and a dismissed servant might sell those secrets to Ximen Qing’s rivals.

angles.directive: Publicly flog and expel the cook, announcing the theft to the entire household—accept the ensuing fury from Pan Jinlian, the void in the kitchen, and the danger of exposed secrets, relying on raw power to reassert dominance.
Bury the evidence, feign ignorance, and quietly instruct the steward to let the skimming continue for now—delay any reckoning until the cook’s protector loses influence or a safer moment arises.

Summon the cook in secret, reveal you know, but instead of punishment, negotiate a pact: he keeps a small cut in exchange for absolute loyalty, flawless banquets, and acting as your spy among the servants.

Anonymously leak proof of the skimming to the rival concubine Li Ping’er, letting her expose the cook and absorb Pan Jinlian’s fury, while you position yourself as the impartial peacemaker who then decides the cook’s fate.

threads: Ximen Qing will have the cook seized in the main courtyard at dawn, publicly present the altered ledgers proving twelve taels missing from the last two banquets, and administer twenty lashes before permanent expulsion on pain of death if he ever discloses household business, simultaneously decreeing that Pan Jinlian—as his former guarantor—must personally staff and supervise the kitchen’s transition, thereby converting her defense of the cook into a public test of loyalty she can only pass by delivering a flawless replacement.

Ximen Qing will have the household steward quietly reclassify the missing silver as “miscellaneous kitchen wastage” in the official ledgers and personally instruct the secondary cooks never to question the head cook’s requisitions, ensuring the skimming continues without the cook ever realizing he was detected. This converts the theft into a controlled bleed that preserves both the banquet machinery and Pan Jinlian’s public honor, buying time until her influence decays or a safer moment to excise the cook arises.

Ximen Qing will secretly summon the cook, present the proof of skimming, and dictate a cold bargain: a fixed three percent commission on every banquet fund, contingent on flawless execution and a weekly written report detailing servant gossip and Pan Jinlian’s movements. The cook signs a private memorandum and begins depositing duplicate receipts in a locked coffer only Ximen Qing accesses, turning the embezzler into a permanently bound informant.

Slip a falsified kitchen receipt that names Li Ping’er as the embezzler’s silent partner into her quarters via a disposable go-between, triggering her to publicly expose the cook to gut Pan Jinlian’s standing. Then, decry the chaos as the master betrayed by factional squabbling, privately grant the cook clemency in exchange for his continued culinary bribes and absolute silence about all past payments, and let the two women exhaust each other while I consolidate the cook’s secrets under a debt of survival.

judge.note: Strategies 3-4 assume unrealistic compliance; downstream reactions underspecified.

theme: protecting a POSTHUMOUS male HEIR from rivals who would kill to preserve their own futures
problem: You are the widowed main wife of a corrupt merchant household, and the sole posthumous male heir—a sickly infant—is the only barrier to your rivals seizing the family fortune. Tonight, the concubine who poisoned the last son plans to have the midwife smother the child in its crib; the servants are bribed, the gates watched, and you lack proof that would stand in court. You must act to save the infant and secure the succession.
facets: The baby’s frailty and the narrow window before the midwife strikes, demanding immediate action.
A household riddled with spies and bought loyalties, making an open accusation suicidal without hard evidence.
The legal and symbolic weight of the male heir, whose death would invite clan relatives to plunder the estate and erase your own standing.

angles.directive: Set a trap for the midwife, catch her in the act, and force a confession implicating the rival concubine—then deliver both to the magistrate for public execution, permanently removing the threat.

Smuggle the infant out of the compound tonight with a single loyal wet nurse to a distant, trusted cousin, then spread the story that the child died of fever, letting the rival believe she has won while the heir grows up safe in hiding.

Offer the murderous concubine a formal adoption of the child as her own, with a guaranteed generous allowance and honored place as the mother of the heir, so that her financial future becomes tied to his survival.

Publicly declare the infant the reincarnation of his father and pledge him to the ancestors in a grand ceremony attended by all clan elders and local gentry, making any harm to the child an unforgivable sacrilege that would invite immediate clan vengeance.

threads: I will conceal myself inside the locked nursery before the midwife’s scheduled visit, having substituted the infant with a weighted swaddle that mimics breathing, so that when she presses the cloth to smother it I seize her wrist mid-act and force a written confession naming the concubine under threat of immediate strangulation. Then I will bind both women and haul them to the magistrate’s tribunal at dawn, presenting the intercepted cloth and the signed confession as irrefutable proof for their public execution.

I will have the wet nurse conceal the infant in the empty grain cart that the kitchen boy, the one she has been feeding information to in exchange for his mother’s medicine, wheels out for the night market, timing the departure to the quarter-hour when the midwife is occupied with her own sleeping draught in the concubine’s quarters. At dawn, I will place a bundled piglet in the crib with a hot brick to mimic fever heat, then summon the household to witness the “corpse” before the midwife can inspect it, spreading the tale that the child succumbed to a swift fever while I send a sealed letter ahead to Cousin Shu in the northern prefecture to expect the heir and raise him under a false name.

I will draft a formal adoption covenant registering the concubine as the child’s legal mother with a lifetime annuity drawn directly from the estate’s rents, and I will seal it with the household stamp before a neutral scribe tonight, placing it in her hands the hour before the midwife is due to move. That document transforms her from a disposable accomplice into a financial hostage of the infant’s pulse, making every breath he draws the condition of her newfound status and income.

By invoking the ancestors at dawn before the assembled clan elders and gentry, I will place the infant on the ancestral tablet and declare him the father’s living reincarnation, binding his survival to the family’s sacred honor so that any harm triggers automatic clan retaliation without my needing to name the plotters.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 assume compliance; 2&4 ignore verification gaps.

theme: a newcomer concubine PROTECTING CONCEALED WEALTH from predatory rivals and the master himself
problem: As Sixth Sister Plum enters the household, she must secure the gold and jewels hidden inside her dowry’s false-bottomed wedding chest before the First Wife's maids discover it in their routine 'inventory,' but the chest is too heavy to move silently and the master believes her dowry was entirely paid by his own purse.
facets: The master's ignorance of her wealth is her only leverage; if he learns she deceived him, she loses face and his trust permanently.
The First Wife's inventory is an institutional right; interfering directly confirms the presence of contraband and invites formal confiscation.
The chest's physical weight and sealed status mean any transport or opening leaves witnesses and evidence among the servants, who report to different mistresses.

angles.directive: Voluntarily approach the First Wife, tearfully confess a small portion of 'bridal savings from my late mother' and offer her a generous cut as a sisterly gift, thereby making her guardian of the secret rather than its prosecutor.

Stage an accident where a clumsy, easily-blamed kitchen maid knocks over the chest, scattering its contents publicly; in the chaos, absorb the loss of some pieces to the mob while salvaging the rest as 'miraculously intact,' shifting blame to the maid and framing the concealment as mere disorganization.

Open the chest in the master’s presence under the guise of a 'romantic game' for his amusement, framing the gold as theatrical stage-prop ingots for a private erotic performance, thereby converting currency into a shared pleasure secret that permanently redefines the assets as conjugal playthings.

Bury the chest in the garden under the cover of planting auspicious osmanthus trees, feigning simplicity and poverty for the first year, sacrificing potential interest and immediate luxury to ensure discovery becomes impossible until household politics shift.

threads: Sixth Sister Plum approaches the First Wife before the inventory begins, tearfully confessing a small portion of the chest’s jewels as her late mother’s bridal savings and immediately offering a one-third cut as a sisterly token, thereby transferring the secret’s legal jeopardy into a shared financial stake that makes the First Wife its guardian. This move preempts formal discovery by converting the chest’s contraband from an institutional violation into a mutually beneficial, unspoken arrangement that the First Wife will protect to preserve her own illicit gain.

I will have the kitchen maid, notorious for her clumsiness, stumble against the wedding chest with a stack of firewood precisely as the servants gather for the midday meal, cracking the false bottom and broadcasting jewels across the floor. Amid the scramble, I will immediately declare the pieces “old trinkets I stuffed in disordered haste” and pocket the most valuable while letting the scullions snatch a few trinkets as the price of a permanently blurred narrative that pins the concealment on the maid’s accident, not my design.

Sixth Sister Plum will stage an impromptu boudoir performance, unlocking the chest before the master under the pretense of a theatrical game and displaying the gold as gilded stage ingots she has secretly commissioned for an erotic tableau. She thus permanently reclassifies the assets as intimate conjugal playthings, making their existence a shared secret that preemptively nullifies any inventory as a violation of the master’s private pleasures.

Sixth Sister Plum will personally dig the planting pit by night, using the delivery of osmanthus saplings as cover, entombing the sealed chest directly beneath the root ball so that the disturbed soil is indistinguishable from the planting mound. She then wears only plain wooden hairpins and coarse silk for the first year, erasing every visible trace of wealth to render the garden’s secret inert until the household alliances have shifted.

judge.note: Strategies 1,3 assume complicity without testing resistance; 4 ignores soil disturbance detection.

theme: securing HONEST COUNSEL while surrounded by flatterers, without eroding authority
problem: A newly crowned prince suspects his courtiers' flattery masks disloyalty and hidden ambitions, yet to rule firmly he must know the truth of his realm and their hearts. How can he obtain unfiltered counsel without appearing suspicious or weak, thus undermining the authority he seeks to secure?
facets: The need for accurate intelligence to pre-empt conspiracies and policy failures.
The risk that openly soliciting criticism signals insecurity and invites challenges.
The danger that flatterers, once entrenched, become power brokers who distort information for their own gain.

angles.directive: Purge the court of suspected flatterers ruthlessly, making an example that only blunt honesty is tolerated, even if it means creating enemies who may plot in the shadows.
Withdraw from open court, relying instead on a secret network of lowborn informants and foreign advisors who have no stake in flattery, preserving authority through distance and unpredictability.
Institute a formal council requiring written, anonymous proposals on state matters; by integrating their ideas into policy, you channel ambition into constructive service and make flattery futile.

Proclaim that a prince demonstrates his power by inviting public disagreement in court, reframing honest challenge as a ritual of loyalty, so that flattery becomes a mark of cowardice and truth a sign of strength.

threads: The prince will arrest his three most prominent sycophants during a public feast, charge them with poisoning his judgment rather than his cup, and have them beheaded before the dessert course while decreeing that any courtier who voices agreement without a documented dissenting alternative within one week forfeits their title and right to speak. He then seeds a deliberate false rumor of rebellion among the remaining nobles to smoke out those who whisper rather than spit their disloyalty in his face.

The prince will permanently vacate the throne room and govern from a sequestered hunting lodge, issuing all commands through sealed, untraceable couriers while compensating a covert network of servants, foreign merchants, and displaced scholars exclusively in anonymous coin and asylum to relay unfiltered court conversations. By never appearing in person and striking based on intelligence no noble can trace, he converts physical absence into a mechanism of terror that makes flattery pointless and replaces sycophancy with the constant, unpredictable pressure of invisible surveillance.

He installs a Privy Council of State where every member submits sealed, unsigned position papers on a fixed rotation of policy domains, and he publicly credits the council as a collective entity when adopting proposals, stripping individual flattery of currency by making authorship invisible and merit the sole vector of influence.

The prince issues an irrevocable edict establishing a weekly “Court of Contraries,” where every minister must publicly articulate one substantive objection to a royal policy or face exile for cowardice, while the prince silently grades each objection’s rigor and later cross-references it against private intelligence reports to map hidden alliances. He then rewards the most incisive challengers with visible tokens of favor, mechanistically converting dissent into a performance metric of loyalty that starves flatterers of relevance and forces conspirators to expose their reasoning in the open.

judge.note: Strategies 1&2 risk destabilizing rule; 3&4 underestimate courtier adaptation.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: You are the Second Wife in a prosperous merchant household who has uncovered proof that the head cook—the First Wife’s trusted protégé—has been skimming silver by inflating market prices and selling off delicacies, threatening the household’s finances and safety ahead of a crucial official banquet. Exposing him will shatter the fragile truce between the inner quarters, ignite a ruinous scandal, and lose you the master’s favor, yet staying silent invites slow ruin.
facets: The cook’s fraud bleeds household funds and raises the risk of a poisoning incident at the upcoming banquet that would destroy the family’s public face and official connections.
The cook is deeply entangled with the First Wife’s faction; any attack on him becomes an attack on her, fracturing the delicate power balance among the wives and concubines.
Your own standing depends on managing this without appearing either weak (allowing theft) or destructive (igniting a civil war that humiliates the household before influential guests).

angles.directive: Lay the irrefutable evidence directly before the master at the next family council and demand the cook’s immediate dismissal, publicly shaming the First Wife for her oversight and accepting the resulting explosion of rivalries as the price of cleansing the household.

Confront the cook in secret, offer him protection from exposure and a larger hidden share of the skim if he will spy on the First Wife’s intrigues and quietly channel part of the proceeds to you, turning his corruption into a covert instrument of your own influence.

Anonymously slip clues to the sharp-eyed steward about the kitchen discrepancies, then conspicuously remove yourself from the estate for a few days; let the steward uncover and report the crime, so that the First Wife’s wrath and blame fall upon him while you remain a distant, uninvolved party.

Propose a new 'harmonious household' provisioning system where all wives jointly inspect and seal incoming supplies, framing it as a virtuous reform to honor the ancestors and protect the master’s hospitality at the banquet—making the cook’s skimming structurally impossible without ever naming his crime or aggravating the First Wife.

threads: I will table the cook’s duplicate ledgers and a sworn servant affidavit at the family council, then move for his immediate expulsion while reading aloud the First Wife’s signed approval of his provisioning—converting her patronage into a public indictment of her oversight. The ensuing factional detonation is the necessary cautery that excises the embezzlement and signals zero tolerance for poison-risk before the banquet, regardless of the inner quarters’ shattered truce.

I summon the cook to my private storeroom at the third night watch, lay out the doctored receipts and the testimony of the grain merchant’s boy I’ve already bought, then offer him a stark choice: I conceal his fraud and increase his take by an eighth, in return for weekly reports on the First Wife’s correspondence and a fixed percentage of the skim routed to me through a blind account at the oil presser’s guild.

I will slip an unsigned ledger page detailing the cook’s off-book saffron sales and inflated poultry invoices into the steward’s record chest during the servants’ midday meal, then conspicuously depart to a fabricated family funeral for five days. The steward’s discovery compels him to audit and expose the entire fraud directly to the master, redirecting the First Wife’s inevitable retaliation onto the steward as the visible accuser while I remain untouched in the outer courtyard upon my return.

I will convene the inner quarters and propose a “Joint Provisioning Seal” protocol, requiring that all incoming supplies be inspected and stamped with each wife’s personal chop before the cook ever touches them, framed as a filial ritual to guarantee the purity of the master’s banquet offerings. This converts the cook’s monopoly on procurement into a transparent, multi-party verification gate that starves his skimming without a single accusation, while positioning the reform as an elevation of household virtue that the First Wife cannot publicly oppose.

judge.note: Strategy 2 assumes corruptibility without testing; Strategy 3 risks steward exposure backfiring onto Second Wife.

theme: a newcomer concubine PROTECTING CONCEALED WEALTH from predatory rivals and the master himself
problem: You are Sixth Sister, newest concubine in a wealthy household, who has secretly brought in a cache of silver and heirlooms from a previous protector. The Second Wife has begun sniffing out hidden valuables through the servants, while the master grows suspicious of any private resources not under his control. You must decide how to safeguard this concealed wealth—your only escape and security—before it is discovered, confiscated, or turned against you.
facets: The household economy is a zero-sum game: any wealth you possess is wealth denied to rivals, who will weaponize its discovery to paint you as disloyal or a thief.
Secrecy is already compromised—a maid saw the locked chest and rumors spread; now both the predatory Second Wife and the master's steward are circling.
The wealth's existence is both your insurance for a future escape and the greatest immediate liability that could destroy your tenuous position.

angles.directive: Preemptively reveal a sanitized portion of the wealth to the master as a ‘willing dowry contribution’ while framing the Second Wife’s curiosity as embezzlement envy—destroying the rival’s credibility before she can strike, and recasting yourself as the loyal, self-sacrificing concubine.

Spirit the chest out of the household entirely through a bribed outside merchant contact, storing it in a neutral location far from the compound, while publicly maintaining an austere, possession-free persona that starves all suspicion of its source.

Secretly approach the Second Wife and offer her a share of the hidden wealth as ‘sisterly tribute’ in exchange for her protection and a pact of mutual silence—transforming a predator into a compromised, invested ally who now has skin in your survival.

Convert the physical wealth into an intangible, unconfiscatable form by investing it through a trusted female go-between into a discreet pawnshop or lending circle outside the household gates, so that even if discovered, the ‘chest’ proves empty and the trail leads to a legitimate external enterprise you can disavow as a charitable trust.

threads: I will present a curated selection of the silver to the master under the guise of a dowry contribution while planting the steward’s own ledger—marked with forged discrepancies in the Second Wife’s household accounts—to reframe her investigation as a cover for her own skimming.

I will transfer the locked chest to a blind silk merchant from the river market, paying him with a single silver hairpin to store it in his warehouse loft under a false trader’s name, and then I will immediately request the steward conduct a public inventory of my belongings to demonstrate that I possess nothing beyond a servant’s dowry.

I will bring the Second Wife a selection of the most identifiable pieces—the jade bracelet and half the coined silver—as a ‘sisterly tribute’ in her private chamber, and secure her verbal agreement that accepting them makes her an equal partner in the secret, so any exposure of me automatically exposes her as a receiver of stolen property. This turns her from a predatory rival into a compromised guardian, because she now has more to lose from discovery than I do.

I will transfer the entire cache to Widow Chen, the rice merchant’s sister outside the east gate, to be recorded as a charitable trust capital in her pawnshop, with a notarized deed naming me only as a minor interest beneficiary and explicitly disclaiming ownership.  
When the empty chest is inevitably discovered, I will produce that deed and perform grief that my late protector’s bequest was already donated to fund orphans, making any accusation of hoarding a slander against a pious act and leaving the master no confiscatable asset to seize.

judge.note: Strategies 3&4 assume unrealistic compliance; notarized deed implausible in setting.

theme: a relative's or son-in-law's GAMBLING DEBT that threatens the family business
problem: Your profligate son-in-law has secretly signed away half the family silk shop’s capital to a gambling-den enforcer who is the nephew of the county magistrate; repayment falls due in three days, or he will present the IOUs along with fabricated evidence of tax fraud to the yamen.
facets: The debt-holder wields direct yamen influence and can weaponize a false tax-fraud charge to seize the entire business.
The son-in-law is married to your eldest daughter; open disgrace would destroy her marriage prospects and your household’s standing.
The shop provides livelihood for three branch families and a dozen hired hands, all dependent on its uninterrupted operation.

angles.directive: Sell the daughter’s dowry lands immediately to raise silver, deliver the cash with a sworn witness, and simultaneously deliver a private bribe to the magistrate detailing the enforcer’s own extortion racket—then publicly disown the son-in-law, strip him of all family name and shelter, and let the enforcer know the source of new trouble is cut off.

Transfer the shop’s stock, ledgers and liquid capital to a trusted cousin in a neighbouring county under a new shop sign before the deadline; leave a skeleton staff to offer a partial payment and a promissory note, then move the core family silently by night, leaving the son-in-law to face the enforcer alone.

Invite the enforcer to a private banquet and offer him a permanent 20% share of the silk shop’s annual profit and a voice in family councils if he destroys the IOUs and instead uses his uncle’s position to secure the business a tax exemption and protection from other predators.

File a counter-petition with the prefectural court alleging that the son-in-law was drugged and swindled by an organised extortion ring; present the IOUs as proof of a criminal syndicate preying on respectable households, reframing the debt as a public menace that requires official intervention on your behalf and rallying other merchants to your cause.

threads: I will liquidate the eldest daughter’s dowry lands within two days, deliver the full debt sum to the enforcer before a sworn village headman, and simultaneously place a private bribe in the magistrate’s hand along with a written account of the enforcer’s extortion racket. The moment the debt is receipted, I will publicly disown the son-in-law in the ancestral hall, expunge his name from the lineage register, and send word to the enforcer that the family conduit is severed and any further threat will confront a direct counter-accusation lodged with the magistrate.

The cousin in Lin’an will receive the stock and ledgers tonight under the name “Zhuohua Textiles,” while I drain the merchant account into his guild tally and leave only the shop boy and a promissory note stating that the son-in-law alone holds the remaining equity.

I host a private banquet for the enforcer and offer a legally witnessed partnership granting him 20% of annual net profits and a permanent seat in family council deliberations, conditional on him immediately destroying the IOUs and using his uncle’s authority to secure a formal yamen tax exemption and protection writ for the shop.  
This converts a hostile debt-holder into a shielded co-owner whose income now depends on the business’s uninterrupted success, dissolving the extortion threat and erecting a magistrate-backed barrier against all future predators.

I will file a counter-petition with the prefectural court naming the enforcer and his uncle, the county magistrate, as co-conspirators in an organized extortion ring that drugged and swindled my son-in-law, submitting the IOUs as tangible proof of the syndicate’s pattern of preying on respectable households.
Simultaneously, I will convene the silk guild and other merchants with comparable grievances to co-sign the petition, reframing the private debt as a systemic public menace that forces the prefect to investigate the magistrate’s clan and protect the commercial class.

judge.note: Strategies 3 & 4 assume magistrate cooperation without accounting for corruption incentives.

theme: after the master's death, a SHAKEDOWN forcing a choice between REPUTATION and LIQUIDITY
problem: After Ximen Qing's sudden death, a yamen runner presents his widow with ledgers detailing Ximen's bribery and murder of a rival, demanding 5,000 taels of silver or he will denounce the household to the censor. The family holds only 2,000 taels in cash, the rest locked in a silk business and concubines' jewelry; paying would force a ruinous fire sale and leave them destitute, while refusal risks scandal, criminal charges, and confiscation of the entire estate.
facets: The blackmailer holds genuine incriminating documents, but his political reach is uncertain—he may be a lone operator or backed by a hostile magistrate seeking to strip the Ximen estate.

The household is fragmented: the official wife controls the sealed silver, the former courtesan-concubine holds portable wealth (jewelry), and the steward manages the business ledgers, each with conflicting priorities and no trust.
Paying once signals weakness and likely invites further extortion from other officials and rivals; exposing the crimes could trigger a feeding frenzy that strips the household of all assets anyway.

angles.directive: Silence the threat by force: commission Ximen Qing’s former underworld associates to abduct the runner and destroy the ledgers, risking bloodshed and retaliation if he has hidden allies or official backing.

Stall the blackmailer with promises of payment while secretly appealing to a powerful eunuch patron who owed Ximen a favor, seeking to have the runner transferred or discredited before the censor can act, shifting the risk upward.

Transform the blackmailer into a permanent partner: offer him a salaried post as 'household protector' with a regular cut of the silk business, binding his interests to the family’s survival and converting a one-time shakedown into an ongoing, manageable relationship.

Preempt the scandal by voluntarily disclosing a lesser offense—such as falsifying customs fees—to the magistrate along with a generous public donation to a temple, framing the family as honest penitents targeted by a malicious extortionist, thereby defusing the blackmail and winning sympathy.

threads: Commission Ximen Qing’s former enforcer, Tiger Zhang, to intercept the runner on the Jinan road at night, kill him, and seize the ledgers before burning them at the silk warehouse kiln.

Offer the runner 500 taels now as a "sincerity payment" against a sworn promissory note for the balance due in thirty days, binding him with a witnessed document that implicates him in extortion if broken. Simultaneously, dispatch the steward to Eunuch He at the Directorate of Palace Servants with Ximen’s personal seal and a copy of the murder ledger, invoking the unpaid favor to secure an immediate transfer order reassigning the runner to a frontier garrison and a preemptive counter-denunciation for blackmail lodged with the censor’s office.

The widow, acting through the steward, will immediately offer the runner a legally witnessed contract appointing him "household protector" with a fixed monthly stipend of twenty taels and a three-percent share of the silk business’s net profits, contingent on his surrendering all incriminating ledgers for joint sealing in a neutral merchant guild vault. This permanently aligns his financial survival with the estate’s viability, converting him from a one-time extortionist into a vigilant insider who must actively suppress any official inquiry or rival shakedown that would destroy his ongoing income stream.

The official wife, through the steward, will file a preemptive confession of minor customs-fee falsification with the magistrate and simultaneously make a conspicuously large public donation to the Temple of the City God, casting the household as remorseful, upright supplicants. She will then lodge a formal denunciation of the runner as a malicious extortionist who fabricated the more heinous charges, ensuring that any subsequent revelation of the true ledgers is dismissed as a retaliatory slander from a corrupt underling.

judge.note: Strategies 2-4 assume institutional leverage; runner's actual power underestimated.

theme: a CENSOR or MAGISTRATE threatening to reopen the household's crimes unless paid off
problem: A newly appointed Imperial Censor demands an exorbitant monthly bribe or he will reopen the sealed case of your household’s poisoning of a rival merchant—a crime that would mean execution for your entire clan.
facets: The Censor is freshly installed and zealous to prove himself, yet he lacks deep local alliances, making him both lethal and isolated.
The household’s internal order is brittle: rival wives and concubines may exploit the crisis to settle private feuds or seize control.
Political currents in the capital are volatile; a household patron could rise or fall within months, drastically shifting the Censor’s leverage.

angles.directive: Have the Censor assassinated before he can file the reopened case, using a hired blade from the riverside underworld whom no one can trace back to your gate.
Liquidate the estate in secret, set a diversionary fire, and flee overnight with your core household and portable wealth to a distant province where you are unknown.

Propose a marriage alliance between your eldest son and the Censor’s daughter, while simultaneously installing the Censor as a permanent silent partner in your silk monopoly, binding his fate to yours.

Submit a preemptive memorial to the capital’s chief investigator, volunteering minor past tax indiscretions and charging the Censor with fabricating the poisoning accusation as part of an extortion scheme by a rival clique.

threads: We commission a “drowned man” from the Rivermouth Guild to intercept the Censor during his unaccompanied dusk survey of the Lower Sluice, forcing a slip that snaps his neck against the stone revetment and leaves the corpse adrift for three tide cycles before discovery, erasing all jurisdictional scent. Meanwhile, I plant a forged promissory note in the Second Wife’s coffer, backdated to the day of the Censor’s arrival, which frames her for conspiring to trade household secrets for her own immunity; I then trigger its exposure through a maid loyal to the First Concubine, so that the resulting intra-household execution silences the only faction brittle enough to fracture our wall of silence before the capital’s political winds shift.

We will compress the entire estate—warehouses, deeds, and vessels—into unmarked gold taels and gem-grade jade through a chain of blind brokers activated by midnight sealed instructions, completing the liquidation within two days before the Censor secures local informants. On the third night, we ignite the old timber market and the Censor’s temporary chambers simultaneously, then slip the household, stripped to a single-file string of unlit carts, through the River Gate and southward to the salt-fief of Xuning, where no ledger connects our name to the capital.

I will extend a formal betrothal of my eldest son to the Censor’s daughter, the marriage contract to be signed within the week, binding his bloodline to our survival. Simultaneously, the dowry deed will be drafted as an irrevocable silent partnership conferring thirty percent of the silk consortium’s net profits to a cutout trust in his name, disbursed quarterly, making his execution of us a direct forfeiture of his own wealth.

We will transmit a sealed memorial by fast courier directly to the Chief Investigator of the Censorate, self-reporting a three-percent underdeclaration of silk duties over four years while simultaneously embedding a detailed accusation that the Censor’s poisoning charge is a fabrication procured by the Zhou clique, supported by forged correspondence showing their coordination to extort us. This instantly recasts the reopened case as a corrupt factional attack, forcing the capital to investigate the Censor’s own collusion and stripping him of unilateral lethality before he can act.

judge.note: Each strategy commits decisively but assumes unrealistic downstream silence or control.

theme: a discovered AFFAIR — explosive knowledge a concubine can wield, trade, or bury
problem: You are a concubine in the Xi Men household. You have discovered that the master’s eldest son is having a secret nightly liaison with the personal maid of the master’s most favored concubine—a woman who has been eclipsing you. This knowledge could shatter the son’s inheritance prospects, implicate the rival concubine in negligence, and plunge the household into a purge. But any misstep could see you accused of fabrication and cast out. What do you do?
facets: The master’s mercurial temper: He is capable of violent rage but also of shrewd calculation; he may kill the messenger if he suspects manipulation.
Your own tenuous position: You have recently lost favor and income, and your servants are beginning to gossip—you need leverage, but cannot afford a failed gamble.
The maid’s dual loyalty: She is the favorite concubine’s trusted servant, so revealing the affair could be spun as an attack on that concubine, uniting them against you.

angles.directive: Expose the affair directly to the master, emphatically framing it as a threat to the household’s moral order and urging that the son be severely disciplined and the maid sold off—thereby removing the son’s future claim and tarnishing the rival concubine’s stewardship.

Lock the secret in your heart and feign ignorance. Watch and document, but take no action now, hoping either that the affair will fizzle out or that a more opportune moment will arise when you can wield the knowledge from a safer position.

Secretly approach the son, reveal what you know, and offer to shield the affair in return for a binding pact: his future support in household politics, financial favors, and a pledge to side with you against the favorite concubine when the time comes.

Reinterpret the liaison as a cunning plot: go to the master and claim you have uncovered a scheme by the favorite concubine to seduce the son through her maid in order to entangle him in a scandal and seize control of his inheritance. Present yourself as the loyal detector of a conspiracy, thereby redirecting punishment onto the rival while sheltering the son and earning his gratitude.

threads: I will present myself to the master at dawn with a rehearsed account of the son’s nightly rendezvous, framing the maid’s breach of quarters as a moral rot that demands the son be stripped of future privileges through public discipline and the maid sold immediately.
