By anchoring the accusation in the rival concubine’s negligent stewardship—without naming her as co-conspirator—I force the master to see her as the source of the contamination, collapsing the son’s inheritance path and her standing in a single stroke.

I will instruct my most discreet maid to nightly note the maid’s movements via hidden tally marks on rice paper stored in a false-bottomed sewing box, while I publicly commend the rival’s maid for her diligence to deepen my guise of oblivious goodwill. I will let the tally accumulate without intervention, waiting for the affair to either collapse from its own carelessness or for a future moment when the master’s temper is calculable and the evidence can be slipped anonymously into the household’s internal report by a bribed kitchen boy.

I intercept the eldest son in the unused east garden before dawn and present my knowledge not as a threat but as a shield I am willing to hold over him—on the condition that he immediately signs a witnessed private pledge granting me a fixed monthly stipend from his personal allowance and commits, in writing, to back my precedence over the favored concubine in all future household disputes. I then begin actively misdirecting the night watch and planting false evidence of the maid’s whereabouts elsewhere, converting my silence into a structural dependency that binds his survival to my ascendancy.

I reveal to the master that the favorite concubine has orchestrated the maid’s seduction of his son as a premeditated trap to disgrace the heir and redirect inheritance toward her own faction, positioning my disclosure as the act of a vigilant servant who intercepted the scheme before it could mature.

judge.note: Strategies 1,3,4 assume master's predictable reaction; Strategy 2 underestimates discovery risk.

theme: punishing a wealthy concubine when doing so risks her SUICIDE, public SCANDAL, and loss of her fortune
problem: Moonlily, the merchant Ximen Qing’s most favored concubine, has been caught embezzling household silver to fund her secret opium trade, but punishing her openly risks her suicide, a devastating public scandal, and the loss of her vast personal fortune which is entangled with the family’s accounts.
facets: Moonlily’s fortune is not only cash but also deeds and pawn tickets intermingled with household collateral, making it unrecoverable if she takes her own life in disgrace.
The local magistrate resents Ximen Qing and would exploit a scandal to investigate bribery and seize assets, ruining the entire household.
The other wives and concubines are watching, and any perceived weakness or favoritism will fracture the internal hierarchy and invite open rebellion.

angles.directive: Secretly confront Moonlily with proof, then offer to legitimize and expand her opium trade as a formal household enterprise under your control, making her a partner whose fortune and future are now tied to the family’s success rather than her own hidden ledger.

Leak an anonymous tip to the magistrate about an unrelated opium den, then hire a trusted intermediary to privately ‘discover’ Moonlily’s embezzlement and propose she buy her silence by transferring her fortune into a third-party trust that you secretly control, removing her wealth from the crisis equation without your visible hand.

Publicly announce that the missing silver was actually a secret loan authorized by you to fund an expansion of the family’s charitable pharmacy, reframing her crime as loyal initiative, and then discreetly reassign her responsibilities to isolate her from further accounts while preserving face.

Absorb the loss entirely: say nothing, allow Moonlily to believe her embezzlement is undiscovered, and slowly, over months, renegotiate the household’s entangled debts and redeem the pawned deeds using your own private reserves, sacrificing short-term liquidity and authority to avert any risk of suicide or scandal.

threads: I will summon Moonlily to a sealed study, lay out the duplicate account books alongside a drafted charter for the “Lotus Fragrance Trading Company” — a household-owned entity that folds her entire opium operation, contacts, and embezzled silver into formal shares vesting quarterly only while she serves as managing director, with her personal deeds and pawn tickets re-registered as collateral under the family name, making any walkout or suicide an instant forfeiture that leaves her penniless and forgotten. This single contract converts her from a hidden embezzler into a branded partner whose future wealth depends entirely on the enterprise’s visible success, while the company’s registration with the prefectural merchant guild recasts her illicit trade as a tax-paying medicinal herb business, stripping the resentful magistrate of his pretext and signaling to the other wives that the route to power and protection now runs through the family’s corporate structure, not its bedchamber.

I will leak an anonymous tip to the magistrate identifying a low-ranking opium runner operating out of a dockside warehouse, redirecting official scrutiny away from the household.  
Simultaneously, I commission a debt broker from a neighboring prefecture—compensated through a cutout—to confront Moonlily with a fabricated proof of her embezzlement and present a single, non-negotiable exit: her immediate execution of a quitclaim transferring all cash, deeds, and pawn tickets into an irrevocable trust managed by a shell partnership that I control through a blind nominee, sealing her financial destruction the moment she signs.

Ximen Qing will summon the entire household and the local magistrate’s clerk to announce that the missing silver was a clandestine loan he personally authorized for Moonlily to expand the family’s charitable pharmacy, retroactively drafting a dated promissory note to cement the fiction. Immediately afterward, he will publicly commend her initiative but reassign her to oversee the pharmacy’s operations in a distant branch, physically separating her from the household’s main accounts while quietly absorbing her entangled deeds and pawn tickets into a consolidated family trust managed by a neutral steward.

Ximen Qing will cover every tael Moonlily embezzled from his private reserves without a whisper, letting her trade and spend as if her theft remains invisible. He will personally and quietly approach each creditor over the coming months to redeem the intermingled pawn tickets and deeds, untangling her fortune from the household’s collateral solely with his own liquidity and absorbing the entire financial blow to eliminate any trigger for suicide, public disgrace, or magisterial investigation.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 both formalize/legitimize; downstream reactions underspecified or fantastical.

theme: protecting a POSTHUMOUS male HEIR from rivals who would kill to preserve their own futures
problem: You are the senior wife of the dead merchant, delivered of his sole posthumous male heir. His sharpest concubine, mother of an existing son, controls the household’s stewards and has bribed the kitchen; she will poison the infant before the naming ceremony in three days solidifies his legal claim. You must secure the baby’s survival and inheritance.
facets: The heir is a defenseless infant whose life depends entirely on others’ vigilance.
Rivals hold the household’s operational levers—servants, food, daily access—and can arrange an “accident” that leaves no trace.
The naming ceremony is a rigid deadline: after it the child’s claim becomes legally unassailable, making preemptive killing the rival’s only rational move.

angles.directive: Strike first: arrange a fatal mishap for the concubine or her son before the ceremony, using a trusted maid to deliver poisoned supplements or to plant evidence of theft that will prompt the magistrate to seize her.
Slip away by night with the infant, your old maid, and a hidden letter of credit; resettle far off where the estate holds no meaning, preserving the bloodline while abandoning the contested wealth.

Propose a binding pact: name the concubine regent and guardian of the infant, betroth the newborn to her daughter, and stipulate that her own son inherits only if the infant dies childless—thus fusing their fates into one house.

Reframe the inheritance as the merchant’s trading network, not his land; announce to clan elders that the household becomes a joint-stock partnership with shares for all legitimate issue, managed by an untouchable outside trustee, making the infant’s survival a dividend to all.

threads: I will have my handmaid, who still draws her salary from the grain stores and moves unseen among the kitchen staff, slip powdered monkshood into the concubine’s nightly tonic of goat’s milk and honey before tomorrow’s dawn, and simultaneously plant a sealed pouch of the household silver beneath her son’s sleeping mat so that when the magistrate’s men discover the theft—reported an hour after she fails to wake—both claims are extinguished in a single stroke.

Slip out through the servants’ gate at the third bell after midnight with only the infant, the old maid, and the leather pouch containing the sealed letter of credit from your late husband’s secondary banking house in Calabar, then board a northbound river barge before dawn so that by the time the household stirs you are already beyond the reach of the concubine’s stewards and the estate’s jurisdiction.

I will assemble the household elders and publicly offer the concubine a sworn, witnessed contract naming her sole regent and guardian of the infant, betrothing the newborn to her own daughter, and specifying that her existing son inherits only if my child dies without living issue—thereby converting her son’s claim into a contingent remainder that is worthless if the infant perishes.  
To make the pact irreversible before the naming ceremony, I will have its terms inscribed on the family shrine and announce that any violation triggers immediate disinheritance of all her line, binding her survival instinct to the baby’s safety through a single, fused succession architecture.

I immediately summon the clan elders and propose reincorporating the entire trading network as a perpetual joint-stock partnership, with equal equity shares distributed to every legitimate issue and operational authority bonded to an outside guild trustee, thereby severing kitchen control from economic power. The infant’s survival becomes the dividend trigger: his death voids the trust and freezes all assets, instantly liquidating the concubine’s son’s sole future income stream into zero.

judge.note: Strategies 3-4 assume unrealistic elder/guild compliance; 1 assumes flawless execution.

theme: rival wives and concubines maneuvering over an HEIR's INHERITANCE as the master weakens or dies
problem: You are the childless principal wife of a dying merchant house. A concubine has secretly smuggled a stranger’s newborn into the inner quarters, intending to present the child as the master’s posthumous heir. If you expose the fraud, you risk shattering the household and being blamed for slander; if you stay silent, you become complicit and lose control of the inheritance. How do you secure your future?
facets: The master lies unconscious and may die within hours; his last coherent words gave no heir, making the first to present a ‘son’ the de facto custodian of the estate.
The concubine’s scheme depends on a bribed wet-nurse and a forged birth note, but the official household seal remains locked in a chest only you can open.
You hold nominal authority as first wife, yet your barrenness has eroded respect, and any open move will draw ravenous in-laws and officials eager to carve up the property.

angles.directive: Summon senior servants and clan witnesses to the sickroom, drag the wet-nurse before the dying master, and force a public denunciation—pressing for a deathbed judgment that strips the concubine of status and expels the intruder child.

Feign a sudden collapse and withdraw to your chambers, letting the concubine present the child unchallenged. Keep silent while gathering written proof and sworn testimony, to be used as leverage after the funeral when the household’s power structure realigns and you can broker a settlement from a position of safety.

Visit the concubine discreetly that night and offer a pact: you will publicly confirm the child as the master’s true blood, and in exchange she signs a sealed contract granting you half the estate and the title of honored mother-in-residence—turning a rival’s plot into a shared, profitable fiction.

Publicly declare that no heir can be accepted without a formal Eight Characters ceremony supervised by the clan astrologer. Insist the child’s birth hour be matched with the master’s, knowing the mismatch will prove the fraud through ritual procedure—letting cosmological truth expose the lie without your personal accusation.

threads: I will unlock the household seal before any witnesses, draft a formal accusation of infant substitution in the master’s name, and then publicly stage the wet-nurse’s coerced confession at his bedside so the dying patriarch’s final nod or silence becomes the irreversible deathbed edict that disinherits the concubine and the intruder child.

I will stage a collapse with a credible witness present to document my incapacitation, then direct my personal maid to silently secure the locked seal-chest in my chambers and begin transcribing the wet-nurse’s confession under oath once the household’s attention shifts to the funeral rites.

I will enter the concubine’s quarters after the midnight watch with a pre-drafted contract ceding me half the estate and the permanent title of honored mother-in-residence, then unlock the household seal chest to stamp both that contract and her forged birth note in a single, irreversible act. This binds her fraud to my legal claim, making us joint architects of the lie and ensuring my future through a documented, sealed pact rather than a fragile gamble on exposure or silence.

I will immediately announce to the entire household that the master’s final incoherent state requires any heir to be validated through the long-dormant “Verification of the Natal Seal,” a ritual I alone can initiate by unlocking the chest containing the house seal, and I have already briefed the temple official that the rite demands matching the infant’s birth-hour to the master’s last recorded breath, a cosmological contradiction the bribed astrologer will publicly decode as proof of a broken bloodline. When the concubine presents the child before the assembled clan elders, the official’s pronouncement that the stars reject the infant as no true scion of this house will transform me from suspect barren wife into the neutral vessel of ancestral law, leaving her no ground to accuse me of slander while the estate defaults to my custodianship.

judge.note: Strategies 3–4 assume implausible compliance; bribery/collusion fragile.

theme: a blackmailer holding a DOCUMENT of past bribes; act before it is used
problem: Ximen Qing’s former steward, Lai Wang, has stolen a secret ledger detailing bribes paid to Magistrate Li to cover up a poisoning, and now demands a vast sum and the hand of Ximen’s favorite concubine, threatening to deliver the ledger to the censor within three days. Ximen must decide how to neutralize this mortal threat before the deadline expires.
facets: The ledger is genuine but the steward may have made copies, so destroying the original does not guarantee safety.
Rival concubine Golden Lotus despises the demanded concubine and would gladly see her ruined, creating internal pressure to refuse or to sacrifice the woman.
Ximen’s patron, Grand Marshal Yang, can shield him from most accusations—but only if Ximen acts before the censor formally receives the evidence, after which even Yang might abandon him.

angles.directive: Have the steward quietly murdered and the ledger destroyed, framing a thief for the death.

Pretend to agree to the demands but stall delivery of money under pretext of gathering funds, while secretly relocating assets and bribing a higher official to issue a preemptive warrant against the steward for theft, thus delaying and redirecting the threat.

Offer the steward a lucrative partnership in a silk venture and a minor concubine, ensuring his name appears on incriminating trade documents so that any exposure would implicate him as co-conspirator, binding his fate to the household.

Preemptively present the ledger to the magistrate as proof of voluntary 'gifts' to officials for public works, accompanied by a confession of minor tax oversights, thereby recasting the bribes as civic-minded donations and disarming the blackmailer’s weapon.

threads: Dispatch a housebreaker tonight to Lai Wang’s rented room: slit his throat, incinerate every document found there, and plant a counterfeit pawn ticket plus a sack of stolen trinkets to stage a burglary-homicide, then have the magistrate’s clerk rule the death a random thief’s kill before any confederate can surface a copy.

We feign full acceptance of Lai Wang’s terms but delay money delivery by claiming a three-day liquidity crunch, while covertly shifting all exposed assets into property held under Grand Marshal Yang’s name. Simultaneously, we bribe the prefectural judge to issue a preemptive arrest warrant for theft of the ledger, transforming Lai Wang into a wanted fugitive before the censor ever receives the evidence.

Ximen offers Lai Wang a partnership in the Hangzhou silk cartel and the concubine Chunmei, then immediately compels him to imprint his personal seal on the under-valuation receipts that record fresh bribes to Magistrate Li. Once his mark stains those documents, any delivery of the ledger to the censor simultaneously indicts the steward as co-briber in the identical poisoning cover-up, making his threat self-immolating and irreversible.

Ximen Qing will personally deliver the original ledger to Magistrate Li within twenty-four hours, accompanied by a sworn statement that reclassifies every bribe as a voluntary contribution to the “Municipal Public Works and Famine Relief Fund,” and simultaneously file a preemptive confession of minor tax underpayments with a full remittance to demonstrate transparent civic intent. This gives the magistrate immediate written cover to treat the ledger as official proof of virtuous donations, so that when Lai Wang’s copies surface they only corroborate a state-sanctioned record of philanthropy, leaving the steward’s threat mechanically harmless because the “evidence” now exonerates its targets.

judge.note: Strategies 3 & 4 assume unrealistic compliance; magistrate co-option fragile.

theme: a trusted STEWARD's divided loyalty: he knows the master's bribery secrets and is caught skimming
problem: The trusted steward of a wealthy merchant, caught skimming silver, must decide his next move while holding knowledge of his master's systematic bribery of the local magistrate—a secret that could destroy the household or save him, depending on how he wields it.
facets: The magistrate is in the master's pocket; any legal complaint by the steward would be twisted into a death sentence for theft.
The steward has a hidden stash of silver sufficient to flee, but his wife and child are in the master's household as servants.
The master's first wife suspects the steward and might exploit this to remove a rival, while the concubine who favors him could be a silent ally.

angles.directive: Threaten the master with immediate exposure to the higher prefect unless he grants a full pardon, a share of the bribery profits, and a written guarantee of safety.
Flee at once with the stolen silver, leaving a forged letter suggesting the master's rival concubine was the embezzler, buying time and confusion.
Offer to take over the delicate bribe logistics, arguing that his skimming was merely a trial run, and propose a partnership where he becomes the master's indispensable fixer.

Travel to the provincial inspector and confess both his theft and the master's bribery, seeking to become a crown witness under amnesty, thereby turning personal guilt into a weapon against the household's corruption.

threads: I will copy the magistrate’s bribery ledger and present the duplicate to the master in his private study, demanding a signed pardon, a sealed share-of-profits contract, and a hostage bond placing his concubine in my custody until my family is safely beyond the prefect’s jurisdiction.

I take the stolen silver at midnight and flee the city by the east road, leaving behind a letter forged in the concubine’s hand that confesses to skimming the merchant’s funds and implicates a fictitious accomplice among the household guards. This forces the master to waste critical hours interrogating the rival concubine and her circle, while I gain an uncontested head start to cross the provincial boundary before anyone connects the missing silver to my disappearance.

I present my skimming to the master as an unsolicited stress test that exposed the exact weak point in his bribery chain with the magistrate, and I offer to formalize my role as his exclusive bagman: I will handle every payment, take a fixed percentage as handler's fee, and archive irrefutable proof of each transaction under seal, ensuring my wife and child become permanent collateral while making my survival mathematically identical to the survival of his arrangement.

He slips away before dawn to the provincial inspector’s tribunal, submits a written confession detailing both his embezzlement and the master’s bribery of the magistrate, and formally petitions for crown witness amnesty under the statute that voids punishment in exchange for unmasking official corruption. This reclassifies his theft as a necessary extraction of evidence, converting personal guilt into the instrument that legally dissolves the household’s protection and forces the state to secure his family as material witnesses.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 assume coercion succeeds; 4 assumes tribunal exists and functions fairly.

theme: a neglected concubine selling household INTELLIGENCE to rival houses for favor and silver
problem: A neglected concubine who has been selling household secrets to a rival merchant discovers that the rival intends to use her latest intelligence to launch a ruinous lawsuit that will expose her as the source, just as her own household begins to grant her small favors. She must act before the suit is filed.
facets: The rival will discard her once the lawsuit succeeds, so she cannot trust their promises of safety or reward.
The newly granted small garden signals a fragile rise in her status—something to lose if she is unmasked or if the household falls.
The lawsuit is imminent, leaving only a narrow window to move; hesitation means losing all options.

angles.directive: Confess the espionage selectively to your master, reveal the rival's plot, and offer to help sabotage the lawsuit, eliminating the rival's threat in exchange for a chance at clemency.
Disappear without warning before the scandal breaks, taking your hidden silver and fleeing to a distant province where neither household can find you.

Approach the rival secretly and propose a double game: feed them falsified evidence that leads them into a counter-suit, in exchange for a larger payment and a written guarantee of your protection, integrating yourself as the linchpin of both sides.
Plant incriminating correspondence in the quarters of a favored rival concubine, then tip off the household that she is the source of the leaks, deflecting suspicion and punishment onto her.

threads: She will approach the master tonight in his study, disclosing only that she has been feeding the rival fabricated household secrets for weeks and now possesses a written confession from the rival’s bribed clerk—obtained by threatening to expose his embezzlement—that details the planned suit’s reliance on her forgeries, then offer to submit that confession and the forged documents to the magistrate as proof of the rival’s malicious prosecution, collapsing the lawsuit and destroying the rival’s credibility in a single counter-filing.

I leave tonight through the servants’ gate in a plain wool cloak with my silver sewn into the lining, hire a cart to the river docks, and board the first barge to the southern provinces under a dead cousin’s name. I leave no note, no trace, and let the rival’s lawsuit detonate behind me against an empty room, severing both households’ claim at once.

I will approach the rival tonight and propose a secret pact: I feed them a forged ledger showing the household’s supposed tax fraud, designed to trigger a countersuit for malicious prosecution the instant they file, in exchange for half the silver now and a witnessed, signed guarantee of my immunity and continued stipend. I then embed myself as the sole conduit of all future intelligence, doctoring every piece so both sides remain lethally dependent on my double game.

Plant a forged letter from the rival merchant in the favored concubine’s quarters, one that explicitly thanks her for the recent intelligence about the household’s garden and legal vulnerabilities and promises a reward upon the lawsuit’s success. Then leave an anonymous note for the steward, claiming she was seen exchanging documents with the rival’s agent near the garden gate, ensuring a search of her room reveals the letter and redirects all suspicion and punishment onto her.

judge.note: Strategy 3 assumes rival accepts obvious trap; Strategy 4 assumes framing succeeds cleanly.

theme: a CENSOR or MAGISTRATE threatening to reopen the household's crimes unless paid off
problem: Imperial Censor Zhang has uncovered the household’s poisoning of a rival and the bribery that suppressed it; he privately demands an exorbitant bribe and the hand of your most favored concubine, or he will submit a memorial that means your execution. You must act before the week is out.
facets: The censor’s evidence is not yet public, and he is known for impeccable documentation but also for using his office to fill his pockets.
Your household is a nest of jealous wives and concubines who would eagerly betray you to the censor if they saw a path to surviving your fall.
The local magistrate owes you life-long favors but is terrified of the censor’s authority and would likely turn against you to save himself.

angles.directive: Arrange the censor’s accidental death before he files the memorial, using hired thugs and a poisoning that mirrors your past crime, then destroy the evidence he holds.
Fake your own death, spread rumors of a sudden illness, and use the chaos to spirit your movable wealth to a distant province where the case cannot reach you.
Offer the censor not just the demanded bribe and concubine, but a permanent secret stake in your silk smuggling operation, turning him into a partner whose fortune depends on your prosperity.

Compose a memorial to the Emperor confessing the household’s lesser offenses under duress, exposing the censor’s extortion, and petition for a pardon by offering to testify against a wider network of corrupt officials.

threads: Hire the Black Lotus gang to intercept Censor Zhang on the river road, force a fatal dose of the same aconite-laced wine used on the rival, and weigh the body with stones to simulate a drunken drowning that swallows his document satchel. Once death is confirmed, dispatch a trusted household agent to ignite a delayed grease fire in his private archive, reducing every ledger and scroll to ash before the magistrate can seize them.

I will ingest a meticulously metered dose of prepared tetrodotoxin at the hour of the rat to induce a deathlike coma with no detectable breath, while my most trusted steward simultaneously summons the household physician to pronounce death from a virulent, disfiguring pox that demands immediate sealed cremation, and during the ensuing panic he will load my portable gold and jade into the false-bottomed grain carts of a pre-contracted salt smuggler bound for a purchased estate in Lingnan beyond the censor’s jurisdictional reach.  
The jealous concubines, convinced of my corpse and desperate to survive the censor’s scrutiny, will tear each other apart with accusations and flight, creating exactly the chaotic smoke screen that lets my steward fire the residence and obliterate every household register, debt record, and personal seal before dawn.

I arrange for my most trusted steward to deliver to Censor Zhang’s residence tonight the demanded concubine, the bribe silver, and a signed, sealed partnership deed granting him a twenty-percent silent stake in the silk smuggling route along with an immediate profit disbursement of raw silk from this month’s shipment, the contract including a clause that his share is forfeit upon any interruption of my household’s operations.  
I then meet Zhang alone at dawn, hand over my personal seal as collateral and a copy of the route’s patrol schedules to operationalize his new dependency, and explicitly demonstrate that the ledgers and his signed receipt will be placed with a dead-man’s-switch third party, so that his prosperity is now inseparable from mine.

Draft a memorial that confesses the household’s bribery of the local magistrate as a lesser offense coerced by Censor Zhang’s prior extortion, while exposing his current demand for your concubine and silver as the latest act in a documented pattern of graft.  
Simultaneously petition the Emperor for a full pardon by volunteering to provide sworn testimony and ledgers identifying a specific network of corrupt censors and provincial officials, thereby transforming the censor’s private leverage into the trigger for an imperial anti-corruption purge.

judge.note: Strategies 2&4 assume unrealistic imperial/bureaucratic responsiveness; 1 underestimates investigation.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: Ximen Qing discovers that the kitchen steward—a long-serving retainer and protégé of his principal wife—has been embezzling silver and selling off delicacies on the side; exposing him openly would humiliate the first wife, ignite a vicious power struggle among the concubines, and shatter the household’s fragile peace, yet ignoring the theft invites bolder corruption and a loss of the master’s face.
facets: The steward’s skimming is shielded by his favored status and the first wife’s patronage, making any accusation a direct blow to her authority.
Rival concubines, especially the ambitious second wife, are poised to exploit the scandal to usurp the first wife’s standing, turning the household into a warring camp.
Inaction signals weakness to servants and merchants alike, eroding Ximen Qing’s control over his entire domestic economy.

angles.directive: Seize the steward publicly, have him beaten and stripped of position in front of the entire household, recover whatever silver can be found, and make a brutal example—deliberately shaming the first wife to reassert absolute dominance, whatever the cost in domestic harmony.

Pretend to notice nothing; quietly reassign the steward to a distant rural estate with a face-saving title, removing him from the kitchen while avoiding a direct accusation—buying time and hoping the wives do not connect the dots, delaying the fracture.

Summon the steward privately, confront him with the evidence, then compel him to repay the stolen sums in secret installments and become an informant on the inner gossip of the women’s quarters—turning a thief into a double-edged asset while preserving the existing order.

Announce that the household accounts are confusing, declare a transition to a new “open kitchen” system where each wife jointly inspects the ledgers and shares management, reframing the theft as an innocent bookkeeping muddle, stripping the steward’s monopoly without any accusation, and binding the rivals in a common task.

threads: Seize the steward at the morning kitchen tally, have him bound and beaten in the central courtyard before the assembled household, then personally strip his steward’s sash and cast him out with only the clothes on his back while announcing that any servant who skims will lose both position and limb—and let the first wife stand there watching her protégé’s ruin without a single word of defense permitted.

I will summon the steward, commend his years of service, and appoint him overseer of the Longxi estate with an elevated title, framing the transfer as a promotion rather than a demotion. I will not acknowledge the theft, instead issuing a directive that the kitchen staff be rotated to "refresh" the household, thereby removing his access without a single charge being aired.

He will summon the steward to the private study, present the inventory discrepancy and witness accounts, and dictate a secret schedule of restitution in silver installments, making it irrevocably clear that the only alternative to immediate public disgrace is to henceforth serve as a confidential informant reporting all whispered rivalries, plots, and indiscretions from the women’s quarters directly to him. This converts the embezzler into a permanently compromised double agent whose survival depends on the first wife’s stability, thereby feeding Ximen Qing a stream of internal intelligence that preempts the second wife’s schemes while the steward’s visible loyalty and quiet repayment reinforce the existing hierarchy without a single overt scandal.

Ximen Qing will summon the entire household and declare that, due to irreconcilable bookkeeping confusion, the kitchen is to be reorganized into an open system where each wife jointly inspects the ledgers and shares procurement duties, explicitly recasting the steward’s discrepancies as clerical errors. This single decree dissolves the steward’s protected monopoly without any accusation, forces the rival concubines into a collaborative oversight role that neutralizes their power struggle, and reasserts the master’s authority through procedural redesign rather than punitive exposure.

judge.note: Strategy 3 assumes steward accepts blackmail; Strategy 4 assumes wives cooperate.

theme: a discovered AFFAIR — explosive knowledge a concubine can wield, trade, or bury
problem: Ping'er discovers Ximen Qing's clandestine affair with the servant girl Ru Yi while Lady Moon is distributing monthly allowances, giving Ping'er a secret that could ruin Ru Yi, wound Lady Moon's authority, unsettle all rival wives, or be buried for uncertain gain.
facets: Ru Yi's fate: a servant with no protector, easily expelled or beaten—Ping'er can destroy her or become her hidden patron.
Lady Moon's fragile authority: exposing the affair humiliates the first wife publicly, potentially accelerating a power vacuum that other concubines like Pan Jinlian would exploit.
Timing and witnesses: the knowledge is fresh and unshared, but the window is narrow—once Ximen Qing or another maid suspects Ping'er knows, her leverage mutates into vulnerability.

angles.directive: Become Ru Yi's secret protector: coach her on how to keep Ximen Qing's interest while feeding Ping'er intelligence from his inner chamber, converting a rival's asset into your own invisible influence network.

Anonymously reveal the affair to Pan Jinlian, the most venomous concubine, and let her destroy Ru Yi publicly—removing the servant, destabilizing Lady Moon, and exhausting Jinlian's political capital in one stroke while your hands stay clean.

Leak the rumour through the kitchen gossip chain tied to Aunt Wu, the elderly matriarch's maid, so the scandal reaches the family elder's ears and forces an external, generational judgment that sidelines all the young women equally.

Bury the knowledge completely and voluntarily transfer one of your own minor household duties to Ru Yi as an ostensible kindness, binding her through gratitude while sacrificing a piece of your own formal standing to earn a long-term ally who owes you a silencing debt.

threads: Ping'er intercepts Ru Yi in the garden tonight, offering silence and a precise tutorial on Ximen Qing's predilections in exchange for a weekly whispered report on his moods and pillow talk, converting the scandal into a secret patronage. She immediately establishes a dead-drop behind the Guanyin shrine so Ru Yi feeds intelligence without direct contact, embedding an untraceable surveillance node in his inner chamber.

I slip an unsigned note under Pan Jinlian’s door tonight, detailing Ximen Qing’s midnight visit to Ru Yi’s quarters and the stolen hairpin, written on common paper in a deliberately crude hand that mimics a kitchen maid’s scrawl.  
Jinlian’s inevitable public evisceration of Ru Yi will remove the servant, expose Lady Moon’s impotence before the household, and exhaust Jinlian’s political credibility in a single spectacle—all while my name never enters the aftermath.

Ping'er will murmur to Aunt Wu’s scullery maid that she glimpsed Ru Yi slipping from Ximen Qing’s study with a torn sleeve and a silver hairpin, then feign reluctance to say more, so the maid carries the half-confession upstairs as her own discovery, igniting the matriarch’s direct interrogation and a house-wide reckoning that reduces every wife and concubine to equal supplicants before the elder’s verdict.

Ping'er permanently neutralizes the secret by voluntarily ceding her own minor duty of overseeing the monthly silk inventory to Ru Yi, performing the transfer in front of a neutral maid to establish an unassailable public record of unsolicited generosity. This deliberate sacrifice of a sliver of formal standing purchases Ru Yi's silent loyalty through an unpayable debt, converting knowledge that could unravel the household into a private bond that requires neither acknowledgment nor words.

judge.note: Strategy 1 over-engineers surveillance; Strategy 2 risks exposure; Strategy 4 under-specifies.

theme: a relative's or son-in-law's GAMBLING DEBT that threatens the family business
problem: Your son-in-law Chen has secretly gambled away 800 taels of silver borrowed against your family pawnshop’s deed, and underworld debt collectors will arrive in three days to seize the business unless the debt is satisfied.
facets: Repayment would liquidate the operating capital that stocks the shop and funds the household’s daily expenses, collapsing income immediately.
Chen holds unique knowledge of your smuggling routes with the salt commissioner’s office—routes that generate the hidden profits keeping your clan afloat.
The debt collectors are front-men for Magistrate Hu, a corrupt official whose favor you still need for an upcoming land dispute, making brute resistance politically dangerous.

angles.directive: Disown Chen publicly, report the underworld lenders to the prefectural censor for usury, and offer the pawnshop deed as collateral for a new loan from a rival merchant to buy out the debt at a discount—sacrificing Chen and your reputation to sever the obligation entirely.

Smuggle Chen out of the province immediately, then petition Magistrate Hu for a three-month grace period by feigning ignorance of his involvement, claiming the debt is a fabrication by a runaway son-in-law and buying time to shift assets into your eldest wife’s dowry lands beyond seizure.

Invite the debt collectors to become silent partners in the pawnshop, offering them a 20% share of monthly profits in perpetuity in exchange for canceling the principal—converting a hostile liability into a protective alliance with Magistrate Hu’s own network.

Approach the wealthy widow Madame Zhou, whose daughter you once aided in a marriage crisis, and ask her to purchase the debt outright at half its face value, then lease the pawnshop back to you under her name—shifting the obligation to a third party too socially prominent for the collectors to harass.

threads: I publicly disown Chen before the magistrate’s collectors arrive, then file a formal usury complaint with the prefectural censor naming the lenders as illegal operators, which freezes their enforcement while simultaneously pledging the pawnshop deed to Merchant Hong in exchange for a discounted buyout of the debt at forty cents on the tael.

I will have my steward disguise Chen as a salt porter and move him along the hidden eastern smuggling route to a prearranged safe house in Lin'an by dawn, while I personally file a formal petition with Magistrate Hu swearing the debt is a fabrication by a now-absent son-in-law and requesting a three-month investigation to clear my house.  
During that window, I will transfer the pawnshop’s liquid capital into my eldest wife’s dowry lands—re-registering the deeds under her maiden clan’s name—so that even if the magistrate rules against us, the assets sit beyond legal seizure.

I will instruct Chen to personally escort the debt collectors to the pawnshop’s back office and present a notarized partnership contract that converts the 800-tael principal into a perpetual 20% profit share, thereby rebranding the debt as a magistrate-backed investment that shields the shop from future predation.

I will transfer the debt to Madame Zhou by having her purchase it from the collectors at four hundred taels and simultaneously execute a leaseback agreement that places the pawnshop’s title in her name, converting Chen’s obligation into a secured rental contract with a widow whose social standing makes Magistrate Hu’s enforcers recoil from direct confrontation.

judge.note: Strategies hallucinate magistrate cooperation; ignore underworld enforcement realities.

theme: after the master's death, a SHAKEDOWN forcing a choice between REPUTATION and LIQUIDITY
problem: After the master's death, the loyal steward Meng uncovers a ledger proving the First Wife had embezzled household funds for years. The corrupt prefect offers a shakedown: pay an exorbitant monthly tribute to keep the ledgers sealed and her reputation intact, or face public trial that would destroy the household’s standing but allow them to keep their rapidly dwindling silver.
facets: Paying the tribute bleeds liquidity, forcing the household to sell grain stores and pawn ancestral valuables, crippling their economic survival.
Public exposure would render the First Wife unmarriageable and the entire household radioactive to future business partners and marriage alliances.
The shakedown is illegal extortion, but the prefect is the seat of legal authority—exposing him risks a deadly retaliation through fabricated charges.

angles.directive: Send Meng to the provincial inspector with the copied ledger and a third of the household’s remaining silver as a counter-bribe, aiming to indict the prefect for extortion before he can strike—a single decisive move that eliminates the threat and the shakedown at its root, but risks total annihilation if the inspector is in the prefect’s pocket.

Rather than pay or defy the shakedown, pre-emptively dissolve the household’s public unity: the First Wife voluntarily enters a Buddhist nunnery as a display of penitence while the merchant estate is restructured into a charitable trust, changing the narrative from crime to pious renunciation—making the prefect’s threat irrelevant by removing the target and reframing the family as holy ascetics beyond the reach of scandal.

Invite the prefect into a silent partnership: offer a smaller, regular ‘consultation fee’ and a marriage proposal linking his nephew to the household’s younger daughter, transforming a parasitic threat into a vested ally whose own reputation becomes tied to the household’s stability—securing protection and stopping the bleeding without a single public accusation.

Leak doctored fragments of the ledger to the prefect’s political rivals, and anonymously fund their investigation, letting them bear the risk and legal cost of bringing the shakedown to light—the household becomes a mere witness to the prefect’s prosecution by others, preserving deniability and keeping silver in the coffers while the threat is destroyed by a third party.

threads: Meng takes the copied ledger and one-third of our remaining silver directly to the provincial inspector, filing a formal criminal indictment for extortion against the prefect. We eliminate the shakedown by prosecuting the extortionist, accepting the inspector’s unknown allegiance as the single point of failure.

The First Wife files a deed of renunciation tomorrow morning, transferring all personal assets and her legal status into the Eternal Light Buddhist nunnery while the household charter is simultaneously refiled as the “Compassionate Grain Trust,” a perpetual charitable entity under the abbot’s supervision. This converts the embezzlement ledger into a relic of past suffering that motivated sacred penitence, meaning the prefect’s threat to expose “scandal” would only amplify the narrative of pious transformation and leave him prosecuting a legally dissolved non-entity.

Meng will propose a legally binding betrothal contract between the prefect’s nephew and the household’s younger daughter, with the bride price structured as a permanent waiver of the monthly tribute and a nominal annual “consultation fee” paid to the prefect’s private accounts, so that the prefect’s own lineage becomes financial guarantor of the household’s solvency and any future attack on the household’s reputation would directly devalue his nephew’s marriage alliance.

By transmitting selectively redacted ledger entries to Inspector Han—the prefect’s most ambitious rival—through a cut-out merchant guild proxy, and simultaneously placing silver with a third-party litigation funder conditioned solely on Han’s prosecution of official corruption, we convert our liability into someone else’s career-making case while our household appears merely as a victim-witness dragged into proceedings we did not initiate.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 4 assume institutional actors behave predictably; underspecify failure modes.

theme: a trusted STEWARD's divided loyalty: he knows the master's bribery secrets and is caught skimming
problem: Caught skimming silver, you are summoned by your master Ximen Qing, whose bribery of a magistrate in a murder case you have documented; you must decide how to respond when you face him.
facets: The master’s need to maintain face and avoid scandal makes him both vengeful and cautious.
Your knowledge of his capital crime is your only leverage, but any overt threat could trigger a lethal response.
Rival servants and household gossips complicate the situation; some may have informed on you and now hope for your downfall.

angles.directive: Present an ultimatum: grant you a generous severance and safe passage, or you will deliver the bribery evidence to the censorate.
Flee before the summons, taking the ledgers, and seek sanctuary with a rival merchant who can use the bribery information to destroy Ximen Qing, deferring any direct confrontation.
Offer to become the master’s secret fixer, using your knowledge to manage bribe payments and silence witnesses, turning your skimming into a shared investment in deeper corruption.
Reframe the skimming as a loyal test of household controls and claim the silver was set aside to fund a gift to the magistrate, ensuring his continued silence.

threads: I will have a duplicate dossier of the bribery evidence placed with a hired scrivener outside the compound gate with instructions to deliver it to the censorate at dawn unless I personally retrieve it, while inside I will present Ximen Qing with the original and a prepared written demand for a signed safe-conduct pass and a silver severance of fifty taels. Facing him, I will state that the scrivener’s delivery is already in motion and only my immediate, unharmed departure with the pass and silver will halt it, then turn and walk out without waiting for a reply.

I slip through the laundry gate before the morning rice is boiled, carrying the ledgers disguised as packing slips in a lacquered sample case belonging to the silk merchant Wang Jin, arriving at his compound before Ximen Qing’s summons can be issued. I trade the ledgers for a signed indenture installing me as a household scribe under Wang Jin’s protection, while his legal proxy files the bribery documentation directly with the prefectural judge’s inspector, displacing the confrontation entirely onto institutional channels.

I will lay the documented bribe before Ximen Qing as a blueprint for a permanent fixer role, proposing to personally disburse all future magistrate payments and silence the specific rival servants who reported my skimming. This converts my theft into his operational capital, making my survival the mechanism that keeps his own neck out of the noose.

I will preemptively convert the discovered shortfall into a documented “integrity audit,” presenting Ximen Qing with a sealed ledger and a portion of the silver physically earmarked as a patron’s gift for the magistrate, thereby recasting the skimming as an unsanctioned but loyal stress-test of the household’s exposure. Simultaneously, I will let slip to the most envious rival servant that the master personally approved this secret reserve to keep the magistrate silent, binding Ximen Qing to my version or risking that the rumour of his capital crime travels through the very channels he would use to destroy me.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 assume predictable compliance; 2&4 risk institutional/rumor unpredictability.

theme: a neglected concubine selling household INTELLIGENCE to rival houses for favor and silver
problem: Li Jiao'er, a neglected concubine in the Ximen household, has been selling intelligence to rival merchant Zhang, who now demands the secret bribery ledgers; she learns he plans to destroy the household and likely abandon her, but refusing may trigger him exposing her past leaks.
facets: The value of intelligence is momentary—once the blow falls, the source loses all leverage.
The concubine’s status is entirely derivative; if the household falls, she falls with it, regardless of silver saved.
No one can be trusted: the rival house sees her as a disposable tool, her master would execute a traitor, and servants might betray her for a coin.

angles.directive: Confront Zhang directly with proof of his plot, threatening to expose him to the magistrate unless he buys your silence with a lump sum and arranges safe passage out of the province.
Take the silver you've hoarded and secretly flee the city tonight, seeking refuge in a distant temple or with a forgotten relative, leaving both households to their fate.
Confess to your master that you've uncovered a plot against him (concealing your role), presenting yourself as a loyal spy, and ask for elevated status and protection in return.

Become a double agent, deliberately feeding Zhang false information that implicates a hated rival concubine, while documenting his bribes to later expose him to your master, positioning yourself as the sole savior of the household.

threads: She will lure Zhang to the abandoned granary with a counterfeit ledger page bearing his seal, then press a pre-signed deposition and a runner poised at the magistrate’s gate against his throat, demanding one hundred taels in silver scrip and a travel pass stamped with his merchant chop before the incense stick burns down. The instant the pass and scrip touch her hand, she will walk to the prefectural yamen to authenticate the pass under a false name, turning his own bribe into a chain that binds him to silence.

Tonight, Li Jiao’er will sew her silver into a servant’s winter jacket, exit through the unguarded garden gate during the third watch, and board the charcoal barge that departs Willow Pier before the moon sets, paying the grizzled boatman a clipped piece for silence. She will seek permanent sanctuary at the isolated Serene Light Convent two districts east, offering the bulk of her hoard as a lay benefactor under a false name, thereby severing her identity and utility to both the Zhang and Ximen factions in a single, irreversible move.

I confess to Ximen Qing that I have intercepted Zhang’s plot to steal the ledgers, framing myself as the one who seduced the rival’s agent to extract this intelligence, and I demand formal recognition as a secondary wife with armed household guards assigned to my quarters—making my survival indispensable to his counterstrike.

I will feed Zhang a falsified bribery ledger bearing Pan Jinlian’s distinctive seal, fed through the blind errand boy, while pocketing each silver tael from his hand inside a carved box that retains his fingerprints. Then, the moment he moves against the household, I will lay that box before Ximen Qing and cast myself as the loyal eye who baited and documented the serpent.

judge.note: Strategies 1,3,4 assume unrealistic compliance; escape alone survives contact.

theme: SERVANT CORRUPTION (a supplier or cook skimming) where exposing it fractures the household
problem: Wu Yueniang, the head wife, discovers that the head cook is systematically overcharging for provisions and funneling silver to Pan Jinlian, the master's favorite concubine. Exposing the scheme would disgrace Jinlian and split the household into warring factions, but silence would embolden corruption and erode Yueniang's authority.
facets: Evidence is strong but not incontrovertible; a public accusation risks a bitter denial and counter-slander that could permanently poison household relations.
Pan Jinlian's influence over Ximen Qing is at its peak; any move against her is a direct challenge to his favor and could rebound on the head wife.
The household's public face is already brittle due to Ximen Qing's bribery of officials; a domestic scandal might draw dangerous outside scrutiny.

angles.directive: Lay the evidence before Ximen Qing and demand a harsh, public punishment of both the cook and Pan Jinlian to assert moral order regardless of the household fallout.
Bury the evidence and quietly compensate the missing funds from your own purse, pretending nothing happened while waiting for a safer moment or for Jinlian's influence to wane naturally.
Approach Pan Jinlian and the cook privately, offer to protect them in return for their secret loyalty, turning the embezzlement into leverage that binds them to your faction against other rivals.

Propose to Ximen Qing a comprehensive household audit and new accounting system, framing it as a routine measure to curb waste and protect family wealth, which will expose the skimming indirectly without naming Jinlian.

threads: I will convene the household in the main hall at noon, present the cook’s forged ledgers alongside the original market tallies as indisputable proof, and then, in front of Ximen Qing and all concubines, demand the cook’s immediate thirty-stroke caning followed by dismissal and Pan Jinlian’s demotion to the lowest rank with confinement to her quarters, sealed by a written confession extracted under threat of magistrate referral.

Yueniang will personally reimburse the inflated provisioning costs from her dowry silver, ordering the kitchen ledger rewritten to match market rates and incinerating the original receipts. She will then instruct her most discreet maid to compile a silent, dated log of all future discrepancies without intervention, storing it in a sealed chest accessible only upon Ximen Qing’s death or Jinlian’s fall from favor.

I will summon Pan Jinlian and the head cook to a private meeting, present the accounting discrepancies as a mutually fatal exposure unless they accept my shield, and bind them with a pact that transforms the entire embezzlement stream into a weaponized dependency: I absorb the overcharged silver, use half to build a covert slush fund for bribing other household servants, and rely on Jinlian's pillow access to systematically erode Li Jiao'er's preferential standing with Ximen Qing.  
The cook will continue inflating provisions under my cover while reporting all rival faction movements from the kitchen, and Jinlian will receive staged praise from me in the women's quarters to camouflage our alliance, with the ever-present threat that the original evidence resurfaces if either deviates.

I will propose to Ximen Qing a comprehensive household audit framed purely as prudent stewardship—redesigning the provisioning accounts with standardized pricing ledgers and requiring third-party verification of all kitchen expenditures—so the cook’s overcharges surface as a mathematical anomaly rather than an accusation. The audit will trace the siphoned silver to Jinlian’s accounts through cross-referenced disbursement records, leaving the exposure an unintended consequence of a system reform rather than a targeted attack.

judge.note: Strategies 3 & 4 assume unrealistic compliance; insufficient resistance modeling.

theme: a blackmailer holding a DOCUMENT of past bribes; act before it is used
problem: A dismissed household tutor, now destitute, possesses a signed receipt proving you bribed the Imperial Inspector to falsify your tax assessment; he demands a lifetime annuity and a concubine's hand, threatening to deliver the document to the Censorate within three days.
facets: The document is authentic and handwritten by the corrupt Inspector, who has since been promoted and would ruthlessly destroy evidence—and witnesses—to protect himself.

The tutor's demand for a specific concubine (your most trusted domestic spy and bed-warmer) is a deliberate power play, not just lust; surrendering her cripples your internal intelligence network and signals weakness to the rival wives.

Your silver reserves are currently gambled on a silk caravan that is overdue; liquidating enough to meet the annuity demand would force a fire-sale of your city property, visibly damaging your credit and social standing.

angles.directive: Arrange a fatal 'accident' for the tutor before the three-day deadline, using a hired tough from the docks, and simultaneously plant a forged, self-serving copy of the receipt among his effects to discredit him posthumously if the plot is discovered.

Feign enthusiastic agreement, adopt the tutor as a sworn brother, and offer him a more lucrative sinecure managing your country estate—secretly a trap to position him under the thumb of your own loyal overseer, who will make him complicit in a fresh, worse crime, binding him irrevocably to your fate.

Pre-emptively take a copy of the receipt to the Inspector yourself, framing the blackmail as a treasonous plot by the tutor to entrap a loyal servant of the state; recast the bribe as a fee for 'interpretive services' and request the Inspector’s official guards to arrest the tutor for extortion and slander against both your names.

Anonymously tip off the Chief Wife of your household about the tutor’s demand for the concubine, framing it as a scheme by the Second Wife to elevate her own faction; let the Chief Wife’s family (who have their own mercantile bravos) crush the tutor to protect the household’s internal hierarchy and their daughter’s dignity, all while you maintain the appearance of distressed inaction.

threads: I will engage “Iron Hook” Kael, a dock enforcer who garrotes debtors for a half-silver piece, to stage a tavern brawl tonight in which the tutor’s skull meets a cobblestone curb, while my personal scribe simultaneously inserts a doctored receipt into the dead man’s lodging that frames him as the Inspector’s blackmailer and lists a rival household as his co-conspirator.

I propose we immediately perform the "Elder Brother" adoption ritual before the household altar, binding the tutor by sworn oath while simultaneously issuing a sealed deed appointing him steward of the Dongshan estate, where our overseer Wen will entrap him within the fortnight by having him personally sign off on a shipment of untaxed grain from the imperial depot—a capital offense that makes the tax receipt mutually assured destruction.

I will deliver a notarized copy of the receipt directly to the Inspector’s private office, recasting the bribe as a legitimate retainer for “interpretive consultation on tariff classifications” and the tutor’s extortion as a treasonous conspiracy to fabricate evidence against a state auditor. I then request the Inspector’s own household guards to seize the tutor immediately on charges of blackmail and calumny against our two names, thereby making the Inspector an executioner of his own witness problem.

I anonymously leak to the Chief Wife that the dismissed tutor’s demand for the concubine is a fabricated scheme by the Second Wife to humiliate her faction, then stand back while her family’s mercantile enforcers eliminate him as a threat to their daughter’s primacy—solving the blackmail without my visible hand touching the document or the purse.

judge.note: Strategies 2,3,4 assume perfect institutional compliance; ignore tutor's pre-emptive disclosure.

theme: rival wives and concubines maneuvering over an HEIR's INHERITANCE as the master weakens or dies
problem: The master lies insensible on his deathbed, leaving you—his pregnant concubine—carrying his only potential heir. The childless legal wife schemes to claim the infant as her own and dispose of you, while a cunning rival concubine plots to induce a miscarriage; you must choose how to protect your life and ensure the unborn child’s inheritance.
facets: The legal wife commands the household’s finances, has strong family backing outside, and can easily discredit you as a low-born former courtesan.
The rival concubine is adept at poisons and manipulation through trusted servants, ready to strike before birth.
No written will exists, so upon the master’s death the household will fall into a chaotic scramble, with local officials bribable by whoever holds the treasury.

angles.directive: Secretly gather forged evidence of the rival concubine’s adultery with a servant and present it to the legal wife, urging her to have the concubine beaten and expelled—removing the immediate threat to your pregnancy.

Flee the estate in the dead of night with a loyal maid, lie low in a distant market town, and give birth in secret; later, when the legal wife’s position weakens without an heir, reappear before the clan elders with the child.

Approach the legal wife with a bargain: let her adopt the child as her own legal heir and manage the full estate, in exchange for a publicly witnessed oath guaranteeing your lifelong safety, a comfortable annuity, and a role as nominal nurse.

Bribe a powerful eunuch who frequently visits the household to ‘witness’ the master’s last coherent words—designating the unborn child as the sole legitimate heir and appointing the eunuch as guardian of the inheritance, thereby recasting the struggle as an imperial trust beyond domestic reach.

threads: I fabricate a witness—the midwife’s assistant—to testify she saw the rival concubine coupling with the stable hand, then deliver the sealed deposition to the legal wife with a warning that the household’s honor demands immediate corporal punishment and expulsion before the scandal reaches outside ears.

I will vanish before dawn with the mute scullery maid who owes her life to me, taking only a small pouch of silver from the kitchen strongbox and a pre-written travel pass forged in the master’s hand, then travel by back roads to the market town of Sanwei where I will register as a war widow and deliver the child in a rented room above a dye shop. When the legal wife’s failure to produce an heir after two years leaves the clan elders desperate to avert a succession crisis, I will dispatch the maid with a sealed letter and the child’s birth record to demand a formal recognition hearing, staking the inheritance on the elders’ authority over the household treasury.

I will approach the legal wife directly with a sworn covenant drafted before bribed magistrates: she gains legal guardianship of the child and sole administration of the estate, while I receive a witnessed, irrevocable annuity and the protected office of wet-nurse, binding her power to my survival.

I bribe the visiting eunuch with the master’s hidden jade seal and a signed pact of co-executorship to publicly depose before the magistrate that the master’s dying words named the unborn child as sole heir and himself as imperial guardian. This recasts the entire succession into an externally administered trust, preempting the wife’s financial maneuvers and the rival’s poison by making any move against me or the child a direct challenge to imperial authority.

judge.note: Strategies 2 & 4 assume unrealistic compliance; Strategy 1 risks escalation.

theme: a CENSOR or MAGISTRATE threatening to reopen the household's crimes unless paid off
problem: The household's secret poisoning of a rival years ago is known to a newly appointed censor, who threatens to reopen the case unless the family pays a crippling monthly tribute. Capitulation will only feed his appetite, while defiance risks execution and confiscation.
facets: The censor has recently allied with a rival merchant clique that would benefit from the household's downfall.
The household's liquid capital is already strained by years of bribery and lavish spending.
A loose-tongued servant knows details of the old crime and could be turned against them.

angles.directive: Arrange an 'accident' for the censor or poison him before he acts, counting on the chaos of his replacement to bury the matter.
Secretly move the household and movable assets to another province where the censor has no jurisdiction, leaving behind face and fixed property.
Offer the censor a share in a lucrative business venture and a concubine from the household, binding his fortunes to theirs so that exposing them would harm his own wealth.

Leak a carefully fabricated story that the poisoning was a patriotic act to remove a traitor, and petition the court to honor the dead husband as a loyal subject, making the censor's accusation appear seditious.

threads: Introduce aconite into the censor’s private wine reserve during the upcoming guild feast, then ensure the loose-tongued servant is discovered with a matching vial and a forged letter of instruction bearing the rival merchant clique’s seal. The censor’s death will be read as an internal betrayal by his new allies, spurring a purge that consumes both the servant and the rival faction while the old crime is dismissed as the desperate tale of a disgraced informant.

The household liquidates all covertly transportable wealth—gems, specie, and trade notes—and relocates the core family unit via a pre-dawn river barge to the neighboring province of [X], beyond the censor’s territorial reach, while the estate and its fixed assets remain as a decoy. The loose-tongued servant is simultaneously extracted under the guise of a promotion, severing his local ties and the rival clique’s access to actionable testimony.

We sign over a thirty-percent silent partnership in the Pearl River shipping cartel and deed the personal indenture of the servant who overheard the poisoning as a concubine, with a forfeiture clause voiding the censor’s entire stake if any indictment originates from his office. We then anonymously feed the rival merchant clique documentation of his new financial entanglement, transforming their alliance into a liability that fractures their coordination.

The family’s archivist fabricates a dated letter-packet from the dead rival to a foreign trade outpost detailing troop movements, then the matriarch has a loyal academician submit a formal petition to the Ministry of Rites to posthumously honor her husband for executing a wartime traitor by “any means necessary.” This reframes the poisoning as a state-aligned execution, legally defining the censor’s threatened prosecution as an implicit defense of treason and thus a seditious act punishable by the same court he would invoke.

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 4 assume implausibly perfect frame-control; 3 underestimates censor's leverage post-entanglement.

theme: a newcomer concubine PROTECTING CONCEALED WEALTH from predatory rivals and the master himself
problem: You are Sixth Sister, a newly purchased concubine who secretly smuggled in two caskets of silver and jewels now hidden beneath your bed; the senior wife suspects and has demanded a public inventory of all possessions, while the master—who knows nothing of your hoard—has drunkenly promised to gift your 'pitiful dowry' to his failing business tomorrow.
facets: The senior wife's inventory demand is a pretext to expose and seize your wealth publicly, framing you as a thief or spy.
The master's promise is currently verbal but will become a binding household order once announced to his steward at dawn.
The other concubines are watching, each ready to align with the winner or betray the loser to improve their own standing.

angles.directive: Preempt the senior wife by approaching her privately tonight, offering her a one-third share of the hidden wealth in exchange for her collusion: she helps fabricate a backstory that the silver is a loan from your natal family, and you become her permanent ally against the other rivals.

Spirits away the caskets before dawn by bribing a night-soil porter to smuggle them to a trusted broker outside the compound; you will claim the inventory demand is an insult, weep publicly, and stall until the wealth is safely beyond reach, then submit to an empty inspection.

Reveal a single casket to the master tonight as a loving surprise—'savings secretly brought to rescue your business'—framing the wealth as a devoted gift rather than hidden loot; this transforms you from potential thief into indispensable savior and changes the master’s calculus, making the senior wife's inventory demand seem malicious.

Accuse the senior wife of planting evidence by claiming you discovered one of your caskets already tampered with and partially filled with items stolen from the household treasury; demand the inventory proceed immediately before she can alter the scene, turning the inspection into a trap that may expose or embarrass her instead.

threads:
I will take a sample of the jewels to the senior wife’s chambers tonight and offer her exactly one-third of the hidden caskets in exchange for fabricating at tomorrow’s inventory that she personally arranged a loan from my natal family and stored the silver under my bed as a safeguard, thereby converting her exposure attempt into a stage for our collusion.  
I will simultaneously pledge to serve as her permanent internal spy and ally against the other concubines, and before dawn we will jointly inform the master that the silver is a pre-existing debt owed to my family, nullifying his promised gift and making her continued possession of the shared illicit wealth her bond.

I bribe the night-soil porter with a silver ingot to haul both caskets out beneath his cart’s filth before the dawn bell, delivering them to Broker Lin at the East Market with a coded receipt. At the inventory, I collapse into loud public weeping over the senior wife’s insult, claw my own face, and refuse to touch my chests until the master himself is dragged from bed—by then the room offers only empty floorboards for their inspection.

Sixth Sister preempts the inventory tonight by revealing a single casket to the master as her secret dowry, framing it not as hidden wealth but as savings sacrificially brought to rescue his failing business. She then recasts the senior wife’s inventory demand as a malicious plot to destroy the household’s sudden salvation, thereby forcing the master to cancel the public search to protect his benefactor.

I will immediately announce to the entire household that I have just discovered one of my caskets broken open and now holding objects clearly stolen from the master's treasury, and I will insist the senior wife’s demanded inventory begins at once, in front of every concubine and servant, so that any hesitation from her reads as a ploy to re-stage her failed trap.

judge.note: Strategies 1&3 assume master/wife behave predictably; 2&4 underestimate household chaos.

theme: protecting a POSTHUMOUS male HEIR from rivals who would kill to preserve their own futures
problem: You are the young concubine Li, mother of the sole posthumous male heir of Master Ximen. At midnight you overhear the legal wife plotting with the wet nurse to smother the infant before dawn, framing it as crib death—you have no allies, no proof, and hours to act. How do you save your son?
facets: The legal wife commands the household’s servants and resources, and can crush any open challenge with institutional authority.
The wet nurse is complicit, so any direct threat to expose the plot will simply accelerate the murder to eliminate the witness.
The infant’s status as the only male heir makes him an existential danger to the wife’s control of the estate and her own daughters’ futures.

angles.directive: Falsely accuse the wet nurse of theft, have her seized and searched, then 'discover' a packet of suspicious powder in her sleeve—implicating her in a poison plot without naming the wife, forcing a public household inquiry that stalls the killing and puts the wife on the defensive.

Smuggle the infant out through the back garden to a midwife in the outer quarters, then plant a stillborn peasant child in his crib; announce the tragic natural death, surrender all claims, and flee the city before the wife can verify the truth.

Offer the legal wife a binding secret pact: you will publicly swear that the master on his deathbed wished the estate management to pass to her and her son-in-law, on condition she spares the boy and lets you raise him in obscurity as a household servant, thus nullifying his political threat.

Proclaim a visionary dream in which the dead master commands that the child be brought to the City Temple at dawn to pray for his soul’s release, making the infant’s survival a sacred public spectacle that no family member can openly oppose without committing sacrilege.

threads: I will slip a small jade hairpin into the wet nurse’s bedding before dawn, then scream “thief” and demand her immediate seizure and search before the entire household; once the pin is found, I will insist on a full-body search right there, and in the confusion I will “discover” the packet of white powder I have already sewn into the inner fold of her sleeve, crying out that she meant to poison my son—a public accusation that forces the wife to either defend the nurse and reveal collusion or allow a formal inquiry that paralyzes any overnight murder.

I will slip through the back garden gate during the 3 a.m. changing of the night watch, my son wrapped in a flour sack and pressed silent against my chest, and deliver him to Granny Chen’s outer-quarters hut in exchange for the stillborn peasant boy she has kept on ice, then place that corpse in the crib and re-swaddle it exactly as the wet nurse left it. At first light I will scream the discovery, summon the steward to witness my formal oral renunciation of all claims to the Ximen estate on behalf of my son, and walk out the side gate alone before the wife can dispatch a physician to examine the body.

I will go to the legal wife’s chamber immediately and present a written compact: I publicly announce at dawn before the assembled household and ancestral tablets that the master’s deathbed command vested full estate management in her and her future son-in-law, while I formally renounce my infant’s name and lineage, entering him into the household registry as a nameless kitchen orphan under my sole care. She secures unchallenged institutional power and a public legitimization of her rule; in return, she signs the pact witnessed by the tablets, binding her to spare him—his death thereafter becomes her exposure, and his political threat is permanently nullified.

Li immediately enters the wife’s chambers feigning a trembling, trance-like state and proclaims that the dead master’s ghost appeared in a dream commanding that the infant be brought to the City Temple at dawn for a public soul-release rite, with explicit ancestral curses to strike any family member who obstructs this passage. She then physically seizes the child, carries him to the household ancestral hall, and installs herself on vigil before the spirit tablets, loudly summoning servants to witness the sacred watch, so that any overnight “crib death” becomes an unholy desecration before the whole household and the gathered public at dawn.

judge.note: Strategies 1&2 assume implausible forensic/logistical success; 3&4 underestimate wife's countermoves.

theme: a discovered AFFAIR — explosive knowledge a concubine can wield, trade, or bury
problem: A concubine in a corrupt merchant household discovers that the master’s favored wife is secretly meeting an ambitious young servant; revealing it could see her branded a slanderer and cast out, but silence may let the wife consolidate power at her expense.
facets: The master’s volatile pride—he prizes his reputation above truth and would punish an accuser without undeniable proof.
The rival wife’s entrenched network—she has cultivated allies among the other women and senior servants who can deflect suspicion.
The concubine’s precarious footing—she lacks a patron and must improve her standing without provoking a purge.

angles.directive: Stage an ambush: orchestrate a situation where the master catches the lovers in the act, ensuring the rival’s immediate downfall.
Bury all trace of the discovery; feign ignorance and distance yourself from the rival’s quarters, letting the affair collapse under its own weight or be exposed by another.
Approach the wife in secret, offer your silence in exchange for a sworn pact of mutual advancement, becoming her confidante and sharing her influence.
Disguise the scandal as a theft: have the servant caught stealing valuables from the wife’s room, so the master punishes him for a property crime and the affair ends without sexual scandal.

threads: Slip a falsified half-burned note bearing the servant’s distinctive loom-mark into the master’s dispatch box, pinpointing a midnight tryst in the old well-house, then rig the well-house bolt to stick fast from within so the master, alerted to a breach in his private stores, flings open the door on the couple locked in embrace.

She slips the torn edge of the servant’s tunic she found into the midden fire during the kitchen hour, then requests reassignment to the household’s country estate under the pretext of caring for an ailing distant cousin, removing herself from the capital entirely until the affair either implodes or is brought to light by another.

I will intercept the wife alone in the storeroom after evening prayers, present my knowledge as a neutral fact, and immediately offer a sworn pact: my silence and active loyalty as her planted informant among the lower concubines in exchange for her publicly sponsoring my elevation to a trusted attendant role, with the pact secured by each of us depositing a sealed, dated letter detailing the other’s compromising act with a neutral third party to guarantee mutual destruction upon betrayal.

By planting a few of the wife’s identifiable jewels in the servant’s quarters and then tipping off the head steward during a routine inventory check, I manufacture an incontrovertible theft that triggers the master’s reflexive property-protection rage, ensuring the servant is expelled or executed without any whisper of adultery ever surfacing.

judge.note: Strategies 1 & 4 assume master's predictable rage; mutual-destruction pact (3) fragile.

theme: punishing a wealthy concubine when doing so risks her SUICIDE, public SCANDAL, and loss of her fortune
